At the time, he had forced her to change the ownership to his name so that Charlotte couldn’t run away from her marriage if she wanted to live on the leasury.

"By law, the assets you bring into the marriage are yours after the divorce. He may have put every asset in his name, but we can still fight for those assets in court. Especially if it’s proven that he manipulated or coerced you into signing those papers."

Damon told her, "Tomorrow my legal team will meet with you and we can discuss this further tomorrow."

Charlotte was speechless. She hadn’t expected Damon to do so many things to help her.

He even hired the legal team just for her.

How did she deserve a man like him? How could she repay him when money wasn’t enough to make up for what he had done to her?

"I ... I don’t know what to say." Charlotte looked into Damon’s gray eyes. "All this time, I thought I lost everything."

"I’m going to make sure to return what’s yours, Charlotte," Damon promised.

That night, Charlotte finally got a long, good night’s sleep because of that promise.

• •

The next day, three lawyers arrived at Crimson Moon Manor. They were the best lawyers in the supernatural business and had won many cases in the Order’s court.

When Theodore led them to the Alpha’s study room, Damon and Charlotte were waiting for them. Charlotte stood and shook the lead attorney’s hand.

"Good morning, Mrs. Dawson." A tall man with straight brunette hair and hazel eyes smiled at her. "I’m Victor Hale, and these are my associates, Sophie Marks and Finn Blackwood. We’ve reviewed the preliminary details of your case, and I must say that the evidence you’ve gathered is enough to convince the judge to terminate your marriage to Mr. Julian Warren."

He continued, "However, we still have a lot of work to do to get your assets back from Mr. Warren."

They sat on the sofa while Finn opened the suitcase, and then placed the stack of documents on the table.

He shared a copy of the documents with Damon and Charlotte before saying, "Your divorce case is simple, but getting your assets back is a bit complicated."

Victor asked them to read the documents and said, "Mrs. Dawson, you didn’t sign a transfer of ownership like you think you did. In fact, you signed sales agreements. Mr. Warren officially purchased all of your assets, making them part of the marital estate instead of separate property."

Charlotte was dumbfounded by this statement. Julian had never let her read the documents, so she didn’t know the contents in detail. But what was the point of knowing? He would still ask her to sign those documents even though she was against it.

"You mean ... everything I owned is now legally his?" She clenched her fist. "No wonder he’s so confident I’ll be empty-handed after the divorce."

chapters.title: Chapter 15: New Script
chapters.content:
Chapter 15: New Script

Victor nodded. "Exactly. He knew what he was doing. He manipulated you into selling your assets."

She had earned every dime and every asset by sacrificing her blood, sweat, and tears. But Julian could get it all by simply getting Charlotte to sign a piece of paper.

Selling my assets, my ass , Charlotte didn’t even get a dime from him!

Sophie straightened her posture and met Charlotte’s gaze. "However, we can get those assets back if we can prove that you sold them under duress."

She continued, "Fortunately, the messages he sent, especially the threats, will help strengthen our case."

Charlotte clenched her fists, a hint of anger flickering in her eyes. "Do whatever it takes. He doesn’t deserve a penny of what I’ve worked for."

Victor lifted the corners of his mouth. "Don’t worry, Mrs. Dawson. We’re not called the best legal team for nothing." He said confidently, "We’ll make sure you get what was yours."

After discussing all of the matters, the attorneys promised to take care of her court schedule and to learn more about her cases.

"Please contact us immediately if Mr. Warren threatens you again, ma’am." Victor handed Charlotte his business card. "See you later, Mrs. Dawson."

"Thank you for your time, sir," Charlotte replied.

As the lawyers left the room, Theodore ushered them to the gate, leaving Charlotte and Damon alone in the study.

The room was silent for a moment before Charlotte opened her mouth. "How did I ever end up marrying a man like him? I’m so stupid."

But honestly, she knew exactly why.

He had always treated her with kindness and showered her with so many beautiful gifts.

Even though Charlotte had to admit that Julian sometimes couldn’t handle his anger very well, at least he had never hit her back then.

But maybe he had done that to trick her into marrying him. He pretended to be perfect so he could trap Charlotte in his cage.

"There’s no point in dwelling on the past." Damon said, "What matters is what you do now to fight back and not let him control you again."

Charlotte sighed and leaned back on the couch. "I just can’t stop thinking about how blind I’ve been. Maybe it would be better if I couldn’t fall in love at all."

That way Charlotte wouldn’t have to suffer for love.

Damon was suddenly quiet, his eyes fixed on her as if he was weighing his words carefully. "Love isn’t the problem, Charlotte. It’s the people who don’t know how to appreciate it."

Charlotte started to open her mouth, but Damon had already gotten up from the couch and walked over to his desk. "I have something for you."

"What is it?

Damon took papers out of his desk. "A new movie script." He approached her and handed her the papers. "I think this script is suitable for you."

Charlotte hesitated as she took the thick stack of papers from Damon’s hands. The title, Broken Bond, was bold and striking on the cover.

"What’s it about?" she asked quietly, looking up at Damon.

"It’s about a single mother trying to rebuild her life after leaving her abusive husband," Damon explained, his voice soft but firm. "She’s raising her five-year-old daughter and doing everything she can to give her a happy life."

Charlotte’s grip on the script tightened. "It sounds ... so close to home," she murmured.

The story was similar to her own life. The abusive husband, the desperation of the main character, but unfortunately she didn’t have a daughter.

"This is your ticket to fight back, Charlotte." Damon explained, "Through this movie, you can show your fans, the world, what your husband did to you."

One of the most powerful powers in the world was women’s empathy. They would support a woman who has gone through hell without being asked, especially if the story could resonate with women who have faced similar struggles.

This story could give courage to women who were trapped in an abusive marriage but were too afraid to leave. This kind of story could also be more meaningful if the actress knew the struggle and could relate to the character.

Charlotte flipped through the pages, catching glimpses of scenes that were all too familiar: screaming at each other, quiet nights filled with tears and bruises, and the constant feeling of being unworthy.

"Many women experience all of this." Damon comforted her, "You are not the only woman who has ever made a bad choice. You are not alone, Charlotte."

Therefore, she didn’t have to be afraid. She didn’t have to feel like a fool when the real problem was her abusive husband, never her.

"I hope you want to try this movie. But if it makes you uncomfortable, you don’t have to force it." Damon said, "The choice is yours."

Charlotte thought about it for a while before she said, "When do the auditions start?"

Damon smiled when he heard her question. "A month from now. Is that too soon?"

She shook her head. "That’s enough."

She didn’t want Damon to postpone the audition just for her, so she just had to try her best to practice her acting skills again.

Unfortunately, she couldn’t focus on practicing while the court hearing was haunting her. Two days later, Victor informed Charlotte that the Nocturnal Order would have their divorce hearing in two weeks.

"You don’t have to worry, Mrs. Dawson." Victor said, "My team and I will do our best to win the trial. All you have to do is sit in the courtroom and follow our instructions."

Charlotte replied over the phone. "Thanks for your hard work, Mr. Hale."

She ended the call with Victor and put the phone down on the table before falling back onto the bed. The stack of papers was scattered around her bed, words highlighted in color.

Her eyes stared at the ceiling, feeling both anxious and depressed at having to think about two things at once. Still, it was better than feeling stressed about watching her ex-husband sleep with another woman every week.

Knock. Knock.

She turned her head when she heard the knock at the door. She felt reluctant but still stood up from her bed. When she opened the door, she saw Vera and Haven standing at the door, carrying a tray of muffin cakes.

Vera smiled at her and said, "Haven and I are trying to make muffin cakes for the New Year. Would you like to taste them?"

chapters.title: Chapter 16: Muffin Cakes
chapters.content:
Chapter 16: Muffin Cakes

It took Charlotte by surprise because she had never expected Vera and Haven to meet her except in the dining room.

"Yes, of course." Charlotte opened the door wider. "Please come in."

She immediately picked up the papers that were scattered everywhere. "I’m sorry, my room is a bit of a mess right now."

Vera glanced at the script and immediately felt guilty for coming in without notice, "Did we disturb you, Charlotte?"

"No! Not at all. I’m glad you came." Charlotte clapped her hands once after putting the papers on the table and letting them sit on the couches. "So, which muffins should I try?"

Haven replied enthusiastically. "All of them! We usually bake cookies for the New Year, but Mommy and I want to bake something new this year!"

Charlotte bit her inner lip when she saw that there were six muffin cakes on the tray, each topped with colorful decorations, sprinkles, fruit, and a generous dollop of whipped cream.

Too many calories.

"Well, I don’t think I can eat them all at once," Charlotte said with a smile, "but I’ll try them all. Which one is your favorite, Haven?"

Haven’s face lit up as she pointed to a muffin with a swirl of vanilla whipped cream and dried strawberries on top. "This one! Mommy says it’s our best batch."

Charlotte picked up the muffin and took a small bite, the rich flavors of vanilla and strawberry melting in her mouth. "This is delicious! You and your mom did a great job."

Haven clapped her hands excitedly. "You really like it? Mommy said it was good, but I wanted to hear it from you!"

Charlotte chuckled and wiped a crumb from her lips. "I love it, Haven. You are a talented young lady."

Haven spoke with pride. "Will you help us bake next time? Mommy says it’s fun to bake with friends."

Friends?

It had never occurred to Charlotte that Haven would consider her a friend.

Charlotte smiled. "I’d love to. Maybe I could learn a thing or two from you."

Haven grinned and picked up another muffin to offer her. "Here, try this one next! It’s blueberry lemon, Mommy’s favorite."

Charlotte took the muffin and smiled warmly. She rarely baked cakes because Julian hated it when he saw her eating something sweet. He said she would gain weight easily if she ate those things.

However, Julian wasn’t around anymore, so she could try it once in a while.

For the first time in a long time, Charlotte felt a sense of warmth from others, even if it was just over a tray of muffins.

From tasting the muffins, Haven and Vera ended up talking to Charlotte about many things, from their favorite recipes to funny stories about Haven’s attempts to bake cookies on her own.

"Last week, Haven tried to surprise me by baking cookies," Vera said, laughing as she told the story. "But instead of sugar, she accidentally used salt. Luckily, her daddy loved her cookies so much that he ate them all."

"Daddy said my salty cookies taste good!" Haven defended herself.

Vera laughed. "That’s because your daddy’s tongue is numb."

Charlotte watched their interaction and smiled. If only she had children of her own, maybe she would teach them to bake cookies for fun and let their father taste them.

Wait, if she had a child now, didn’t that mean that Damon would be the father?

Her face suddenly turned red at the thought of it.

She knew she couldn’t bear a child, but wouldn’t it be great if her children had Damon’s appearance?

Charlotte shook her head, trying to dismiss the thought, but her cheeks remained red. The idea of having children with Damon was both impossible and strangely comforting.

She imagined their little ones running around with Damon’s gray eyes and his smile, filling the house with laughter. Maybe they’d tug on Damon’s coat, asking him to read them stories or wanting to follow their daddy to work.

As her imagination ran wild, her heart ached with a mixture of longing and sadness because she wouldn’t be able to turn that imagination into a reality. "What am I thinking?" she muttered to herself.

"Did you say something, Charlotte?" asked Vera confusedly.

Charlotte’s eyes widened as she realized that she had been talking to herself when other people were around. "Ah, no. Nothing." She immediately changed the subject. "I used to love making cheesecake. It was ... something I did a lot when I was younger. But I haven’t baked for years."

Vera, "Maybe it’s time to give it a try again. Baking can be therapeutic for some people, you know. Damon would love to try your cake."

"Does he like sweet things?"

"Who cares what he likes?" Vera chuckled. "If you make him something, he has to eat it."

Haven nodded in agreement. "Yeah! My daddy always eats everything my mommy cooks!"

Charlotte lifted the corner of her mouth. "That’s because your daddy loves your mommy very much."

They talked for a while until Haven started to feel sleepy. Vera carried her daughter in her arms and said, "I think it’s time for the princess to take a nap."

"No ... I want to play with Charlotte a little longer..." Haven murmured.

Charlotte rubbed her head. "We can play again later. Besides, we live together, so we can see each other every day."

Haven’s eyes lit up. "Would you like to play in my room tomorrow, Charlotte?"

"Yes, I’d love to."

Vera, "I’m sorry if we bother you. This kid is very clingy when she gets close to someone."

"Don’t worry about it." Charlotte waved her hand. "I like playing with children."

As they headed for the door, Vera patted Charlotte on the shoulder. "I heard from Louis that you’re going to divorce court."

Charlotte nodded. "Yes. Julian refused to sign the divorce papers, so I have no choice."

Vera gave her a sympathetic look. "I can’t imagine how difficult it must be. But you seem strong, Charlotte. You’ll get through this."

"Thank you," Charlotte said quietly. Her heart filled with warmth and happiness as Vera looked so concerned for her. "It’s not easy, but I’m trying."

Vera gave her a genuine smile before speaking again. "If you ever need someone to talk to or even just a distraction, you can always come to us. Haven would be overjoyed to have you around more often."

Charlotte smiled faintly. "I appreciate that. Really."

She waved at Haven, who was half asleep before she closed the door. She let out a sigh of relief as most of her anxiety disappeared after spending time with Vera and Haven.

She then looked at the tray of muffin cakes on the table and planned to throw them away. But as she almost tossed them into the trash bin, her hands stopped halfway. She muttered, "Just today ... I can eat them for today."

chapters.title: Chapter 17: Divorce Trial
chapters.content:
Chapter 17: Divorce Trial

As two weeks passed, Charlotte finally had to attend the hearing. She hadn’t expected Damon to come with her because of his busy schedule, but he decided to drop all his workload for one day to accompany her.

"You really don’t have to do this." Charlotte lowered her head and looked at her shoes on the ground. "Mr. Hale and his team will accompany me into the courtroom."

"They’re your legal team, while I’m your mate. We’re not the same," Damon replied firmly, his tone leaving no room for argument.

Charlotte felt a warmth creep up her cheeks. His words were simple, but they were enough to let her know that someone cared about her and would be sitting behind her in the courtroom the whole time.

As they approached the courthouse steps, Damon gently placed a hand on her back, guiding her forward. "Listen, Charlotte," he began, his voice softer now, "I know it will be hard for you to face your abuser again, but I need you to stay calm in the courtroom."

"Don’t let your emotions control you, so he has no chance to manipulate you." Damon turned her around to face him. "But if he does, remember that you’re not alone. He can’t hurt you as long as I’m with you."

She wasn’t alone.

Her mate was protecting her, so whatever happened, Julian would never be able to touch her again.

She looked up at him, her blue eyes meeting his gray ones. "Thank you," she said softly. "For giving me a second chance."

If Damon had refused Charlotte’s request to renew their bond, she might not have had a chance to escape Julian’s cage.

Because of him, Charlotte found a new purpose in life.

Damon smiled faintly. "Anytime."

After strengthening her heart, Charlotte entered the courthouse with Damon. Her legal team was waiting for her in the lobby.

Victor stepped forward with a broad smile. "Good morning, Mrs. Dawson," he said. "Are you ready to win your case?"

Charlotte nodded and smiled. "More than ready."

Sophie informed her, "The trial will begin in five minutes. We may enter the courtroom now."

As they entered the courtroom, Charlotte felt her heartbeat quicken. Although she tried her best not to show her emotions, the thought of facing Julian again made her feel nauseous.

Her hand suddenly moved, searching for Damon’s hand as he walked behind her. She gripped it tightly as if she wanted to find a solace for her fear.

"You’re going to be okay," Damon whispered into her ear.

Charlotte took a deep breath before turning her gaze to Julian, who was already sitting at his table.

Unexpectedly, Julian turned his head so he could see Charlotte clearly. He gave her a crooked smile, the kind of smile he always showed when Charlotte did something wrong.

Julian’s eyes narrowed when he saw Damon’s presence. He clenched his jaw, looking angry that his own uncle had stolen his wife.

Priscilla sat behind Julian, her sharp gaze fixed on Charlotte. It was clear that she was ready to support her son no matter what.

"I’ll be here." Damon couldn’t follow her to the plaintiff’s table, but he made sure to sit right behind her. "Whatever happens in court, remember, I’ll always support you."

Charlotte nodded and smiled at him. "I know."

Soon after, the judge entered the courtroom and everyone rose. After the formalities, the trial began.

Victor Hale spoke first and presented the evidence of Julian’s physical abuse of Charlotte. The pictures of her bruises and severe wounds were plastered across the projector, causing the people in the courtroom to grimace.

"Your Honor, as you can see, Mr. Warren has abused my client, Mrs. Dawson, on multiple occasions," Victor Hale continued, "These photographs are evidence not only of physical harm but also of the emotional and psychological trauma she has endured in their marriage."

Julian’s attorney, Mr. Anders, stood up to object. "Your Honor, the defense contends that these photographs lack proper context. Based on my client’s testimony, Mrs. Dawson’s mental state was unstable and she would hurt herself to get her husband’s attention."

Charlotte’s jaw dropped after hearing Julian’s lawyer’s statement. Her mental state was unstable, he said. She occasionally hurt herself.?

Like hell, she would do that to herself.

If he said her mental state was unstable, it was because her husband had abused her every single day!

"Objection, Your Honor!" Victor defended her, "Mr. Anders’ allegations are baseless and deeply offensive. My client has no history of self-harm. Furthermore, the majority of these wounds are in areas that Mrs. Dawson could not reach by herself."

Julian liked to use a belt or whip to punish her, so most of her injuries were on her back.

It truly didn’t make sense for her to cause whiplash wounds on her own, and it made even less sense for her to ask someone else to whip her.

Mr. Anders responded to him, "Your Honor, we argue that they lack proper verification of their evidence."

"The wounds have not been examined or documented by a licensed medical professional. Without proper examination, the authenticity of these injuries remains speculative."

Victor’s expression remained calm. "Your Honor, Mrs. Dawson is a werewolf, a supernatural being with the ability to heal quickly. Hence, she took the photos of her injuries immediately after the abuse occurred."

He continued, "Also, Mr. Warren forbade my client from leaving the house, so she was unable to seek immediate supernatural medical help."

Mr. Anders smiled. "A werewolf’s ability to heal depends on the individual. Some werewolves need only minutes or hours, but others need days to fully recover from serious injuries."

He continued, "If we want to know how fast Mrs. Dawson’s healing ability is, I suggest we do a live demonstration in court. Have someone inflict a minor wound on Mrs. Dawson and observe how quickly it heals."

Damon immediately turned his sharp gaze on Julian. He clenched his fists tightly and whispered to himself, "Julian, you bastard."

chapters.title: Chapter 18: A Witness
chapters.content:
Chapter 18: A Witness

"Objection, Your Honor!" Victor exclaimed, "This suggestion is absolutely reprehensible. I will not accept any suggestion to harm my client in the courtroom to protect her dignity."

The judge seemed to dislike the idea as well, so he said, "Mr. Anders, this is a court, not a circus. Your suggestion is denied."

Charlotte clenched her fists until her nails dug into her skin. She understood that Julian was barbaric and heartless, but she hadn’t expected him and his lawyer to go this far.

To suggest harming a victim of abuse? Ridiculous.

Only a truly heartless person would suggest such a thing in court.

She turned slightly to look at Damon. He was the one who had told her not to show her emotions in court, but he looked even more angry than she did.

He looked like he wanted to punch Julian right in the face.

"Thank you, Your Honor." Victor nodded, then continued, "To corroborate the evidence, I have submitted an affidavit from a former housemaid who worked at Mr. Warren’s house for three years."

Julian’s face darkened. He had fired the maid who sympathized too much with Charlotte and sent her abroad last year.

But how could she come back without him knowing?

He growled, then looked back at his filthy rich uncle. If there was anyone who could get her back immediately and protect her, it had to be Damon.

Mr. Anders said, "Your Honor, while the affidavit is compelling, it remains a written statement. We request that this witness be brought into court for cross-examination to test the credibility of her testimony."

Victor smiled faintly. "Your Honor, the witness is indeed in court today. With your permission, I’d like to call her to the stand."

A middle-aged woman suddenly rose from her chair. She covered her face with a scarf so Julian couldn’t recognize her earlier.

The judge nodded. "Permission granted. Call the witness to the stand."

Victor gestured to the woman. "Please state your name for the record."

"My name is Maria Lopez," she said, her voice calm. "I worked as a housemaid for Mr. Warren for three years."

Charlotte’s eyes softened as she looked at Maria. When she lived with Julian, Maria had been the only maid who had cared for her and comforted her when she wanted to cry.

She cared for her so much that she bravely confronted Julian when he tried to hit Charlotte. Her action infuriated Julian, who immediately fired Maria and punished Charlotte more severely that night.

Victor approached. "Maria, can you confirm the details of the affidavit you filed regarding the abuse you witnessed?"

Maria looked at Charlotte, her eyes softening. "Yes, I can confirm it. I saw Mrs. Dawson’s injuries on several occasions. I also heard Mr. Warren threaten her, saying that no one would believe her if she tried to report him."

Mr. Anders quickly interjected, "Your Honor, we contend that this testimony could be biased, considering Maria was fired by Mr. Warren."

Maria looked directly at the judge. "I was fired because I refused to turn a blind eye to the abuse. I tried to stop Mr. Warren when he was about to beat Mrs. Dawson when she had passed out, but he threatened to destroy my life instead."

"He forcibly sent me abroad and would not even let me return to this country to see my family."

Maria gave Julian a sharp look. "If someone hadn’t helped me and guaranteed my protection, I might have died as soon as I crossed the border."

The tension in the courtroom grew. Charlotte bit her inner lips, fighting back tears as Maria’s words brought back memories she had tried to suppress.

If Damon hadn’t helped her find Maria, Charlotte might have carried the guilt for the rest of her life for letting Julian hurt the one person who cared about her.

The judge leaned forward. "Mr. Hale, continue your questioning, but make sure the testimony stays relevant and factual."

Victor nodded. "Maria, do you have any physical evidence to support your testimony?"

Maria reached into her pocket and pulled out a small notebook. "I have been keeping a record of the dates and details of the abuse I have witnessed, in the hope that one day it might help Mrs. Dawson."

Victor handed the notebook to the court. "Your Honor, this is additional evidence to support my client’s claims."

The judge looked at the notebook briefly before handing it to the clerk. "This evidence will be considered."

Julian’s jaw tensed, his usual composure beginning to crack. For the first time, the courtroom felt like it was slipping out of his control.

Mr. Anders cleared his throat before speaking, "Your Honor, we can’t ignore the possibility that this notebook was fabricated."

Maria, "Your Honor, I wrote the notebook as soon as I witnessed each incident in detail. If I forged it, how could some of the ink have faded? If necessary, I am willing to submit the notebook for forensic analysis to prove its authenticity."

Charlotte couldn’t help but feel touched that Maria would go to such lengths to defend her. She didn’t even know that Maria had made the notebook for her.

The Judge, "That will be considered. Mr. Anders, unless you have tangible evidence of fabrication, I suggest we continue with the testimony for now."

Anders adjusted his tie, looking a little frustrated. "Very well, Your Honor."

Victor gestured for Maria to continue. "Maria, can you describe a specific incident you witnessed in detail?"

Maria nodded solemnly. "I remember one night when Mr. Warren came home late. He brought home a young woman, a human woman I suppose. They both reeked of alcohol and made love in the living room."

"Mrs. Dawson heard their voices and came out of her room. She told the young woman to leave immediately, but Mr. Warren threw a vase over her head instead."

Julian had made a habit of bringing random women into their home, but that night Charlotte was fed up with his infidelity. She simply asked them to make love outside, find a hotel, or whatever, so she didn’t have to witness her husband’s disgusting behavior.

But Julian was furious. He shouted at her and then threw a flower vase and a glass at her head.

Maria’s voice trembled slightly as she continued, "The vase hit her head and she collapsed on the floor, bleeding. I tried to intervene, but Mr. Warren told me to leave the room. He said if I stayed, I’d suffer the same fate.

The courtroom was eerily silent, Maria’s words causing hatred to grow in everyone’s heart, especially Damon’s.

Victor asked, "Maria, did you see what happened after Mrs. Dawson was hurt?"

Maria nodded. "Yes. Mr. Warren dragged her back into her bedroom while she was still bleeding. I tried to call for help but the house phone had been disconnected and my cell phone had been confiscated earlier that week."

Victor turned to the judge. "Your Honor, this testimony is crucial. It shows not only Mr. Warren’s physical abuse but also the control and isolation he imposed on Mrs. Dawson and the household staff."

Mr. Anders immediately said, "Your Honor, I object. This testimony is hearsay and lacks corroborative evidence."

"What evidence do you need again?" Victor started to lose his patience. "A woman had been abused for five years by her husband and how dare you call her a liar when the evidence is in front of your eyes?"

The judge interrupted, "Mr. Hale, please do not let emotion guide your argument. This court values facts and evidence above all else."

Victor took a deep breath and adjusted his tone. "My apologies, Your Honor." He glanced at Charlotte briefly before continuing, "But it’s incredibly frustrating to see a victim’s suffering questioned when we’ve provided testimony and evidence that perfectly matches her claims."

The judge considered his words, then took his gavel. "Because there is new evidence that needs to be thoroughly reviewed, the trial of Mrs. Dawson and Mr. Warren will be adjourned to allow both parties adequate time to analyze the submissions and prepare their arguments."

He glanced between Victor and Mr. Anders. "Both attorneys will submit their written responses to the new evidence within five business days. We will reconvene next Friday at 9:00 a.m. to continue the proceedings."

chapters.title: Chapter 19: Shameless Ex-Husband
chapters.content:
Chapter 19: Shameless Ex-Husband

As the judge left the courtroom, Julian immediately approached Charlotte, who was about to leave.

"Charlotte, you still have a chance to return to me." He said, "We can start over, and I promise I will be a better person for you."

Charlotte glared at him, feeling disgusted with her soon-to-be ex-husband. "You’ve said those words hundreds of times, but it is nothing more than an empty promise."

Julian’s jaw clenched, but he forced a smile, trying to keep his composure. "This time I mean it, Charlotte. Look, I know I’ve made mistakes, but don’t let the people around you manipulate you into thinking I’m the enemy."

Charlotte raised an eyebrow and scoffed. "The only person manipulating me is you, Julian."

Julian raised his hand to touch Charlotte’s face. But before he could put his fingers on it, someone pushed him away. "Back off, Julian." Damon glared at him.

Julian stumbled slightly but quickly regained his balance. His eyes narrowed as he looked at his uncle. "She’s still my wife, so why can’t I touch her?"

Damon gritted his teeth, looking like he was trying his best not to punch Julian in the face in the courtroom. "She’s my mate now. If you hurt her, every werewolf in the Crimson Moon pack will come after you."

"Is that a threat?" The corner of Julian’s mouth lifted slightly. "My dear uncle, did you just threaten me? Hey, Mr. Anders, you have to take note of this!"

Damon’s face darkened further, his patience clearly reaching its limit. He took a step toward Julian. "This is not a threat, Julian. It’s something I will do if you dare to hurt Charlotte again."

"You scare me, uncle." Julian taunted him with a smile. "Do you think my father would let you hurt his only son?"

Julian’s father---Ronan Warren---was not only the Alpha of the Silverfang Pack but also the Governor of Suncreast City, a position that was crucial to both werewolf and human society.

He was also the intermediary between the human government and the Nocturnal Order, so many believed Ronan to be untouchable on both sides.

"Your father means nothing to me," Damon said confidently. "He may hold the politics, but I’m the one who runs the economy in this country."

Ronan might control the law and business in Suncreast City, but Damon’s investments extended throughout Hamary Country, even into the Nocturnal Ordel’s covert operations.

So in his eyes, Julian’s talk was nothing more than a kid’s bluff.

Julian wanted to say something else, but Charlotte had already pulled Damon away. "Don’t talk to him anymore. He’s not worth your time."

Besides, Charlotte didn’t want him arguing with Julian in the courtroom and making things worse.

Damon let himself be led away, but his eyes never left Julian. Priscilla stood beside her son the entire time, but she didn’t say anything except to give Damon her fierce glare.

It seemed that Priscilla was beginning to notice her son’s misdeeds, but she refused to believe in those things.

Charlotte gripped Damon’s arm tightly as if to tell him not to fall into Julian’s provocation.

"You’re right." Damon finally turned his eyes to Charlotte. "He’s not worth my time. But if he crosses the line again, I won’t hold back."

Charlotte tightened her grip on him. "No. I don’t want you to get into trouble because of him."

Damon’s expression softened as he looked down at Charlotte. Her concern was evident in her eyes because she didn’t want Julian to bring chaos into his life.

He placed his hand on her head and patted it gently. "Don’t worry," he said, "I know how to deal with people like him without touching them. Julian has a perfume business, right?"

Charlotte nodded. "Why?"

Damon smiled. "Just asking."

Charlotte sensed something suspicious in his tone, but she remained silent. She had forbidden him to hurt Julian in the courtroom, but that didn’t mean she wouldn’t be happy to see Julian finally get what he deserved.

After all, her ex-husband deserved more than a slap on the face.

"Damon," she said carefully, "whatever it is, just ... make sure it’s legal, or at least it won’t cost you to jail."

Damon laughed softly, then opened the car door for her. "No one can put me in jail."

Before Charlotte got in the car, she heard someone call her name. "Mrs. Dawson!"

She turned to see Maria running toward her. She was out of breath, but clearly determined to catch Charlotte before she left.

"Maria?" Charlotte asked, stepping back from the car. "Why aren’t you with your bodyguard?"

Honestly, Charlotte had been trying to avoid Maria and not meet her face-to-face ever since Damon had brought her to this city. It was because she felt guilty for not being able to help her when Julian sent her away.

"I just want to see you, Mrs. Dawson." Maria stood before her, catching her breath before smiling broadly at her. "All this time I couldn’t stop thinking about you and I was afraid I was going to hear bad news about you."

She continued, "But it looks like, I don’t have to worry anymore."

Charlotte froze. Even though Maria had experienced so many bad things because of her, she still cared for her.

How could she? How could someone care about her so much?

"Maria, I’m sorry ..."

"It wasn’t your fault, Mrs. Dawson." Maria held her hands. "We both suffered, but you suffered more than I did, ma’am."

She looked at Damon and Charlotte in turn. "I’m so glad to see you getting better now, Mrs. Dawson." Maria smiled warmly. "I truly hope you’ll get the happiness you deserve."

Happiness? Would she be able to find true happiness in her life?

Her trauma still ran deep in her heart, making her unable to live without thinking of her past self.

How could she feel happy when her heart was still bleeding?

"Thank you, Maria." Charlotte’s eyes softened and she tried her best to put a smile on her face. "Thank you for everything. Without you, I’d never be able to survive alone in that hell."

chapters.title: Chapter 20: Price Tags
chapters.content:
Chapter 20: Price Tags

Charlotte offered to take Maria to dinner, but she politely declined, saying she wanted to visit her family in another city.

"Take care of yourself, Maria," Charlotte said, "and make sure you stay close to your bodyguards."

Damon had assigned three werewolves from the Crimson Moon Pack to protect Maria. It might seem excessive, but considering the lengths Julian would go to silence her, it was a necessary precaution.

"Please take care of yourself as well, Mrs. Dawson." She smiled. "I really hope you’ll be happier with Mr. Sullivan."

Charlotte only gave her a smile in return. For now, at least Damon was able to make her feel safe and sound.

Charlotte then got into the car with Damon, then she waved her hand toward Maria as a goodbye.

"I still feel bad for her," Charlotte said as the car pulled away from the courthouse.

Damon looked at Charlotte. "Charlotte, you don’t have to feel guilty for something you didn’t do. Maria suffered because of Julian, not because of you."

Charlotte lowered her head. "I know, but it’s hard not to feel responsible in some way. If only I had had the courage to stop Julian back then, maybe---"

"Stop right there," Damon interrupted her, "You’ve been through enough. Don’t add guilt that isn’t yours to bear."

"You already did the brave thing by leaving your scum husband. That’s more than enough, Charlotte." Damon repeated, "It’s more than enough."

Charlotte bit her lower lip, trying her best not to shed tears. After being in the courtroom for hours, those words were the last thing she needed to hear.

She whispered under her breath, "I’m enough."

Damon nodded and patted her back gently. "You are enough."

As the car drove away, Charlotte noticed that it wasn’t heading back to Crimson Moon Manor. She looked at Damon curiously. "Where are we going?"

Damon smiled slightly. "You’ll see."

A few minutes later, they stopped in front of a boutique of high-end luxury bags. Charlotte’s eyes widened when she recognized the store. "Damon ... why are we here?"

He turned to her with a knowing look. "I remember seeing one of your interviews back in the day. You talked about your love for collecting luxury bags."

Charlotte blinked, surprised. "You ... saw that?"

She didn’t even remember that interview. However, she did have a soft spot for high-end bags.

Damon nodded. "You looked so passionate when you talked about it. But I noticed you don’t have any of them with you now."

Her expression turned somber. "I sold them all when I married Julian. He said they were a waste of money and sold them to help with his financial problems."

"Nonsense." Damon seemed like a hot plate of anger whenever they talked about Julian. "There’s no such thing as wasting money if it brings you joy. He said that because he didn’t have enough money in his pocket."

He opened the car door and got out, then walked around to open her door. "Come on, let’s forget about court for now."

Charlotte hesitated. "But Damon, I don’t have any money on me."

"Who asked you to pay?" Damon raised an eyebrow. "Just pick whatever you want and don’t worry about money as long as you’re with me."

Really ... Charlotte didn’t understand how she could have such a perfect mate like him.

She took his hand and stepped into the shop. The scent of leather and floral perfume filled the air, relaxing every muscle in Charlotte’s body.

The luxurious interior was immaculate, with glass displays, plush carpeting, and lighting that made the handbags look like works of art.

She felt a sense of nostalgia as she looked around, remembering how she used to visit places like this every week before she married Julian.

"Welcome to Maison Luxe," a senior assistant greeted her warmly. Her eyes widened slightly when she saw Charlotte. "Oh, Miss Charlotte?"

Charlotte was taken aback when the assistant recognized her. She looked her in the face and said, "Audrey?"

The assistant smiled and nodded. "Yes, it’s me, Audrey. I can’t believe you remember me, Miss Charlotte."

"No, it’s actually the opposite, I thought you wouldn’t remember me," Charlotte said.

"Your face is not easy to forget, Miss Charlotte," Audrey replied with a polite smile. "You were one of our most loyal customers some years ago. It’s been a while since we’ve seen you here."

Charlotte gave her a shy smile. "Yes, well ... a lot of things have changed since then."

The assistant seemed to sense something wrong in her tone and quickly changed the subject. "It’s wonderful to have you back, Miss Charlotte."

She continued, "Let me show you our latest collection. I think there’s a piece you’ll absolutely adore."

Charlotte looked back at Damon as if asking for his approval.

Damon gave her a small nod, his lips curling into a reassuring smile. "Go ahead," he said quietly. "Take your time."

Charlotte turned back to Audrey, her heart warming at Damon’s words. "All right, let’s see what you got."

The assistant led them to a private area of the store where the most exclusive pieces were displayed. "This just arrived from our spring collection," Audrey said, lifting a sleek bag in a rich emerald green with gold accents. "It’s a limited edition, only five in the world."

Charlotte’s eyes widened as she admired the bag. She gently ran her fingers over the smooth leather. "It’s beautiful."

Damon, standing a few steps away, spoke up. "Do you love it?"

Charlotte hesitated, glancing at the price tag. It was more expensive than she had imagined. "It’s beautiful, but ... maybe I should look at something else."

Damon sighed, stepping closer. He whispered next to her ear, "Charlotte, I didn’t bring you here to look at price tags. If you love it, it’s yours."

Damon turned his head to Audrey. "She’ll take this one."

Audrey nodded and smiled professionally. She recognized Damon’s face because he had been one of the top 100 richest men in the world for years.

She was actually quite curious as to why Charlotte had suddenly appeared with Damon Sullivan, but she decided to keep quiet and do her job instead. "Of course, Mr. Sullivan. I’ll have it wrapped for you right away."

chapters.title: Chapter 21: Extreme Pampering
chapters.content:
Chapter 21: Extreme Pampering

Charlotte’s cheeks flushed as she turned to Damon. "You didn’t even give me a chance to decide."

Damon chuckled. "I could see it in your eyes. You’ve already fallen for it."

Charlotte’s eyes roamed the shelves, admiring the bags. But suddenly her eyes landed on a vintage bag displayed in the corner.

It was the old summer collection that she had owned years ago. The bag wasn’t just beautiful, it brought back memories.

Charlotte slowly approached it, her fingers brushing the familiar leather. "I used to have one just like this," she murmured, almost to herself.

Damon wanted to ask what had happened, but he stopped himself. The answer was obvious, and the thought of Julian’s control over her life made his blood boil.

"Julian sold it, didn’t he?" Damon said in a soft tone.

Charlotte nodded slightly. "He needed more money for his business, so he sold all my bags."

Her closet, once filled with dozens of luxury bags she had carefully collected over the years, had been emptied without her consent.

"I remember how excited I was when I bought this one," she said softly. "I saved my first paycheck to buy it. It was one of my favorites."

It was a pity she wasn’t able to prevent Julian from selling it.

"Do you see any other bags you bought back then?" asked Damon casually.

Charlotte thought he was just asking out of curiosity, so she pointed at a bunch of bags without thinking. "That one, that one, and that one."

Damon followed her finger as she pointed to each bag and noticed that her smile widened when she saw those bags. He asked, "Tell me about them."

Charlotte proudly told Damon about the memories behind the bags. "This one," she gestured to a bright red bag, "I bought after my first promotion at the company. It was a reward for all the hard work I’d put in."

There were six familiar bags in all, and they all held fond memories of her glamorous life back then.

"But yeah ... They were just my past." She looked a little sad after remembering that she had lost a lot of things in such a short time. "What a pity."

"Miss Charlotte, I packed your bags. Would you like to take a look at the others as well?" Audrey asked, standing with a polite smile and holding the wrapped bags.

Charlotte shook her head. "No—"

"Yes." Damon cut her off. "She wants to take that, that one ...."

Damon pointed to each of the bags Charlotte had mentioned earlier. Audrey smiled broadly and immediately ran to the bags. "I’ll also pack them all for you, Mr. Sullivan!"

On the other side, Charlotte’s face went pale. She hadn’t expected Damon to spend so much money just to buy her bags.

"Damon, you don’t have to—"

"I want to," Damon didn’t let her finish. "You deserve it, Charlotte. You shouldn’t have to think twice about what makes you happy."

Of course, Charlotte had to think twice before buying something. It wasn’t her own money she was spending, it was Damon’s!

He had been spoiling her too much lately. She gladly accepted his kindness, but there was a part of her that was afraid that people would think of her the same as Julian.

What if Damon lost all his money after spending too much on Charlotte?

The thought filled her mind, but she quickly dismissed it.

It seemed utterly impossible.

Damon Sullivan wasn’t just wealthy, he was one of the top 100 richest men in the world for a reason. Unlike Julian, who built his business by stealing other people’s money, Damon built his fortune with his own sweat and tears.

Well, his family fortune played an important role in his success, but that didn’t mean he couldn’t have succeeded without his own hard work.

Just look at Julian, his parents were rich, but he was too stupid to take advantage of it and preferred to squander his money since he was a teenager.

Damon leaned against her. "Is there another bag you like?"

Charlotte immediately shook her head. "No, no, that’s enough! Please don’t spend too much money on me."

"Why?" Damon chuckled. "I don’t mind spending more money to make you smile more often. Your smile is beautiful, Charlotte. More beautiful than your tears."

Charlotte’s heart skipped a beat at his words. She tried to brush it off, but her cheeks betrayed her, warming with a soft blush. "I ... I’ll try to smile more then."

After Damon paid for all the bags, they left the store, the staff sending them off with warm smiles and polite bows. Audrey said, "Thank you for visiting, Mr. Sullivan, Miss Charlotte. Please don’t hesitate to come back anytime."

• •

When they arrived at Crimson Moon Manor, they met Louis and his family, who were about to leave the manor.

"Wow, what’s the occasion?" Louis looked at all the shopping bags in the trunk. "Did you win the trial?"

Charlotte smiled shyly. "It’s actually the opposite. The trial starts again next week."

Louis raised an eyebrow and looked at Damon. "So what is all this? Shopping therapy?"

Vera nudged her husband. "Oh, shut up Louis. A woman doesn’t need a special occasion to go shopping. It’s just our ritual to calm our minds."

"But my wallet isn’t calm."

Vera gave him a sharp look. "What’s the point of having a husband if I still have to use my own wallet?"

While Louis and Vera talked as if they were arguing, but actually just joking, Haven walked over to Charlotte with a big smile on her face. "Auntie, I’m going to the lake with Mommy and Daddy! Do you want to come with us?"

Charlotte knelt down in front of her and stroked her soft hair. "I’d love to, but I’m a little tired today. Maybe next time."

Besides, Charlotte didn’t want to ruin their family day by coming along.

Haven was disappointed, but she didn’t seem too upset. "All right, let’s go together next time!"

Louis picked up his daughter and whispered to them, "Mother will be home with Carmen tonight." He said, "I’ll give you some time to talk to them."

chapters.title: Chapter 22: Women Who Share Pain
chapters.content:
Chapter 22: Women Who Share Pain

Charlotte didn’t know what Carmen would say once they met. Their last meeting had been chaotic and filled with harsh words, accusations, and misunderstandings.

It was a moment Charlotte had tried hard to forget, but the sting of Carmen’s judgment lingered like an old wound in her heart.

"Do you think Carmen will hate me?" she asked Damon.

Damon, reading documents on his tablet, replied, "Who cares what her opinion is? She is not the leader of this pack."

Charlotte smiled slightly at Damon’s bluntness, but his words didn’t completely calm her nerves. "She may not be the leader of this pack, but she is our closest family member. That’s why her opinion matters to me."

Besides, Carmen was Damon’s stepmother, so it would be bad for Charlotte if Carmen didn’t approve of their relationship.

Charlotte sighed inwardly. Having only one mother-in-law was hard enough, but now she had two.

Damon finally put down his tablet and turned to her. "Charlotte, people who care about you will take the time to understand you. If Carmen refuses to understand your situation, that’s her problem, not yours."

Charlotte sighed and leaned back on the sofa. "You make it sound so simple."

"It is simple, sweetheart." Damon reminded her in a gentle tone. "You can’t waste your life chasing the approval of someone who only sees the worst in you."

His words meant a lot, but Charlotte’s mind chose to play his ’sweetheart’ over and over again in her mind.

Her cheeks turned a soft pink as her heart fluttered at the way he said it so effortlessly as if it were the most natural thing in the world.

Charlotte shook her head quickly, trying to put the thought out of her mind. She must have fluttered easily because of their bond. No wonder many werewolves tried so hard to chase their true mates so hard.

"What are you thinking now?" asked Damon when he noticed that Charlotte had stopped talking. "Do you have a fever? Your face looks so red."

When Damon reached out to touch her forehead, Charlotte immediately backed away. "No, I’m fine! I’m perfectly fine. It’s just the air, the air is a bit hot tonight."

The air, in fact, felt cold tonight, but Damon had no chance to ask further because they finally heard the sound of a car pulling up to the manor.

It had to be Carmen and Mona.

"Let’s ... let’s go greet them at the door," Charlotte said hesitantly.

Actually, Damon still thought that Carmen’s approval was not important for his relationship with Charlotte. But as long as it made his mate happy, he would do his best to support her.

"Carmen, you must remember our discussion last night." Charlotte could faintly hear Mona’s voice from a distance. Next to her, Carmen sighed harshly and said, "Can you be quiet for a moment, Mona? Of course I remember, I don’t have dementia yet."

Mona was about to reply to her words but stopped midway when she saw Charlotte and Damon at the door.

The corner of her mouth lifted and she waved at them. "Damon, my dearest son. It’s so rare to see you greet your mother like this."

Mona’s smile widened as she turned to Charlotte. "I only left you for a few weeks, but your mate has had a great influence on you."

Charlotte smiled shyly. "I didn’t do anything to him."

Mona chuckled softly, her eyes gleaming with warmth. "Oh dear, you’ve done more than you realize. My Louis says his brother smiles more often these days."

"Mother, please." Damon sighed helplessly. "You know how Louis likes to exaggerate things."

"No, I don’t think he talks nonsense this time." Mona leaned in and whispered, "You have been smitten, haven’t you?"

Damon lowered his voice so no one could hear their conversation. "Mother, don’t start."

As Mona teased her son so badly, Carmen and Charlotte silently exchanged glances. Charlotte didn’t know what was going on in her head, but she could see that Carmen seemed to want to talk about something important.

"Charlotte," she called her name. "I see you’re still here."

Charlotte smiled politely and put her hands behind her back. "Yes, I’m still here," she said hesitantly, unsure of what Carmen would say next.

Carmen crossed her arms and took a step closer. She had the face of a kind lady, but her sharp gaze made Charlotte stop breathing for a second. "We didn’t get to finish our conversation last time."

Charlotte tensed, her mind flashing back to their last heated encounter. Carmen had stormed out of the room before the meeting had even begun. For some reason, Charlotte assumed that Carmen still held a grudge against her.

"I know my relationship with Damon looks like a big scandal, but I---"

"No," Carmen interrupted. "This isn’t about that." Her tone softened, and for a moment she looked almost ... apologetic.

"I was wrong, Charlotte," Carmen admitted after a short pause. "I judged you too quickly and too harshly. I even blame you for not fighting back when my scumbag grandson abused you. That wasn’t fair, Charlotte."

"That wasn’t fair because I vented my failure in fighting my abuser to you. I feel angry ... because I was in your shoes and I failed to run away while you had a chance to build a whole new life."

At that time, Carmen did not have the courage to run away because she was afraid that her daughters’ lives would be miserable because of her stupid decision. So she decided to endure all the hardships of her marriage.

Even when her husband brought another woman into their home, she decided to swallow a bitter pill and accept his new wife.

However, her husband didn’t treat his new wife with kindness either, and Mona’s life ended up being just as miserable as Carmen’s.

They were both victims of their bastard husband, which was why they refused to hate each other and chose to hate their husband to the core instead.

If Charlotte wanted to find women who could understand her pain, then Mona and Carmen would be her answer.

"I’m sorry, Charlotte." Carmen took a deep breath before she said, "I can’t erase the pain in your heart, but I will make sure my grandson pays the price for hurting his wife."

chapters.title: Chapter 23: Rejection
chapters.content:
Chapter 23: Rejection

Did she hear it wrong?

Charlotte was not sure if she was hallucinating or if Carmen was truly apologizing to her.

Wasn’t Carmen supposed to hate her? Disgusted by her?

But why ... Why was she suddenly standing by her side?

Charlotte stared at Carmen, her thoughts spinning in confusion. She had prepared herself for hostility, for a scream or a slap, but instead, she was met with something unexpected, a heartfelt apology.

No one had ever apologized to her before.

Julian always insisted that her pain was her own fault. He’d remind her time and time again that she had chosen to marry him, and it was her weakness that kept her trapped in a failed marriage.

His mother had been no better, constantly manipulating her into believing she was overreacting, making her feel as though her struggles were trivial and unworthy of attention.

But Carmen ... Carmen had done what they never did. She was the first to say the words Charlotte had desperately longed to hear. A real, genuine apology.

"I failed to raise one of my daughters and, in doing so, turned her into what I despise most," Carmen said with a weary sigh. "Someone who stands by the abuser."

Charlotte looked down, struggling to find the right words. "I ... I don’t know what to say. I thought you’d hate me."

"Hate you?" Carmen shook her head firmly and placed a comforting hand on Charlotte’s shoulder. "No, dear. I could never hate you. After all, you’ll soon be my daughter-in-law."

Before Charlotte could respond, Carmen reached into her purse and pulled out a sleek tablet. She handed it over to Charlotte with a faint smile. "This is my apology gift to you."

Charlotte furrowed her brows in confusion as she powered on the device. Her jaw dropped as page after page of incriminating information about Julian’s business appeared on the screen.

"This ..." Charlotte’s voice was barely above a whisper. "How did you get all of this?"

"I have my ways," Carmen replied.

The screen displayed detailed information about Julian’s business background, the manufacturers he used, where he bought the product materials, and the information about the product’s scandal.

Scandal?

Charlotte had never heard of his company’s scandal before.

[Toxic Truth: Customers Expose Hidden Dangers in Noire Bloom Perfumes!]

Some of the customers shared their stories of using Noire Bloom perfumes.

"My skin burned after just one spray of their ’Eternal Rose’ scent," wrote one user.

Another commented, "I trusted Noire Bloom to be luxury, but they’re using harsh chemicals!"

[Stolen fragrances? Noire Bloom allegedly copied a small perfumery’s formula!]

A small perfumery, Aroma Haven, had accused Noire Bloom of stealing their signature scent, Moonlit Reverie.

The indie brand claimed they had pitched the formula to Noire Bloom two years ago during a collaboration proposal, only to have it rejected and then copied without their permission.

"I don’t understand," Charlotte said, clearly confused. "With news like this, how is Julian’s business still thriving? I don’t see any reports about customers boycotting his products."

Carmen sighed and explained, "If you’re wondering where your money went, here’s your answer. Julian spent a fortune suppressing bad press about his products. That’s the only reason his company is still standing today."

Damon said, "He can suppress the news now because only a few media portals cover it. But if I spread the story to major media outlets, Julian won’t be able to bury it."

After being in showbiz for many years, Charlotte knew better than most how quickly the media could ruin someone’s life.

A single story could tear down even the most carefully constructed reputation in seconds.

If someone like Damon played a part in bringing down his company’s reputation, then Julian didn’t have a chance to fix it.

However, achieving such a result would require a significant investment from Damon. It wasn’t a small gamble, it was a calculated risk that came with a hefty price tag.

Is it worth it for him?

The one who truly stood to benefit from all this was Charlotte, not Damon.

But did she really deserve it?

"See? We can solve every problem if we work well together." Mona nudged Carmen playfully on the arm, a smug grin on her face. "I’m proud of you, Carmen."

Carmen clicked her tongue in annoyance. "Oh, shut up." She started walking toward the manor, her heels clicking sharply on the floor. "If you have nothing to talk again, I’m going to my room."

"I haven’t had a decent night’s sleep in days because some annoying person insists on having conversations all the times."

Mona trailed after her, clearly unbothered. "I never forced you to wake up, though. Just admit it, you like talking to me, too."

Carmen gave a sharp glance over her shoulder but said nothing, her pace quickening as Mona continued to tease her.

Their voices slowly faded as they walked to the second floor, leaving Charlotte alone with Damon.

"Damon," she called to him. "Do you want something from me?"

Damon raised an eyebrow. "What do you mean?"

Charlotte took a deep breath before saying, "You have done a lot for me, but I have never repaid you. That’s why I ... I can offer something else."

"What else?"

She lowered her head and bit her lips. "You’re my mate, but we’ve only had sex once. I ... I don’t mind to---"

"Stop," Damon interrupted firmly, his tone laced with disbelief. He leaned closer, his gray eyes locking onto hers. "Are you saying you want to pay me back by having sex with me?"

Charlotte’s head lowered further as she nodded.

Damon exhaled deeply, running a hand through his dark hair. "Then I reject your offer."

Charlotte’s head snapped up, her eyes wide with confusion. "What? Why?" She suddenly panicked. "Is it because my body can’t satisfy you?"

A bitter thought clawed its way into her mind. Or was it because she wasn’t beautiful enough in his eyes?

Charlotte knew it.

She shouldn’t have eaten so many muffins in one day.

"That’s ... not the reason, Charlotte." Damon could see a hint of panic in her face, so he softened his tone. "There’s nothing wrong with you."

He reached out, his fingers brushing her cheek in a tender caress. "I just don’t see you as a transaction, or something to be used. And honestly ..." He hesitated, choosing his words carefully. "I don’t think you’re ready."

Charlotte’s breath hitched as his thumb traced soothing circles against her skin.

He continued, "We did it once because it had to be done, but now you have no obligation to satisfy me."

What? What did he say?

Was he serious?

What kind of man would refuse a woman’s temptation, especially his mate?

Julian had always forced Charlotte to have sex with him whenever he wanted.

Charlotte looked away. "But ... I have nothing else to give you. You’ve done so much for me, and I feel so ... useless."

"Then don’t think about what you can give me," Damon said, "You’re my mate, Charlotte. I take care of you because I want to, not because I expect anything in return."

chapters.title: Chapter 24: The First Step of Freedom
chapters.content:
Chapter 24: The First Step of Freedom

"Charlotte, are you awake?" A loud knock rattled her door in the early morning.

Charlotte groaned and rubbed her eyes to shake off the lingering sleepiness. Glancing at the clock on her bedside table, she sighed in exasperation. It was barely 5 a.m.

She had stayed up late practicing her script and had only managed to fall asleep around 3 a.m. Now, irritation simmered beneath her exhaustion as the relentless knocking continued.

"What’s going on?" she yanked the door open, but still tried to maintain her politeness.

She was surprised to find Vera standing behind the door. Since Carmen and Mona had returned to the manor, they had been spending more time together and had become closer than before.

But even though their relationship had become good, it didn’t mean that she had to wake up Charlotte before sunrise. Besides, she had to be at the courthouse by nine, so Charlotte wanted to use the remaining time to sleep.

Before Charlotte could say another word, Vera shoved her phone screen in her face. "The news. Have you seen the news?"

Charlotte creased her forehead and took the phone from Vera’s hand. "What news?"

"Charlotte," Vera said excitedly, "I think you’re going to win your case today."

Charlotte’s eyes darted to the screen, her stomach churning as she read the headline:

[Noire Bloom Perfume: Toxic Ingredients Cause Severe Skin Burns in Customers].

Charlotte held her breath for a moment. Many customers who had suffered skin burns from Noire Bloom products had come together to make a statement.

Back then, only small news portals carried their stories, but now there were at least five major news portals that had reported Noire Bloom’s infamy.

[A Small Perfumery Has Files Lawsuit Against Noire Bloom Due to Copied Formula!]

Aroma Haven did not dare to take the case to court because they were sure that Julian would win the case with his money.

However, based on the current situation, Charlotte suspected that Damon must have contacted Aroma Haven and offered them a great deal of help.

She knew that Damon had been trying to wreak havoc on Julian’s business, but she hadn’t expected him to drop the bomb on the day of the trial.

"With a case like this, Julian definitely won’t be able to come to court, right?" Vera said confidentially.

Charlotte nodded. "I hope so."

The trial would still go on even if Julian didn’t show up, but at least Charlotte had a chance to win her divorce case faster.

"I really hope I can divorce him today," she whispered.

• • •

As she suspected, Julian didn’t attend the court, leaving his attorney to handle their divorce case.

"I don’t see his mother either," Charlotte said, glancing around the courtroom.

"That’s good," Damon replied calmly, standing close to her. "Less drama to deal with."

"Did you do something to her, too?" Charlotte asked, her eyes narrowing suspiciously.

"I did nothing," Damon replied with a slight smile. Then he added, "But her mother did."

Charlotte’s mind raced. Carmen?

No wonder she hadn’t received any vile messages or threats from Priscilla in days. The woman who had once made it her mission to torment Charlotte had seemingly disappeared into silence.

Charlotte didn’t ask what Carmen had done to her daughter, because the important thing was that her life was getting calmer these days.

"Hey, Charlotte." Damon held her hand before she walked over to the plaintiff’s table. "You will officially be a divorcee today."

Any other woman might have found those words offensive. But for Charlotte, the word "divorcee" had a different meaning—it meant freedom.

The beginning of a new life.

She couldn’t help but smile. "Oh, I can’t wait."

The younger version of herself would have been terrified at the thought of becoming a divorcee, seeing it as a symbol of failure and shame. But the woman she was now?

She’d throw confetti in celebration.

More than that, this moment meant something even greater. Once she broke free of Julian, she could finally register her name as Luna of the Crimson Moon Pack.

And with that, she would be able to sign the marriage certificate with Damon.

Ah, marriage?

She hadn’t thought about it much further and Damon had never brought it up either.

Actually, the marital status between two werewolves wasn’t that important as long as they were already registered as mates, especially if she mated with an Alpha.

The reason most werewolves chose to marry in the modern world was to gain legal recognition of their relationship and benefit from practical advantages, such as reduced taxes by living together as a married couple.

But in Damon’s case, Charlotte was sure he had no concerns about taxes at all.

"Mrs. Dawson," Victor greeted her, interrupting her random thoughts. "I have news. Mr. Warren officially did not attend the hearing today."

"Great." Charlotte smiled. "I don’t know what will happen in court today, but at least I won’t have to see his face."

A few minutes later, the judge entered the courtroom. He knocked his gavel once. "Good morning, everyone. We reconvene today for the continuation of the divorce proceedings between Mrs. Charlotte Dawson and Mr. Julian Warren."

"The court has reviewed the newly submitted evidence as well as the written responses from the legal representatives of both parties."

The judge looked around the courtroom, pausing for a moment to ensure proper attention.

"After so much reconsideration of the new evidence, and after considering the testimony and financial documents, I find that there are sufficient grounds to proceed with the divorce."

Charlotte’s eyes lit up with excitement. The words she had been dying to hear were finally coming out of the judge’s mouth.

The judge continued, "I do not find it necessary to hear further arguments from counsel at this time."

He lowered his head to look at the papers in his hands. "And the matter of Mr. Warren’s conduct with many female humans will be dealt with in a separate hearing."

Charlotte couldn’t believe that the trial was going so smoothly. If she had known it would be like this, she would have been thrilled to see Julian’s expression when he failed to defend their marriage.

The matter of her fortune had not yet been resolved, but at least she could finally escape the status of being Julian’s wife.

Charlotte immediately turned to look at Damon. She smiled brightly and mouthed without making any sound, "I’m free."

chapters.title: Chapter 25: Happily Divorced!
chapters.content:
Chapter 25: Happily Divorced!

The judge said, "And the matter of assets and alimony will be addressed separately in the coming days."

Mr. Anders quickly stood up. "Your Honor, if I may, I believe there are assets that should be addressed immediately."

The judged, "I understand your concerns, Mr. Anders. However, the court has already determined that such matters will be handled in the separated session due to complicated matters."

The judge continued, "This decision ensures that all financial matters are reviewed thoroughly and fairly. Rushing this process would not serve justice."

Charlotte could clearly see the tension on Mr. Anders’s face. He clenched his jaw, his fingers gripping his pen tightly.

After receiving the hard blow to his business, Julian must have wanted to secure all of his assets as soon as possible.

Fortunately, the judge was a fair person, so he declined Mr. Anders’ suggestion without hesitation.

Not long after, the judge’s gavel struck again. "Court dismissed."

Charlotte immediately stood up from her chair and dashed toward Damon. Ignoring the presence of everyone else in the room, she threw her body to him.

"It’s over." Charlotte hugged him and buried her face in his chest. "I’ve finally divorced him."

Damon chuckled softly, wrapping his arms around her. "You have no idea how glad I am to hear that, Charlotte."

She pulled back just enough to look up at his face. "I don’t think I could have done this without you, Damon."

For her, Damon was like the moonlight in the middle of the night.

He guided her to walk through the darkness. She was scared, helpless, and weak, but as long as she could see the light, she kept walking without looking back.

If she didn’t try to call him that day, maybe she was still stuck in the deepest abyss for the rest of her life.

"I helped you." Damon stroked her long hair. "But you’re the one who fought so hard to win, so let’s take a moment to thank yourself."

Charlotte smiled brightly, nodding. "Thank you, Charlotte."

It felt strange to say it, but at the same time, it felt surprisingly good.

She also expressed her gratitude to her legal team and Maria who had been supporting her for so long. Without all of them, maybe Charlotte would never have had the chance to be free.

"I’m so proud of you, Mrs. Dawson." Maria gave her a warm embrace. "I hope you’ll never meet anyone like Mr. Warren again in your life."

"I hope so." Charlotte said, "What do you want to do after this, Maria? If you need anything, just tell me."

"I will open a fruit store with my family. After leaving them for a few years, I don’t want to leave anymore." Maria continued, "and please don’t say sorry to me, Mrs. Dawson."

Charlotte couldn’t help but laugh softly. "I won’t. Not today."

Although Maria would not need to testify in the next hearing, Damon insisted on assigning her a bodyguard to ensure her safety for the time being.

After finalizing her divorce papers, Charlotte and Damon finally returned home.

She had planned to continue her sleep the moment she get into the manor, but something unexpected happened.

"Congratulations, Charlotte!"

Confetti burst into the air and fell on Charlotte’s brunette hair. Vera and Haven approached her, carrying a two-layer cake topped with the words "Happily Divorcee" written in elegant script.

Charlotte blinked then laughed. "Oh my, did you already prepare this before?" She took the cake from Haven’s hands.

"We did!" Haven said enthusiastically. "My mommy said we have to celebrate everything."

Even a divorce?

Charlotte chuckled to herself, thinking that the kid probably didn’t realize that most people wouldn’t celebrate a divorce.

Her eyes moved toward the people standing before her. She didn’t expect to see many people waiting for her in the house, even Theodore came along with them.

Carmen gave her a genuine smile. "I’d be lying if I said I wasn’t a little jealous of your freedom."

Mona nudged her arm with a playful grin. "Oh, don’t be jealous. We got our freedom and an inheritance by sending our husband to the grave."

"Mom! I’ve told you not to make dark jokes in front of Haven!" Louis covered his daughter’s ears. "Don’t worry, baby. Your Daddy is still a great example of a perfect husband."

Haven tilted her head and looked at them in confusion. Before the atmosphere became darker, Vera clapped her hand and said, "Who wants some cake?"

Haven’s face lit up as she raised her hand. "Me! Me!" she exclaimed.

"Alright, let’s cut the cake now," said Charlotte.

Just a few weeks ago, Charlotte had never dreamt of having a big, warm family in her house. She didn’t even dare to imagine it, knowing how painful it would be to face the reality.

The idea of a home filled with love and laughter had seemed out of reach, too distant, and too impossible.

But now, as she looked around at the faces of the people around her, she realized she had achieved something once thought impossible for five years.

After living in hell for so many years, she was finally able to taste a bit of heaven.

Just as Charlotte was savoring the moment, her phone suddenly rang, breaking the peace.

She walked away from the crowd before she glanced at the screen. "Mom?" Charlotte spoke lightly, almost sounding like a whisper.

All this time, she rarely talked to her parents because Julian limited her communication with them.

Besides that, Charlotte also didn’t want her parents to know about the struggles she had endured during her marriage to Julian.

But deep down, she knew she couldn’t hide the truth forever.

Sooner or later, they would find out.

"Charlotte, dear, we saw the bad news about your husband’s business." Her mother’s voice was as sweet as honey, but there was an underlying concern. "Are you alright, sweetheart?"

"If you need anything, your Dad and I are here for you," she continued, her tone hesitant. "And if you want ... you can always come home for a while. We miss you so much."

"Mom." Charlotte struggled to maintain her composure, but the words felt like a dam breaking inside her.

She held back a sob, but the emotions she had been holding onto for so long flooded her all at once. "Mom, actually ... actually ... I have divorced Julian. I’m sorry, Mom."

Charlotte didn’t like to cry when she was around other people, but once she heard her mother’s voice, she couldn’t hold it in any longer.

The floodgates opened, and tears streamed down her face as she let herself break down.

Damon was the one who approached her. He hugged her without saying a word, giving her space to talk to her parents.

"Mom, I really miss you and Dad." Charlotte talked while sobbing, "Can you come here?"

chapters.title: Chapter 26: The Angry Parents
chapters.content:
Chapter 26: The Angry Parents

A helicopter descended onto the private landing pad near the Crimson Moon manor. As the helicopter door opened, Charlotte’s parents stepped out, their faces filled with anger and frustration.

After hearing their daughter’s cry through the phone, her father, Derek Dawson, immediately rented a helicopter to fly from Velmoria City to Northbridge City.

For five years, they struggled to stay in touch with their daughter after she married Julian. They assumed Charlotte was simply busy and tried to be understanding of her situation.

However, after learning the harsh truth, they finally realized the real reason their daughter had never come home for so many years.

Damon walked to them when they descended from the helicopter. "Alpha Derek, Luna Hattie. I—"

"Where is my daughter?" Hattie Dawson—Charlotte’s mother—immediately cut off his words. After knowing what Julian had done to her sweet daughter, she couldn’t bear to see any of his family’s faces.

Damon didn’t get angry over her attitude and tried to understand. "She is in the manor. Please, follow me."

Hattie exchanged a quick glance with her husband before following Damon without a word. Her face that had so much resemblance to Charlotte seemed cold and fierce, while Derek still had a bit of warmth on his face.

Once they stepped into the manor, Hattie’s icy demeanor cracked the moment she saw her daughter’s face in the living room.

Charlotte broke into tears and all of her strength immediately vanished when she faced her parents. "Mom, Dad ...."

Hattie rushed forward, pulling her daughter into a tight embrace. "Oh, my baby girl," she whispered.

Derek stroked her hair in gentle motion, looking guilty because he let his daughter suffer in silence. "I’m so sorry ... we should have known sooner."

"No ... it’s not your fault." Charlotte lifted her face to see her father. "I was the one who forbade you to visit me and Julian."

Derek gritted his teeth when he heard that despicable name. "It’s because of him! How dare he make my little girl suffer!"

When Charlotte announced her decision to marry Julian, her family didn’t take the news well. Julian didn’t have a steady job at the time and was too dependent on his parents.

Furthermore, Charlotte’s sudden desire to retire from the entertainment industry made them worry about her future.

They knew better than anyone else that their daughter had a deep passion for acting and had always dreamed of becoming a successful actress.

The thought of her giving up that dream so abruptly made them suspicious that Julian had coerced her into quitting.

However, Charlotte assured them that Julian didn’t force her to quit. She explained that she wanted to focus on building a family, and her career would become an obstacle to that.

Besides that, Charlotte’s savings and assets were more than enough to support her in the long run.

Though her parents remained hesitant at first, they eventually accepted Julian after seeing how happy he made their daughter.

"I let you leave our pack in hopes that he could make you happier, but ..." Derek clenched his fists tightly. "... he’s hurting you instead ... oh, my sweet little girl."

Derek Dawson was the Alpha of Moonfire Pack. Compared to Silverfang and Crimson Moon, Moonfire was a small pack with no more than 30 werewolves, but it was still prosperous.

The Dawson family ran a successful business in the fashion industry, and Hattie was a renowned fashion designer whose name was recognized internationally.

That was why they couldn’t allow their beloved only daughter to marry someone they considered a failure.

If Charlotte hadn’t been so determined, they would never have allowed Julian to be with her.

"Mom, I’m sorry I wasn’t trying hard enough to contact you. I really ... really want to tell you everything, but ... I ... I was afraid Julian would hurt you and Dad too."

He was insane and unpredictable. Who knew what kind of scheme he hid behind his sleeve.

After being manipulated too much by him, Charlotte was becoming more timid and lacked confidence. Hence, she didn’t want to take a risk by involving her parents.

"Charlotte, listen to Mom." Hattie cupped her daughter’s face. "Don’t ever feel sorry for that. You did nothing wrong and I’m glad you have the courage to walk away from him."

"But this time, your Dad and I will never let him hurt you anymore." Hattie said firmly, "If he tries to touch you, even just your hair, I’ll make sure to bite him to death."

Derek said, "You don’t have to worry about us, honey. If he wants to hurt us, then we’ll make sure he regrets it."

Charlotte finally realized that her fear had been nothing more than the result of Julian’s manipulation.

How could she have been afraid that Julian would hurt her parents when, in reality, her parents were strong and capable of protecting themselves?

But, that was not the only reason why Charlotte couldn’t tell them everything.

Whenever she called her parents, Julian would listen to their conversations. If she chose the wrong words, he would punish her severely.

The combination of abuse and manipulation from him was enough to break her spirits to pieces.

"Let’s pack your things now." Hattie said, "You will come with us to Velmoria."

Charlotte bit her lower lips, then looked at Damon through the corner of her eyes. "Mom, I can’t ... I ... I’m the Luna of the Crimson Moon pack now."
