"You what?!" Hattie quickly turned her head to Damon and sharpened her gaze. "Is this one of your family trick to torture my daughter?!"

Derek growled at him, ready to shift into his wolf form. "How dare you put your mark on my daughter after knowing what your nephew did to her!"

"Did you force her to take your mark?!" Derek growled, stepping forward.

But before he could get any closer to Damon, Charlotte quickly grabbed his hand. "No, Dad! Mom! This is not what you think it is!"

"He is my true mate from the beginning!" Charlotte exclaimed. "And I was the one who asked him to renew our bond!"

chapters.title: Chapter 27: A Deal With Her Parents
chapters.content:
Chapter 27: A Deal With Her Parents

Damon sat across from Derek and Hattie, while Charlotte sat between her parents nervously.

They decided to move into Damon’s study room to have more privacy, but Charlotte thought the close room made the atmosphere feel heavy and awkward.

Hattie’s eyes never left Damon as she spoke. "Explain your relationship with my daughter."

Charlotte nudged on her mom’s shirt, whispering, "Mom, I told you—"

"No, not you sweetie. I want him to answer it by himself."

The last time she allowed Charlotte to defend her mate, the results had been disastrous. Therefore, she wanted to judge Damon without her daughter’s involvement.

Damon, on the other side, appeared a bit stiff, his calm demeanor suddenly vanishing under the intensity of Hattie’s gaze.

It never occurred to Charlotte that her mother was capable of making a strong and respectable alpha like Damon nervous.

He cleared his throat before speaking, "I understand your concern, Mrs. Dawson. My family issue is not something I am proud of, but I assure you, I don’t have any intention to hurt your daughter."

"Otherwise, I marked Charlotte to protect her from Julian," Damon continued. "As you may know, Julian could track Charlotte’s whereabouts through his mark on her. Forcibly breaking their bond would have caused her immense pain, and I couldn’t let her endure that."

Hattie and Derek were well aware that letting the Nocturnal Order break Charlotte’s bond with Julian could leave her permanently scarred.

They could never allow their sweet daughter to endure such a fate.

Hattie softened her expression. "The last time I entrusted my daughter to one of your family, she suffered more than I could have imagined. How can I be certain this time will be different?"

Derek also spoke up, his deep voice filled with skepticism. "We’ve seen what happens when someone promises to protect our daughter and then fails. Words mean little to us now, Damon. How will you ensure Charlotte never goes through that pain again?"

Damon replied firmly, "I’ve taken a blood oath, swearing on my blood that I will never harm Charlotte or raise a hand against her."

"I vowed to protect her from any danger, including myself, and to remain loyal to her until my last breath."

Derek and Hattie widened their eyes at the same time. They couldn’t believe what they had heard.

"You’re aware of the consequences if you break that oath, aren’t you?" Derek asked in disbelief.

If he broke it, then he would die in a painful way. Therefore, it was rare for an alpha to form a blood oath to others, even their own Luna.

"I know it very well," Damon replied firmly. "But as you said, words alone aren’t enough to prove my sincerity to Charlotte."

He straightened his posture, his gray eyes meeting Derek and Hattie’s unflinchingly. "I promise to take full responsibility for Charlotte’s happiness, safety, and well-being."

And he had proven it to her from the day he took a blood oath.

"Mom, Dad ..." Charlotte assured them, "Damon has already done so much for me. He helped me escape Julian, stood by me through the divorce, and gave me a chance to rebuild my life. I wouldn’t be here without him."

"Please, Dad, give me one last chance to be with my mate," Charlotte pleaded, clutching her father’s arm gently. "I promise things will be different this time."

Derek let out a tired sigh, his gaze shifting between his daughter and Damon. "You know I’m weak when you beg me like this, sweetie, but this time, I can’t just hand you over to someone else without being sure it’s the right decision."

"But, Dad—"

"I have one condition." Derek cut her off and looked Damon straight in the eyes. "I’ll visit every week, and if anything happens to my daughter, I’ll take her home immediately, no arguments, no excuses."

Damon nodded respectfully. "That’s not an issue. This manor will always be open for you, Mr. and Mrs. Dawson."

Hattie added, "I also want you to allow Charlotte to come home whenever she wants, without any restrictions."

"Of course," Damon replied. "Charlotte is free to visit home whenever she wants, and I would never stop her."

Hattie gave him a last warning. "If you fail her, Damon, I’ll hang your head in my living room."

"Mom, no!" Charlotte’s face turned pale when she heard her mother’s threat.

They had abandoned that barbaric lifestyle for over a century and Charlotte didn’t want her parents to bring that tradition back to life.

"I accept that condition." Damon was no better than her parents. "My head will be yours."

Hattie smiled. "Then, we have a deal."

They were still hesitant to let their daughter stay with her mate, but the blood oath was enough to assure them that Damon would never harm Charlotte.

"Charlotte, from now on, you have to tell me everything if something bad happens to you." Hattie gently stroked her daughter’s hair. "No more hiding."

Charlotte nodded. "I promise, Mom. I’ll never keep anything from you again."

"You can stay here for a while, Mr. Dawson, Mrs. Dawson." Damon said, "I can prepare a room for you."

Damon could see that Charlotte was reluctant to part with her parents too soon.

Therefore, it would be better if she could spend more time with her parents, especially after going through the court.

"Mom, you’re not leaving now, are you?" Charlotte asked.

"Oh, how can I leave when you look at me with such a face?" Hattie chuckled, gently patting Charlotte’s head. "We’ll stay here as long as you need us."

Charlotte felt a warmth in her chest at her mother’s words. It had been so long since she had felt this kind of comfort from her parents, especially after everything that had happened.

"Thank you, Mom," Charlotte whispered, "I really need you both right now."

Hattie smiled softly. "We’re not going anywhere now, sweetheart."

Derek, who had been quietly observing, spoke up. "We’re not going anywhere until we know you’re truly okay."

chapters.title: Chapter 28: Audition Day (1)
chapters.content:
Chapter 28: Audition Day (1)

Charlotte had a really good sleep that night.

She could finally sleep next to her mother after so long, her presence giving her a sense of comfort she had been missing.

As the morning came, she led her parents to the dining room, meeting Damon’s entire family who seemed a bit anxious, especially Carmen.

That was because Charlotte’s parents recognized her as Julian’s maternal grandmother and had met her several times before her wedding.

"Mom, Dad. Carmen is the one who helped me to ruin Julian’s business," Charlotte explained, hoping to ease the tension between them.

Before Hattie or Derek could respond, their attention shifted to Haven, who came running toward them with a plate of cake. Her small face lit up with excitement as she offered it to them.

"Hello! I’m Haven!" she said cheerfully, holding up the plate. "This is for you. My mom says we should always give a welcoming cake to our staying guests."

Hattie’s cold expression softened as she saw the bubbly little girl. She crouched slightly to meet Haven’s gaze. "Well, thank you, sweetheart. That’s very kind of you."

Derek smiled faintly, taking the plate from Haven’s tiny hands. "It’s not every day we get served by such a charming young lady. Thank you, Haven."

Haven offered them a big smile before running back to Vera, who watched the scene with a smile.

Charlotte let out a sigh of relief and silently gave Vera a thumb-up for letting the little diplomat handle the situation.

Because of Haven’s cheerful presence, breakfast turned out to be a pleasant affair.

The tension that had lingered between Charlotte’s parents and Damon’s family began to ease, replaced by lighthearted conversation.

The day seemed to fly by as they enjoyed their time together.

Charlotte wished she could spend more moments with her parents, but with her audition approaching, she needed to set aside some time to practice.

"Are you sure you want us to leave so soon?" asked Hattie to ensure her daughter’s wish.

"I know both of you are busy, so I don’t want to bother you further." Charlotte continued, "Besides, I also have an audition in a few days."

"Audition?" Hattie was surprised and delighted at the same time. "So you decide to come back?"

Charlotte nodded. "Mhm. Damon helped me pick a new movie that suits me."

Hattie smiled warmly. She stroked her daughter’s hair in gentle motion. "He is really something, isn’t he?"

"He is ...," Charlotte’s smile seemed so genuine when she talked about Damon. "I’m glad he wanted to accept me as his mate."

"Ridiculous," her mother scoffed gently, shaking her head with a fond smile. "Every Alpha would kill to have a mate like you, dear. Damon is lucky to have you."

Charlotte responded with a soft laugh. It never crossed her mind that she was a perfect mate for Damon.

She hadn’t even done anything for him.

Sure, she had started rebuilding her life, but Damon had been the one to guide her through it all, offering his unwavering support without expecting anything in return.

That was why the audition felt so important.

She had to nail it, not just for herself, but to show Damon that his belief in her wasn’t a mistake.

"Don’t worry too much about the audition. I know my daughter is an expert in acting." Hattie said warmly, noticing the hesitation on Charlotte’s face. "You just have to believe in yourself."

"I’ll do my best, Mom," Charlotte replied.

"Oh, I almost forgot something." Hattie reached into her purse and pulled out a sleek credit card. "Here, you can use my card for whatever you need."

Charlotte blinked in surprise, holding up her hands. "Mom, that’s really not necessary. I can manage."

"Nonsense." Hattie shoved the card into her daughter’s hands. "Your dad and I have worked like mad dogs for years, and all of our fortunes will be yours after we die."

"Mom, don’t say something like that!" protested Charlotte.

Hattie sighed and said something more casual, "You’re our daughter, Charlotte, and we want to support you. Let us."

Charlotte, "Mom, I appreciate it, really, but I want to prove I can stand on my own two feet."

"And you will," Hattie said firmly. "But even the strongest need a safety net sometimes. This isn’t about dependency, it’s about knowing we’re here for you no matter what."

Charlotte hesitated for a moment before taking the card. "Okay, but I’ll only use it if absolutely necessary."

"Good girl." Hattie smiled and patted Charlotte’s cheek. "Now, give your mom a big hug."

Charlotte chuckled before she hugged her parents tightly. "Thank you for being here, Mom, Dad."

"Always," said them at the same time.

Hattie and Derek then went to the landing pad.

They didn’t rent a helicopter anymore because Damon had offered them to use his private helicopter and he had even told them to contact him anytime they wanted to visit the Crimson Moon manor.

As the day passed, the audition day finally arrived and for the first time, Charlotte had other things on her mind besides her divorce.

She and Damon arrived at the Luminix Studio building separately, as she didn’t want anyone to see her with the CEO before the audition.

Because she hadn’t signed a contract with any agency, Damon had arranged for her to audition independently.

He knew it would be better for her to showcase her talent first before signing an official contract with Luminix Entertainment Group.

"Mrs. Dawson, you’re here," Tatiana Petrova, the show’s director whom she had met before, greeted her.

She had just stepped into the building when she bumped into Charlotte.

"Mrs. Petrova." Charlotte smiled at her, asking, "Will you be in the audition room today?"

"Yes." Tatiana said, "Actually, Mr. Sullivan asked me to become the vice director in the movie you are about to audition."

Charlotte was taken aback. "Then, maybe we should keep a bit of distance? I ... I don’t want anyone to misunderstand me."

Tatiana chuckled. "Oh, Mrs. Dawson. Everyone knows you’re not a rookie."

chapters.title: Chapter 29: Audition Day (2)
chapters.content:
Chapter 29: Audition Day (2)

Even though five years had passed, Charlotte’s appearance still looked as young as before, and that was the advantage of being a werewolf.

They weren’t immortal, but their aging process was three times slower than humans. Therefore, werewolf celebrities tended to have longer careers than ordinary humans.

Unfortunately, Charlotte didn’t have the same spark she used to. Her appearance hadn’t changed, but the way she saw the world had shifted drastically.

The unhealed wound in her heart made it difficult to keep her spirits up, but she would try her best not to drown in her pain anymore.

"I need to go first, Mrs. Dawson." Tatiana patted her shoulder lightly before walking away. "I can’t wait to see your best performance."

Right.

Charlotte reminded herself as she watched Tatiana leave. She had come here to give her best, to rebuild her career from the ground up.

Today, she only needed to focus on herself, so she could stand on equal ground with Damon in the future.

Charlotte then went to the 12th floor, where the audition was being held. After registering her name, she waited in the waiting room with the other candidates.

She deliberately wore a mask to avoid being recognized by others in the room.

However, the real reason was that she simply didn’t want to have a conversation before the audition.

The coordinator called their names one by one.

The time they spent inside varied, some stayed in the room for a long time, while others came out after just two minutes.

Just from that alone, everyone could guess how their performance went.

After waiting for almost two hours, the coordinator finally called her name. "Charlotte Dawson."

"Charlotte what?!" The room erupted into chaos.

Several candidates turned to look at her, their eyes wide with disbelief. Whispers filled the air as they began murmuring to each other.

"Is that Charlotte Dawson?" one of them asked.

"It can’t be! Why would she audition like the rest of us?" another added, craning her neck to get a better look at the masked woman.

"Isn’t her husband’s business in trouble now?"

Charlotte sighed inwardly, silently cursing anyone who brought up Julian’s name.

Charlotte took a deep breath before she walked into the casting room, ignoring the talk about her scum ex-husband.

There were five people inside the room; the producer, the director, the vice director, the book writer, and the CEO.

Charlotte gave Damon a faint smile before she shifted her gaze to the book writer.

The movie "Broken Bond" was based on a best-selling novel written by Nina Montgomery.

Her novel was loved by so many readers for its depiction of the painful journey of Lena Carter as she escaped an abusive relationship and did her best to rebuild her life with her daughter, Olivia.

Charlotte had read the novel numerous times while practicing her script, and she could feel the pain of the main character so deeply.

The book was phenomenal, so she was a bit scared of ruining the readers’ expectations of the movie.

"Good afternoon, Charlotte." The director—Logan Barnes—greeted her.

Damon hadn’t mentioned anything about Charlotte to the movie director, so Logan had been caught off guard when her name appeared on the list.

"Charlotte Dawson," Logan continued, "I wasn’t expecting to see you here today. It’s been a while since we’ve seen you on the screen."

Charlotte offered a polite smile. "Yes, it has. But I’m ready to make a comeback."

Logan lifted his eyebrows, feeling intrigued. "And you chose this role to make your return? Interesting choice."

"Then, let’s see what you’ve got." The director turned the page to the scene Charlotte had chosen to perform. "The stage is yours."

Charlotte took a deep breath, focusing her mind to play the scene in her mind over and over again.

Damon had been expecting Charlotte to choose the scene where the main character’s husband abuses her. Because it would be easy for her to portray that scene with her real experience.

But Charlotte ended up choosing a different scene—the monologue where the main character broke down after a grueling workday at the laundromat.

Once she was ready, she suddenly sat on the floor, imagining herself in the quiet living room of a small apartment.

She pulled her knees to her chest and rested her head on them. Charlotte’s blue eyes lost their spark in a second, looking empty, like an ocean under the dark night sky.

She had only acted for no more than two minutes, but everyone was already holding their breath.

Charlotte let out a tired sigh before speaking her monologue. "Can anyone hear me? God? Ghost? Whatever ... just ... please, hear me."

Her voice cracked, coated in desperation and exhaustion. She chuckled lightly, realizing she was talking to herself like a madman.

But at that moment, she had no one to rely on, except for the ghost in her room.

"I met a horrible customer today," she began, her voice trembling with suppressed frustration. "She accused me of stealing her gold necklace that she claimed was in the pocket of her clothes."

"I didn’t do it, but she kept yelling at me, and no one believed me," Charlotte whispered, "They said a poor single mom like me must love to steal."

"Haha ... what a joke. I don’t even see her necklace" She buried her face on her knees, sobbing softly. "But no one ... no one wanted to believe me. Even the owner fired me without listening to my words."

"Did I make a wrong choice?" Charlotte lifted her head, looking up at the ceiling. "Leaving him? I thought I was doing the right thing for her ... for us. But now ...,"

Her expression shifted from sadness to anger. Charlotte raised her voice when she shouted, "Did I make the wrong choice?! Leaving him? Was it a mistake?!"

Her breathing became labored as her anger boiled over. She clenched her fists tightly. "I thought I was saving us!"

"But look at me now... beaten down, accused, fired, and struggling to keep a roof over our heads." Charlotte ruffled her hair. She yelled out of her lungs. "Fuck! Answer me!"

The director was so captivated by her performance that he almost forgot to deliver his line.

For a moment, the room was filled with an awed silence until he softly spoke, "Mama."

In just one deep breath, Charlotte cast away all her anger and sadness as she heard the imagined voice.

She turned her head toward the door, her expression softening as if she were seeing a little girl standing there, clutching a broken doll close to her chest.

"Oh, sweetheart." Charlotte’s lips curved into a warm, bright smile. "Did you break your doll again? Come here, let me fix it for you."

"Cut!" The director stood up from his chair and clapped so hard that everyone started to do the same. "That was phenomenal!"

The writer, Nina, was also impressed by her extraordinary performance.

She wasn’t the one who made the final decision on choosing the actress, but the director also valued her opinion.

"I’ve seen many performances of Lena since this morning," Nina said, a soft smile appearing on her face. "But this is the first time I’ve seen someone truly bring Lena to life."

chapters.title: Chapter 30: Tears of Joy
chapters.content:
Chapter 30: Tears of Joy

The writer looked at Charlotte in compassionate eyes.

She was on the verge of tears, unable to believe that the character she had imagined for years had finally come to life.

"I might not have the authority to choose the actor," Nina said confidently, "but all I can say is ... I think you would be perfect for my Lena."

Tatiana, the vice director, nodded in agreement. "I don’t have much to say because your performance has exceeded my expectations, Mrs. Dawson."

Charlotte, still immersed in the character’s emotions, tried her best to wipe away the tears from her eyes, but a bright smile had already formed on her lips.

"Thank you," said Charlotte politely.

Their compliments boosted her confidence and silenced the doubts that had been filling her head for the past few days.

Logan clapped again and grinned. "I rarely compliment actors during auditions, but right now my mouth would itch all day if I didn’t say this, you’ve made me want to see this movie right now."

The producer, Diego West, couldn’t help but smile. "We can’t officially accept you yet, but just so you know, you’re definitely our top candidate."

"What do you think, Mr. Sullivan?" Diego asked Damon, who had been silent the entire time.

Damon leaned back in his chair. He rarely smiled at work, but when his eyes landed on Charlotte, the corner of his mouth lifted. "She’s perfect."

As the only one who knew about Damon and Charlotte’s relationship, Tatiana could only sigh and think to herself, "Our boss’s heart has been stolen."

"I think that’s all, Charlotte." Logan smiled. "We’ll contact you later with the results."

Charlotte nodded. Her heart filled with happiness and a sense of relief, but she knew she had to remain calm in front of the others.

Their words had meant a lot to her, but she couldn’t celebrate this moment before she got the results.

"Thank you for your time," Charlotte said politely.

She gave them a smile before leaving the room.

"How’s the audition going?" the staff greeted her as she stepped out the door. She hadn’t asked any of the other candidates that question, but she made an exception for Charlotte.

"It’s great," Charlotte smiled. "Thank you for asking."

She no longer covered her face with a mask, so the other auditioners could be sure that she was the ’Charlotte Dawson’.

People auditioning for the lead role could only sigh, knowing they didn’t stand a chance if Charlotte made a comeback.

But instead of being upset, they were incredibly happy to see Charlotte again after such a long time.

"Charlotte! Are you making a comeback?" a man shouted from across the room.

Suddenly, two women with a pen approached her. "Charlotte, we’ve been becoming your fans since your first appearance on screen. Would you sign our shirts?"

"Charlotte, why did you decide to make a comeback?"

"What about your married life? Didn’t you say you wanted to focus on building your family?"

"Charlotte ..."

People began to surround her, bombarding her with so many questions that Charlotte’s head hurt.

She was glad so many people still recognized her and hoped for her return, but this crowd was definitely too much for someone who had been cooped up in the house for years.

"I appreciate your enthusiasm, but can you please give me some space?" Charlotte tried to politely push them away, but they only pushed harder.

As her back was pressed against the door, it suddenly opened from the inside. Charlotte braced herself for a fall, but instead she bumped into someone’s body.

The familiar scent of cedarwood filled her nose, making her feel safe and comfortable.

"If you don’t step back, security will escort you out," Damon warned them. His voice was commanding and firm.

For some reason, the crowd didn’t have the courage to disobey his words, as if something unpleasant would happen to them if they didn’t comply.

But it was natural for them to feel that way. As an Alpha, his presence alone was enough to make other werewolves freeze, let alone ordinary humans like them.

The crowd stepped back immediately, clearing a path for Charlotte to walk through.

"Wait for me in my office," Damon whispered before she left.

Charlotte just nodded in response, then made her way through the crowd.

When she reached the elevator, Charlotte leaned against the wall and took a deep breath.

She had always been able to handle large crowds, but after staying at home for five years, she felt overwhelmed by having so many people around her.

Now she wondered if she could really be a celebrity again.

No, stop it.

Charlotte immediately tried to push all those negative thoughts out of her mind.

If people didn’t doubt her ability and credibility, why would she do this to herself?

Charlotte straightened her back as the elevator stopped at the top floor. The executive receptionist greeted her immediately, "Good afternoon, Mrs. Dawson."

Charlotte was surprised because she hadn’t expected the receptionist to greet her so quickly, as if she had been waiting for her arrival.

"May I go to Mr. Sullivan’s office?" asked Charlotte hesitantly.

The receptionist—Zoey Adler—smiled. "Of course, Mrs. Dawson. Mr. Sullivan told me to let you in whenever you come here."

Charlotte laughed softly. "Thank you for the information."

Charlotte really wanted to hide from the others as soon as possible, so she immediately went into Damon’s office.

But to her surprise, there was a large bucket of flowers and boxes of chocolates, waiting for her behind the door.

Charlotte froze for a moment, her eyes widening at the sight. She hadn’t expected to see something so ... sweet.

She walked over to the table and picked up a card from under the flowers. The words were written in handwriting, which made it seem more genuine.

For someone who shines brighter than these flowers. Thank you for your hard work. - D

Charlotte’s heart fluttered. She had received many gifts from Julian before, but Damon’s gift felt so different.

The gifts weren’t a symbol of apology, but a genuine gesture of appreciation.

The gifts were so sweet and beautiful that they brought tears to Charlotte’s eyes.

Is this what they call tears of joy? she thought to herself.

chapters.title: Chapter 31: Two News in One Day
chapters.content:
Chapter 31: Two News in One Day

Charlotte caressed the petals between her fingers as the office door opened.

"Do you like your presents?" Damon stood in the doorway with a smile on his face.

Charlotte turned and replied with a smile. "Damon ... these are the sweetest presents I’ve ever received."

No one had ever done that before. Her agency and manager always threw a party when she officially got the part in a movie.

However, the gifts were there before Charlotte even walked into the audition room, which meant that Damon had already prepared the gifts regardless of the results.

"You worked so hard practicing your script." Damon approached her and caressed her cheeks. "These gifts don’t even compare to your efforts."

Charlotte smiled weakly. "What ... what do you think of my performance?"

"Like I said, you’re perfect." Damon assured her, "Every person in that room feels the same way, and I swear I’ve never told them to exaggerate their reactions to you."

Their reactions were genuine, even the writer seemed to like her that much.

"Do you still doubt yourself?" asked Damon.

Charlotte shook her head slowly. "I don’t." She clutched the flowers in her hands. "I don’t doubt myself anymore."

She hadn’t been in front of a camera for five years, but her passion for acting never disappeared.

After all, she was still Charlotte Dawson, the talented actress adored by humans and supernatural beings.

"I really ... really want to believe in myself again, Damon," Charlotte said firmly.

Damon gently ran his fingers through her brunette hair. "And you will," he said softly. "You have already taken the first steps."

• •

About two weeks later, Charlotte received an email from the producer of Broken Bond.

[Dear Charlotte Dawson.

We are pleased to inform you that, after careful consideration, we have decided to cast you in the role of Lena in Broken Bond.]

Charlotte hadn’t read the rest of the message when she jumped out of bed and immediately called Damon.

In just a second he picked up the phone. "Yes?"

"Damon, I did it!" she exclaimed, her voice bubbling with excitement. "I got the role!"

Charlotte could hear a small giggle on the other end. "You got it?" he repeated.

"Yes!" Charlotte nodded eagerly, bouncing on her feet. "The producer just emailed me. I’m going to play Lena in Broken Bond!"

After she managed to control her excitement, she finally realized something. "Oh ... you already knew, didn’t you?"

He was the damn head of Luminix Entertainment Group, so of course they had to tell Damon first before they told her.

"I did," Damon admitted. "But I like hearing the news straight from you."

Her laugh, her smile, and the way she delivered the news with excitement were something that Damon would remember all day.

Because it was rare to hear Charlotte’s joyful laugh every day.

"You’re unbelievable," Charlotte said, "You already know, and yet you let me go on like a lunatic."

"You’re not a lunatic," Damon replied, "You’re just happy, and happiness suits you, Charlotte."

Charlotte was about to reply, but her phone suddenly received another call from her lawyer.

"Damon, Mr. Hale is calling me." Charlotte said, "Maybe it’s about my upcoming hearing. Do you want me to put you on with Mr. Hale?"

Damon nodded. "Yes, I’d like to hear what he has to say."

Charlotte answered the call. "Hello, Mr. Hale. You’re also on the line with Mr. Sullivan."

"Good afternoon, Mrs. Dawson, and Mr. Sullivan," Victor greeted them. "I wanted to inform you of a development regarding the division of your marital assets."

Marital assets, my ass.

Most of those so-called marital assets were probably her hard-earned fortune.

Charlotte clutched her phone tightly but maintained a calm demeanor. "What’s the update, Mr. Hale?" she asked.

Victor sighed on the other end of the line. "Unfortunately, we’ve hit a roadblock. Mr. Warren did not show up for his last court appearance, so we assume he is on the run."

As soon as negative news about his company began circulating, the stock value plummeted dramatically, prompting all of his investors to withdraw their funds.

Moreover, numerous customers demanded compensation upon discovering that the perfume contained harmful ingredients.

Because of this, Julian had accumulated massive debts, and his father seemed unwilling to intervene, likely to protect his reputation and political career.

As for his mother ... Charlotte wasn’t sure what had happened to Priscilla. She hadn’t heard from her in quite some time.

Carmen had mentioned she had taken care of her daughter, but Charlotte had no idea what she meant by that.

With all these issues piling up, it wasn’t surprising that Julian had chosen to flee, taking all the company’s remaining funds with him.

"Since Mr. Warren is on the run, the hearing will likely be delayed," Victor continued. "The court may try to proceed without him, but it’s complicated because it involves your stolen fortune and it’s a criminal case."

If it was just marital property, the court could easily proceed without him, but Charlotte wanted to prove that Julian had stolen all of her premarital assets and property.

Damon asked, "What does that mean for her? Is this case going to drag on any longer?"

Victor paused before answering. "It could, yes. If Mr. Warren doesn’t show up or can’t be found within a certain amount of time, the court may issue a default judgment in Mrs. Dawson’s favor."

"But that doesn’t guarantee that everything will be resolved quickly. If Mr. Warren’s assets are tied up in some illegal activity, that will complicate things further."

Charlotte let out a tired breath. "And if he’s never found?"

"If he’s not found, there’s a chance the case could be closed and the assets returned to you. But that also depends on how the investigation goes." Victor explained. "Right now, it’s better for us to wait."

"We understand, Mr. Hale." Damon said, "Thank you for your hard work."

Victor, "I’ll immediately keep you updated on any developments. Rest assured, we are doing everything we can to track him down."

As she hung up Victor’s call, Charlotte immediately sat down on the bed. "Why is it so hard for me to get away from him? If we can’t track him, he might just come after me one day."

"He can’t hurt you, Charlotte." Damon assured her, "I’ll ask Theodore to arrange some men to protect you while I’m away."

chapters.title: Chapter 32: Fair Share
chapters.content:
Chapter 32: Fair Share

Charlotte took a deep breath before entering the Luminix building. Now that she had officially landed a role in a movie, Damon asked her to sign the contract with Luminix Entertainment Group.

It was fair, to be honest.

Luminix had done so much to help her resurrect her career that they couldn’t possibly want Charlotte to take all the credit.

Charlotte could also repay Damon’s fortune by working with his company.

The last agency Charlotte worked with took about 25% of the profits, so Charlotte assumed Luminix would probably take that much or more.

However, it seemed like she was wrong.

"You want 10% of my profits?" Charlotte’s jaw dropped as she stared at Damon and the company’s legal team sitting across from her.

Damon raised an eyebrow. "Is that too much?"

"Too much?!" Charlotte exclaimed, "This is ridiculously low for everything you’re offering to me!"

He had provided her with numerous resources, including a personal acting coach, free access to skincare products and treatments, and exclusive opportunity to audition for major roles in movies that would be produced by Luminix Studio.

Damon was ensuring she had everything she needed to succeed, yet he was only asking for 10% of her profit? Most actors could only dream of a generous deal.

Charlotte wasn’t new to the industry, so she knew that agencies like Luminix Entertainment Group typically took 25-30% of an actor’s earnings.

The fact that Damon had set her rate so low made it clear that wasn’t a standard arrangement. It was personal, just another way Damon attempted to pamper her.

"This number is too low. Your company will barely make enough profit from me," Charlotte said. "Set it to 25%—no, 30% is actually fine."

"No." Damon said firmly, "I don’t want to set the profit more than 10%."

Charlotte didn’t understand what was going on inside Damon’s head. He wouldn’t get anything if he were too generous to her.

Damon continued, "It might seem too low now, but your earnings will increase in the future so 10% will be enough to cover the expense of your need."

"Yes, maybe my future earnings can cover all of my expenses, but how about your profit?" Charlotte let out her frustration. "You’re running a business. I can’t believe you’re not taking more for yourself."

Damon’s expression softened, and he leaned back in his chair. "Charlotte, this isn’t about profit for me. I don’t need the money from your earnings. What I want is for you to succeed. To build a career that lasts, not just a quick payday."

Charlotte was speechless. She turned her head toward the legal team, but it seemed like they didn’t oppose Damon’s shenanigan.

It meant that Damon had ensured the company would still get profit by spending his own money.

No, no, no way!

Charlotte would never let him go that far just for her.

She wanted to stand equal to him, not becoming his burden.

"Please, leave us alone for a second," said Charlotte to the legal team.

They seemed hesitant at first, but when Damon gave them a nod, they immediately left the room.

"Damon." Charlotte began, "A business is still a business. That’s why I want you to think of me like your other talents. Treat me fairly, not as an exception."

She continued, "I don’t want to be treated differently because I want to earn my place, Damon. I want my success to be mine, not something handed to me on a silver platter."

Charlotte knew Damon meant well, but if he gave her a special treatment, then she wouldn’t see her achievement as something special.

She would see it as a gift from Damon, not because of her own capability.

"Please, don’t make me feel that way," pleaded Charlotte.

Damon could see how much this mattered to her after hearing her plea. After a moment of silence, he sighed deeply and gave a slight nod.

"Alright, Charlotte," he said softly. "I’ll treat you like everyone else. No special treatment. But just know, my intention was never to undermine your efforts or your talent."

Charlotte smiled softly. "I know that you mean well, but if you truly believe in my talent, then you need to trust that I can achieve success, even if you take a fair share of my profit."

Damon’s gaze softened, but his tone remained firm. "Alright. 20%," he said. "No more than that. Is that good enough for you?"

Charlotte nodded. "Yeah. 20% sounds good."

Damon leaned back. "Good, then it’s settled."

After that, he instructed the legal team to revise the contract. Once the adjustments were made, Charlotte reviewed the document carefully before signing it.

"Congratulations, Mrs. Dawson." Damon stood up and extended his hand toward her with a warm smile. "You’re now officially part of Luminix Entertainment Group."

Charlotte chuckled softly as she shook his hand. "Thank you, Mr. Sullivan. I’ll do my best to make you proud."

"You’ve made me proud from the very beginning, Mrs. Dawson," Damon said with a small smile. "I suppose I’m easy to please."

With nothing left to do in Damon’s office, Charlotte made her way to the meeting room for her first official introduction to the producer and the other actors of Broken Bond.

The atmosphere in the room was lively, filled with chatter and laughter.

Diego, the producer, stood at the head of the table. Several actors and crew members were already seated, flipping through scripts or exchanging stories about past projects.

"Ah, Charlotte!" The producer spotted her immediately, waving her over. "Welcome. Come, have a seat."

Charlotte smiled politely as she stepped into the room. It had been five years since she last attended a meeting like this, and the unfamiliar energy made her stomach flutter.

Diego, "Let me introduce you to your daughter."

He gestured to a petite young actress standing nearby.

The girl, who couldn’t have been older than 10, had big, curious blue eyes and long-wavy brunette hair.

Charlotte wasn’t sure where the producer had found her, but at some point, she noticed that the little girl bore a slight resemblance to her.

Even the way she smiled was incredibly similar to Charlotte.

The little girl stepped forward, flashing a bright smile at Charlotte. "Hi, I’m Lily Bennett! I’ll be playing your daughter, Olivia."

Charlotte couldn’t help but stroke her fluffy hair gently and said softly, "Nice to meet you, Lily."

chapters.title: Chapter 33: First Rehearsal
chapters.content:
Chapter 33: First Rehearsal

"It’s been a while since I played in a movie." Charlotte gave a brief speech. "I hope you can all guide me and that we can work together to create this amazing movie."

The director, Logan, smiled at her. "Well said, Charlotte. We’re thrilled to have you on board. Now, let me introduce you to everyone."

After meeting a few supporting actors, Logan gestured toward a man seated at the far end of the table. He was tall and rugged, with sharp features and an intense gaze that seemed to pierce right through her.

"This is Ethan Reed," Logan said. "He’ll be playing Alan, Lena’s abusive husband."

Ethan stood. "It’s nice to meet you, Charlotte," he said, offering his hand.

Ethan’s grip was firm, almost too firm, as if it mirrored the character he was about to portray.

"Nice to meet you too, Ethan," she said.

Even though he seemed like a harsh person, the man was actually so sweet and kind.

He even reminded Charlotte to tell him if he accidentally made her uncomfortable, especially when he acted as Alan.

"Don’t worry about it. I know you’re just acting." Charlotte said, "If I feel uncomfortable when you’re acting, then it means you’re a great actor, Ethan."

Ethan smiled. "I’ll do my best to make you uncomfortable then."

Charlotte laughed lightly at Ethan’s playful response, the tension easing a bit between them. "Fair enough. Just don’t overdo it, or I might actually throw something at you during filming."

The trauma of being abused by Julian still lingered in Charlotte’s heart, but as long as she reminded herself it was just acting, she believed she could handle it.

Still, the thought of facing such scenes on set made her chest tighten slightly.

They were just reading scripts now, but what about when they were on set? The movie was set in modern times, so they would be filming in practical settings like real houses and other everyday locations.

Charlotte wondered how much more intense the scenes would feel in that kind of environment?

At that time, would she be able to separate the performance from her own memories?

"Charlotte." The sound of a little girl’s voice woke her up from her reverie. "Do you want to help me rehearse this line?"

Charlotte blinked, refocusing on the present. Lily stood beside her, holding out a script with a shy expression.

"Of course, sweetheart," Charlotte said, smiling warmly. "Which part are we working on?"

"This one." Lily pointed to a line where her character confronted her abusive father when he abused her mother. "I think it’s supposed to sound brave, but I’m not sure how to say it."

Charlotte crouched down to meet Lily at eye level. "Let’s try it together, okay? You can say it however feels right to you, and I’ll guide you if needed."

Lily nodded enthusiastically. "Okay!"

As they began rehearsing, Charlotte felt a small sense of peace.

Though she still had to confront her fear of portraying scenes with an abusive character, at least, she could finally have a kid in the movie, something that Charlotte couldn’t achieve in her life.

However, sometimes she felt relieved she hadn’t had children with Julian. The thought of raising a child in such a toxic environment made her stomach turn.

She couldn’t imagine letting an innocent kid be manipulated and controlled by Julian.

Even though it meant she couldn’t experience having her own children, it was better than having them and watching them suffer.

Charlotte shook her head, pushing the dark thoughts aside. She focused on the script in her hand and the little girl sitting beside her.

"Let’s try this scene again," she said gently to her young co-star, who eagerly nodded and began reciting her lines with enthusiasm.

Other actors and crew members who witnessed their interaction were touched. They already looked like a mother and daughter, and if they kept this dynamic, the director believed the audience would be in tears by the time the movie ended.

As the rehearsal continued, Charlotte found herself becoming more confident. For the first time in a long while, she started to believe that she could truly shine again.

About three hours later, the producer had finally wrapped the rehearsal up. Charlotte let out a sigh of relief, knowing that her performance wasn’t that bad for the first rehearsal.

"Alright, listen up everyone." The producer asked for their attention. "We’re ready to launch the official actor reveal to the public. The marketing division will handle the campaign, but before they release it, I want everyone to be prepared for the public’s reaction."

"If something goes wrong, we’ll try our best to handle the situation." Diego emphasized his words. "However, as you know, we can’t always control the public’s reaction, so please, don’t do something stupid like commenting on their statements."

Charlotte sat quietly, a slight nervousness creeping up on her. She knew the official public reveal of the actors would be a significant moment, especially for her.

She wasn’t sure how the public would react once they found out she was making her comeback.

What about Julian’s situation?

His bad reputation had tainted everything, and Charlotte feared that people would associate her with him.

Would they talk negatively about her too? After all, the public knew him as her husband, not the other way around.

Would they think Charlotte deliberately made a comeback to clear Julian’s name?

"And Charlotte." She immediately lifted her head as the producer called her name. "Mr. Sullivan wants to talk to you after this, so you can’t leave yet."

What kind of thing did Damon want to talk to her about?

Why hadn’t he mentioned it earlier when they were in his office? Oh. maybe he wanted to discuss it with the producer and director as well.

For some reason, Charlotte suspected their conversation would be related to her current situation with Julian.

Charlotte nodded. "Okay."

Charlotte remained seated as the others left the room. She checked her phone but didn’t see any new messages from Damon.

She sighed. He must be planning something behind her back.

Damon was a man full of surprise after all.

chapters.title: Chapter 34: Public Sentiment
chapters.content:
Chapter 34: Public Sentiment

"I’m sorry to keep you waiting." The door behind Charlotte suddenly opened.

Damon entered the meeting room, brushing his hair back as he walked in with Victor. His expression was cold and nonchalant, as usual.

However, the moment Charlotte turned her head toward him, his demeanor shifted, and he offered her a warm smile.

The sudden change startled everyone in the room, except Charlotte.

"Don’t worry about it, Mr. Sullivan." Diego stood up to greet him. "I know how hard it must be for you to make time in your busy schedule."

Charlotte tilted her head in confusion. Diego’s words implicitly suggested that he had been the one to ask Damon for this meeting.

But about what?

The uncertainty somehow made Charlotte feel uneasy.

Victor and Damon then sat across Charlotte. The atmosphere was too heavy, so she couldn’t help but ask, "What’s this about?"

Diego leaned forward. "Charlotte, you have no idea how thrilled I am to have chosen you as the main actress for my project," he began, "But we need to address an issue, your reputation in the media isn’t in the best place right now."

Charlotte knew they would eventually bring this up. She sighed. "This is about my ex-husband, isn’t it?"

Diego and the others looked at her in surprise. They hadn’t known she was divorced. As far as they were aware, her marriage was simply in trouble, but they hadn’t realized it had already ended.

But it was only natural for them to think that way since Charlotte hadn’t publicly addressed the matter.

She suspected that if Damon hadn’t assured them the issue wouldn’t affect her future career, they might not have taken the risk of choosing her.

Her bad reputation could harm the film’s success. If the controversy continued to circulate in public, the movie might suffer a downfall as audiences might avoid seeing Charlotte’s face on the big screen.

Victor stepped in to address the situation. "Yes, Mrs. Dawson, this is about Mr. Warren’s tarnished image in the media," he said, glancing at the others in the room. He then emphasized firmly, "To be clear, Mrs. Dawson has no involvement whatsoever in her ex-husband’s current scandal."

Victor gave a brief but clear explanation of Charlotte’s past with Julian to others. As the details unfolded, everyone was in shock, with jaws dropping and eyes widening in disbelief.

"Dear lord, Charlotte, I’m so sorry. I had no idea," Diego said, his voice filled with genuine regret.

Charlotte offered a faint smile. "It’s alright, Mr. West. I haven’t exactly been open about it. It’s in the past now, and I’m trying to move forward."

Diego glanced around at the others, his expression still stunned. "But with the movie’s theme mirroring ... that part of your life, I can’t imagine how hard it must be for you to take this on."

Charlotte took a deep breath. "Yes, it’s not easy. But the important part about acting is telling stories, even the hard ones. Besides, I think this role might help me turn my pain into something meaningful."

"Honestly, I was hesitant to cast you in this role," Logan admitted. "But after learning the truth, I became even more convinced that you’re the perfect choice to play Lena in this film."

Damon, who had remained silent until now, finally spoke. "Like you, Mr. Barnes, I believe many people will support her once they know the truth." He turned his gaze directly to Charlotte. "That’s why I want to hold a press conference to clear your name and distance you from your ex-husband’s scandals."

Victor nodded. "We can also use that chance to promote her comeback movie." He asked, "What do you think, Mrs. Dawson?"

Charlotte nervously pinched her fingers, her nails pressing into her skin as anxiety bubbled within her. The thought of facing a room full of reporters and journalists felt overwhelming.

She didn’t feel ready to confront so many people so soon, but it seemed like she had no other choice.

The fate of the movie was now in her hands. If her reputation continued to deteriorate, the producers might decide to cut her from the project to protect public sentiment.

Especially since Julian was the public enemy number one right now.

"What do you think, Charlotte?" Damon repeated Victor’s question. "Do you want to do that?"

Charlotte nodded immediately. "I want to."

"That’s settled, then." Victor suggested, "Since the movie hasn’t entered production yet, I recommend holding the press conference as soon as possible."

"Let’s do it tomorrow," Charlotte said suddenly.

Her words shocked everyone, especially Damon. "Don’t you think it’s too fast? You might need time to prepare yourself."

"No," Charlotte replied. "Like Mr. Hale said, the faster we solve this problem, the better." She continued, "Besides, if the public’s reaction isn’t good toward me, then you can cut me from this project, so it won’t affect the success of the film."

Charlotte could have been selfish and fought to keep her position. However, when she remembered the hard work of little Lily, she couldn’t bring herself to put that little girl in the middle of a chaotic situation.

Just by acting alongside her for a short time, Charlotte could already tell that Lily was a talented actress. That was why Charlotte wanted Lily to receive recognition from a lot of people.

If The Broken Bond received too much criticism, then all their hard work would go to waste, and Charlotte didn’t want that to happen.

Damon could see that Charlotte had made up her mind, so he didn’t want to argue.

"If you think that’s the best way, then I’ll support it." Damon said to Victor. "Please, prepare for the press conference, Mr. Hale."

Victor nodded. "I’ll do my best, Mr. Sullivan, Mrs. Dawson."

As the meeting ended, Charlotte left the room with Damon. The hallways seemed quieter than usual as they walked together, their footsteps echoing in the empty corridor.

"Are you sure about this?" Damon asked, his voice softer now. As the CEO of the company, he wanted Charlotte to hold the press conference immediately, but as her mate, he didn’t want her to suffer.

Charlotte took a deep breath and nodded. "I have to do this. I can’t let my past ruin the future we’re trying to build here."

Damon smiled faintly, giving her a reassuring pat on the back. "I know people can’t hate you, Charlotte."

She looked up at him. "I hope so."

chapters.title: Chapter 35: Press Conference (1)
chapters.content:
Chapter 35: Press Conference (1)

Charlotte took a few deep breaths and clasped her hands, standing in front of the conference room doors, waiting for the staff’s signal to let her in.

The staff had given her a script to help guide her during the press conference, so she wouldn’t get confused while addressing the reporters. However, she knew better than anyone that the script would be useless once the reporters started to ask too many questions.

This wasn’t her first press conference, yet she couldn’t help but feel anxious every time she imagined her name was being called by those reporters.

The last time she stepped into a press conference was to announce that she was leaving the showbiz industry to focus on building a family with Julian.

But now, she was here to declare that she had cut all ties with him.

Wasn’t that a bit ironic?

Back then, she had sat before them with a bright smile, but now, her face was clouded with gloom, and her eyes were filled with regret.

Both of them were Charlotte, but felt like two different sides of the same coin.

"Charlotte."

She jolted in surprise as she suddenly heard that deep, familiar voice. She turned around, looking at Damon who was walking to her. "I can reschedule this press if you’re not ready yet."

Charlotte immediately shook her head. "No, you don’t need to do that," she said firmly. "Besides, they’d think I’m an asshole if I canceled the press conference at the last minute."

Charlotte didn’t want her reputation to deteriorate further.

Charlotte couldn’t afford for her reputation to worsen any further.

"If you’re sure about this," Damon said, placing his hands gently on her shoulders, "then remember, I’ll be right there by your side in the room. You won’t face them alone, Charlotte."

He added, "No matter how things turn out, know that I’ll continue to support you and do everything I can to help you find the best path forward."

Charlotte was momentarily stunned by his words. She lifted her head, her gaze meeting Damon’s gray eyes.

His eyes were so tender and kind, filled with a warmth that made Charlotte wonder, would he go this far for someone else, or was it just for her?

Not only had he supported her by giving her so much money and resources, he had always given her moral support.

This man was like a diamond in the middle of the dirt.

Charlotte even wondered how she could choose to marry Julian instead of him in the first place?

"The reporters will focus on you too if you sit next to me," Charlotte sighed. "Many speculations about us might spread. Who knows what kind of stories they’ll publish to the world."

Damon chuckled softly, leaning slightly closer to her. "Let them speculate. People will always create their own stories, no matter what we do. The truth is what matters."

Charlotte hesitated for a moment, biting her lower lip. "But those rumors might harm your image too, Damon. You’ve been helping me so much already, I don’t want to drag you down with me."

"Image? There is no one in my family who has a good image. Well, maybe Louis has that, but that’s not the important thing." Damon emphasized, his voice firm. "My image is less important than yours, so you don’t have to worry about me."

Charlotte looked at him, her heart fluttering at his words. It was rare for anyone to put her above their own reputation, especially in such a public situation.

"You don’t have to say that," Charlotte murmured, "I can handle this. It’s just ... I don’t want to drag you into this mess."

Damon reached over, gently caressing her hair. "You’re not dragging me into anything. I’m here because I want to be."

Charlotte was about to say something, but the staff suddenly called her. "Mrs. Dawson, they’re ready to see you."

Damon held her hand. "Let’s go now."

As Charlotte stepped into the conference room, dozens of camera flashlights hit her, blinding her for a moment.

She took a deep breath, trying to steady her nerves as she walked further into the room, her heels clicking against the polished floor.

All eyes turned to her, like a pack of lions eyeing a rabbit, but the little rabbit wasn’t alone.

She walked beside a wolf, the fierce wolf capable of tearing them apart if they tried to harm the rabbit.

Charlotte took her seat at the front of the room, with Damon to her right and Victor to her left.

A reporter suddenly asked, followed by others. "Charlotte, do you really want to make a comeback?"

"What about your husband?"

"Your husband is on the run now. Do you know where he is?"

"Is it true you came back to pay off your husband’s debts?"

The microphones in front of her suddenly felt too close, too much. She swallowed hard, trying to push back the wave of anxiety in her heart.

As the room grew increasingly chaotic, Victor tapped the microphone with his finger and said, "Please remain calm. We’ll have a Q&A session later, so you need to be quiet for now, or you’ll have to leave the room."

Charlotte mouthed a silent "Thank you" to Victor.

"You can make a statement whenever you’re ready, Mrs. Dawson," Victor whispered to her.

She nodded. "Okay, I’m ready now."

Charlotte brought her face closer to the microphone before speaking, "Good morning, ladies and gentlemen." Her voice trembled with nervousness, and her hands suddenly became cold and sweaty.

She looked at the crowd before her, and a sense of dread enveloped her heart like a dark cloud in the sky.

Now she realized, maybe she wasn’t ready for this yet.

However, she couldn’t back down now, or her whole reputation would be thrown to the cliff.

Charlotte clenched the paper in her hands, seeking some comfort, but it didn’t work. She had to bite the inside of her lip to keep from showing how out of breath she felt.

As her legs and hands shook, Charlotte suddenly felt someone hold her hand under the table. His large hand wrapped around hers, gently caressing her palm with his thumb, as if reminding Charlotte that she wasn’t alone.

Damon was with her.

chapters.title: Chapter 36: Press Conference (2)
chapters.content:
Chapter 36: Press Conference (2)

Charlotte closed her eyes for a moment and took a deep breath before speaking again. This time, her voice was more confident and firm.

"It’s been so long since I last saw all of you in a conference room. That’s why, please forgive me if I seem a bit nervous."

"I know many of you have a lot of questions about me and my husband, Julian Warren. However, before we move on to the Q&A session, I ask for your patience and hope you will listen to my statement first."

Charlotte gripped Damon’s hand tightly as she began.

"As of March 17, 2024, I, Charlotte Dawson, have legally divorced Julian Warren. I no longer have any association with him—whether personally or in business."

The room immediately erupted into chaos. Reporters hurriedly typed out her statement, while the cameramen snapped countless photos of her face.

Charlotte’s body tensed as their reaction made her even more nervous. What would they say?

Most of them would likely respond negatively to her statement, as it might seem like she was trying to save her own reputation from Julian’s downfall.

But she had to keep going. For her future and for herself.

Taking another deep breath, she straightened her back and continued, "I understand that many of you might think I’m trying to distance myself from him now that his reputation is in shambles."

"However, that’s not the case. Our marriage has been over for a while, long before his scandals came to light. I chose to stay silent because I didn’t want my personal life to become a public spectacle."

Some of the reporters nodded, while others exchanged glances, still skeptical.

"I have nothing to do with Julian Warren anymore, and I am not responsible for his actions. But I am here today because I refuse to let his mistakes define my career and my future."

The room went silent for a moment. Charlotte could feel Damon’s reassuring presence beside her, his fingers lightly brushing against the back of her hand under the table. It was a small gesture, but it was enough to calm her down.

Then, a reporter in the front row raised his hand. "Mrs. Dawson, are you saying that you had no idea about your ex-husband’s illegal activities?"

Victor wanted to tell the reporter that it wasn’t time for questions yet, but Charlotte lightly patted his arm. "It’s okay," she whispered.

"Yes, I had no knowledge of my ex-husband’s illegal activities," Charlotte continued. "I know many of you might think I’m just making excuses, but this is the truth, Julian always forbade me from interfering in his business affairs."

That was really the truth. Julian had never allowed Charlotte to be involved in his company.

Even when she accidentally saw his financial reports, he would lash out at her, saying it was disrespectful to pry into his matters.

He did that because he didn’t want her to know how much profit he was making or for anyone to find out that he was using cheap materials to increase his margins.

He was truly nothing but a fraud.

"Charlotte, what was the reason for your divorce from Julian Warren?" a reporter asked.

Victor had run out of patience. Since Charlotte seemed willing to answer, he simply said, "Since Mrs. Dawson has no objections to answering questions, we will now open for the Q&A session."

Charlotte could sense Victor’s frustration, but what could he do? Her fame had once been too great, and the reporters wouldn’t stop digging into her life until they had uncovered everything.

"The reason for our divorce is simple," Charlotte said. "He was a jerk husband. A fucking deadbeat who stole my fortune and slept with countless women in every city."

"To all the women out there, take this as a warning—this man is a menace to society. Trust me, you’re better off running the other way than ending up with regrets ... or worse, an STD."

Everyone gasped in unison. It was the first time they had ever heard the famous diva curse so bluntly.

But more importantly—there was actually a man who had dared to hurt their beloved diva!

Oh, the audacity.

"That bastard put me through hell during our marriage," Charlotte continued, her voice steady and firm. "He abused me—mentally and physically—every single day. So, it would be foolish to think that I’d ever help him escape his downfall."

She leaned closer to the microphone, her gaze sharp.

"Because out of everyone in the world, I am the one who wants to see him fall the most."

Her words would undoubtedly become the next day’s headline—no, scratch that.

That statement would flood news portals in less than an hour!

However, while many supported Charlotte’s decision to end her relationship with that bastard, some remained skeptical.

"Charlotte, you were married to him for five years. If he was really abusing you, why didn’t you leave sooner?"

"Yes, why wait five years.

Those questions.

Charlotte knew people would ask something like that. She had heard it before. Yet, no matter how many times she did, it still hurt.

It felt like they were calling her a fool over and over again.

Just as she was about to respond, Damon, who had been silent until now, suddenly spoke up.

"Let me ask you something. If you were stuck in a terrible job—your boss was bullying you, your salary was barely enough to survive, but that job was the only thing keeping you afloat—and then one day, your boss threatened you ..."

Damon’s voice was calm yet sharp, his piercing gaze sweeping across the room.

"... They tell you that if you quit, they’ll make sure you never find another job. They’ll ruin your reputation, blacklist you, and destroy everything you’ve worked for. Would you still walk away so easily?"

The room fell silent.

Charlotte clenched her fists under the table. She knew exactly what Damon was doing—turning their own logic against them.

"That’s what abusers do," he continued. "They don’t just hurt you physically or emotionally. They make you believe you have no way out. They strip you of your power, your confidence, and your identity, until you feel like you have nowhere else to go."

chapters.title: Chapter 37: Press Conference (3)
chapters.content:
Chapter 37: Press Conference (3)

Damon leaned back slightly, his eyes locking onto the reporters who had asked the question. "So, tell me. Do you still think it’s that simple?"

A tense, suffocating silence spread across the room. Some reporters shifted uncomfortably in their seats, while others quickly scribbled down notes.

Charlotte took a deep breath, her chest tightening with emotions she had buried for too long.

Then, she finally spoke. "I stayed because I was scared. Because I thought no one would believe me. Because every time I tried to leave, Julian found a way to drag me back." Her voice wavered slightly but remained firm. "And that’s exactly why I’m speaking out now. So no one else has to go through what I did."

Her voice was strong and unwavering, as if she were speaking not just for herself but for every woman who had endured the same pain.

The cameras kept flashing, pens moved swiftly across notepads, but no one dared to interrupt her now.

Charlotte took a deep breath, steadying herself as she met the gaze of the reporters before her.

"I know some of you still doubt my words," she continued, "I know there are people out there who will say I stayed too long, that I should have left earlier. But unless you’ve been in my shoes, you will never understand the fear, the manipulation, the way abuse chains you to a life you never wanted."

The room was silent. All of their eyes were focused on her.

"I stayed because I was scared. I stayed because I was told no one would believe me. I stayed because every time I thought about leaving, I was reminded that Julian Warren was a powerful man who could destroy me with a single word." She swallowed hard. "And for a while, I believed him."

"But not anymore," Charlotte declared. "I’m done being scared. I refuse to let my past define me, and I refuse to let him control my future."

A murmur rippled through the crowd—some in admiration, others still skeptical. A reporter finally found her voice.

"Charlotte, what do you hope to achieve by coming forward now?"

She met her gaze without hesitation.

"The truth," she said simply. "And justice."

"Not just for me, but for a lot of women and men who endured the same pain as me." Her eyes locked onto the camera. "I know it’s hard to move forward when you feel like you have nothing left. When the world convinces you that you’re weak, that you’re alone."

She took a deep breath. "But you’re not alone. And you are so much stronger than you think."

At this point, no reporters dared to think that she was just lying.

Her voice, her eyes, and her gesture were genuine.

She truly looked like a woman who had just risen from her downfall, like a phoenix rising from the ashes to start a new life.

"I’m standing here today, not just to clear my name, but to remind every woman out there who has suffered in silence."

She said, "You are not defined by the pain someone else inflicted on you. You deserve freedom. You deserve happiness. You deserve to take your life back."

The conference room was silent for a moment, then a ripple of murmurs spread across the crowd.

Some reporters exchanged glances, others hastily jotted down her words, realizing that this wasn’t just a press conference anymore.

It was a movement.

Damon, sitting beside her, didn’t say a word, but the way his fingers subtly brushed against her back reassured her.

He was there. He was listening.

A reporter in the front row raised her hand. "Charlotte, do you plan on taking legal action against Julian Warren?"

Charlotte’s jaw tightened, and for the first time since stepping into the room, a small smile appeared on her lips.

"Oh, I already have," she said firmly, emphasizing each word. "I’ll make sure he’s left with nothing but a mountain of debt."

Another reporter raised his hand. "Is it true that you’re making a comeback as an actress?"

"Yes," Charlotte admitted confidently. "The reason I stopped acting before was because Julian forced me to. But now that he no longer has control over me, I’m choosing to step back into the spotlight."

The reporters felt like they had hit the jackpot that day.

Charlotte’s confession was bigger than they had expected—first, the divorce, then the abuse, and now, her grand return to the screen.

All the reporters who missed this conference would surely cry out blood later.

"What kind of project will you be working on?" another reporter asked.

Charlotte straightened her posture. "A movie titled The Broken Bond," she announced. "It tells the story of a woman who, after years of suffering in a toxic marriage, decides to rebuild her life from scratch."

For a moment, silence filled the room. Then, the cameras started flashing even faster.

"Is this film based on your own experiences?"

Charlotte’s lips curled slightly. "While the story isn’t directly about me, I can relate to the character on a deep level.

"That’s why I knew I had to take on this role. If my story can inspire even one person to walk away from their suffering and choose themselves, then my return to acting will be worth it."

The reporters fired more questions, but this time, Charlotte wasn’t nervous. This time, she was ready.

The press conference lasted only half an hour, but in that short time, Charlotte had done what once seemed impossible, she cleared her name and shifted the public’s anger toward Julian.

It wouldn’t be long before social media exploded with support for her. The tide was turning, and for the first time in years, Charlotte felt like she could finally breathe.

As she stepped out of the room, Charlotte finally felt her entire body relax. She let out a deep breath of relief before crouching down and covering her face with her hands.

"Charlotte, what’s wrong?" Damon patted her shoulder. His voice sounded concern. "Are you feeling unwell?"

"No." Charlotte murmured behind her hands before lifting her head, a bright smile spreading across her face. "I’m just ... really happy."

chapters.title: Chapter 38: Fun Comments
chapters.content:
Chapter 38: Fun Comments

Just as many had predicted, within a mere two hours, Charlotte’s name was soaring across the internet.

News portals. Social media. TV broadcasts.

From everyday people to celebrities, everyone couldn’t stop talking about her.

Many were thrilled at the thought of seeing her on the big screen again, while others were moved to tears upon learning what she had endured over the past five years.

[ Pink_butterfly: That ugly-duckling Julian really has the audacity to abuse our beautiful goddess!]

[ Yoyoyoyo: I don’t even understand how Charlotte was attracted to him in the first place.]

[ Babygee48: His face looks like a fucking horse.]

[ Lenitanita: Hey, don’t insult a horse like that! Even a horse wouldn’t have sex with that many females in a year!]

Charlotte smiled faintly as she scrolled through social media. Occasionally, she even laughed at the jokes people made about Julian.

Her fingers paused mid-scroll when she noticed several comments about Damon.

[ Babygee48: Did anyone else notice the handsome man beside her? Who is he?]

[ Greenlady: You don’t know him? He’s the famous CEO of Luminix Entertainment! I was actually kind of surprised to see him at an actress’s press conference.]

[ Bonita555: That’s him?? I thought the CEO was an old man with a big belly!!!]

[ Yoorozu: Wait... isn’t he Julian’s uncle? Does he also despise his own nephew?]

[ CC_Uno: Of course he has to despise his nephew! If he defends that bastard, then his handsome face card will be such a waste!]

[ RedLady: I hope he’s not a jerk. I’m not sure, but they look like they could be a good couple.]

Charlotte’s smile widened as she read the comments, amusement sparkling in her eyes. It was interesting to see how people’s curiosity about Damon was growing, especially with his unexpected public support of her.

Everyone seemed to like him and wouldn’t mind if he became Charlotte’s next husband.

However, Charlotte only chuckled at the idea. Marriage was the last thing on her mind right now.

Still, she couldn’t deny that Damon’s presence had made everything easier. He had been her reason to stay calm in the entire conference. If he weren’t there, she wasn’t sure she would have made it through without breaking down.

Her fingers hovered over the screen as more comments flooded in.

[SweetChoco: So... are they dating or what? ]

[ MysticRose: If Charlotte ends up with him, I’ll believe in love again *cry emoji*]

The comments were endless, and the conversation had swiftly shifted from dragging Julian through the mud to obsessing over Damon’s ridiculously good looks—and, of course, his relationship with Charlotte.

People even went as far as comparing Damon and Julian’s pictures side by side, ultimately concluding that Julian was the failed product of the Sullivan bloodline.

Good thing he didn’t share their last name.

To be fair, Julian wasn’t exactly ugly , but when his face was placed next to Damon’s, he might as well have been a blurry, pixelated image next to a high-definition masterpiece.

Charlotte groaned softly, rubbing her temples. How in the world had she ever fallen for Julian back then?

As she continued scrolling, the comments took a strange turn.

[ Madame_redd: I’ve heard a rumor that the Sullivan family are werewolves. Is that actually true?]

[ Yoyoyoyo: Are you from the 18th century or something? Of course, werewolves aren’t real!]

Even though supernatural beings had their own organization, their identities remained a closely guarded secret from the public.

The only people who truly understood the existence of the Nocturnal Order were politicians and other influential figures, while the rest of society simply assumed that those who looked impossibly young for their age were just blessed with exceptional genetics.

And honestly, it was better that way.

Most people would probably lose their minds if they knew that werewolves or vampires were casually walking among them.

"Auntie," Haven’s small voice pulled Charlotte from her thoughts. The little girl had been sitting beside her the whole time, watching her giggle and smile at her phone screen. "What are you reading?"

"Oh, just a funny story." Charlotte finally put her phone down, not wanting to explain the jokes she had been laughing at. "Where’s your mommy?"

Haven shrugged. "She said she had something important to discuss with Daddy in their room, and my head will hurt if I listen to their conversation."

Charlotte raised an eyebrow. "How long have they been talking?"

Haven tilted her head in thought before answering innocently, "I’m not sure... maybe around two hours? I tried to open the door, but it’s locked."

Charlotte let out a quiet sigh, already guessing what kind of conversation they were having.

Yeah... they’re definitely not just talking.

"Well, that’s okay." Charlotte reached out and gently stroked Haven’s hair. "You can keep Auntie company for now. I don’t have anyone to talk to either."

Because of her press conference, many parties immediately contacted Luminix. They questioned the validity of Charlotte’s statements and sent invitations for her to appear on their TV shows.

Charlotte didn’t have an official manager yet, so no one was handling her schedule. Fortunately, Damon reassured her that she didn’t have to worry—he would take care of everything.

Aside from that, Damon also had to manage social control to ensure that public discourse didn’t shift in a negative direction, especially regarding her divorce.

Charlotte had already prepared herself for any possibility. When a statement was released to the public, it was like throwing a burning ball into a crowd. Some people would try to put out the fire, but others would only fan the flames.

She couldn’t expect everyone to believe her story or respond with kindness.

However, Damon was determined not to let any negative news about her spread on media portals or social networks.

Handling a public backlash would be a nightmare, but fortunately, the majority of people stood by her and offered their support.

Besides, Charlotte thought, no sane person would hear about her situation and still choose to defend a man like Julian.

"Are you really going to act in a movie again?" Haven asked, her eyes shining with excitement. She had watched many of Charlotte’s films over and over again.

Charlotte nodded, moving closer to the little girl and gently caressing her hair. "Yes. If you want, you can visit me on set anytime. There’s a little girl named Lily there, and I think you two would get along well."

chapters.title: Chapter 39: Don’t Judge A Book By Its Cover
chapters.content:
Chapter 39: Don’t Judge A Book By Its Cover

Haven’s eyes gleamed with excitement. "Really?" She had completely forgotten about her parents, who had abandoned her for some ’private’ discussion. "Do they like cookies? Maybe I can bake some for them!"

"They would definitely love to try your cookies," Charlotte said with a warm smile.

To spend the evening together, Charlotte decided to watch a children’s movie. The house was unusually quiet since half of its residents were out.

Carmen and Mona had gone to see an opera together, while Damon was still stuck in his office—who knew for how long?

But the silence was suddenly broken by the sound of a motorcycle approaching from a distance. The low rumble grew louder as it neared the front door.

A motorcycle? Charlotte didn’t recall ever seeing one at the manor.

Damon and Louis always traveled by car.

Just as she was deep in thought, Haven suddenly jumped off the sofa and ran toward the door. "Aunt Diana is here!"

Diana?

Charlotte had heard that name somewhere before.

Oh, wasn’t she Priscilla’s younger sister?

She hadn’t met her since moving here because Diana didn’t live at Crimson Moon Manor. From what Charlotte had heard, she was always traveling and only returned to the manor a few times a year.

But out of 365 days, why did it have to be today?

It wasn’t like Charlotte had any objections to her coming back to her own home, but why did she have to show up when Damon wasn’t around, Carmen and Mona were out watching opera, and Louis was... well, who knew what kind of filthy things he was doing in his bedroom with his beloved wife right now?

The only person who truly knew Diana was Haven—but how could Charlotte rely on a little girl?

Haven bounced excitedly in front of the door, eagerly awaiting the arrival of the aunt she rarely got to see.

Charlotte eventually made her way to the entrance as well, realizing it would be rude not to welcome a guest.

Maybe Diana was different from Priscilla, right?

Maybe she was better.

But the moment Charlotte laid eyes on her, she nearly had a heart attack.

Diana was indeed different from Priscilla—just not in the way Charlotte had expected.

While Priscilla always dressed in elegant, high-end outfits like a sophisticated socialite, Diana exuded a completely different energy. She wore a black leather jacket over matching leather pants, her style leaning heavily toward gothic.

A tattoo of what looked like a wolf adorned her neck, adding to her rebellious aura.

If Charlotte were being honest, Diana looked undeniably cool. Her fashion choices might not be considered "normal" by most people, but they were certainly a breath of fresh air.

"Auntie Diana!" Haven waved excitedly.

Diana removed her helmet, turning her head toward Haven. Her expression was cold and aloof, making Charlotte instinctively want to retreat behind the door. Diana looked intimidating—far more dangerous than her sister.

On top of that, Charlotte wasn’t sure if Diana would accept her presence or if she had come to Crimson Moon Manor to defend her sister instead.

However, after a moment of tense silence, Diana’s expression suddenly softened. A bright smile spread across her face as she waved back at Haven and ran toward her.

"Oh, my lovely niece, I missed you so much!"

She scooped Haven up in a tight hug, planting several kisses on her round cheeks.

"How long has it been since we last saw each other?"

Haven lifted her small fingers, showing the number five. "Five months! We’ve been apart for five months! Auntie, didn’t you promise to visit in three?"
