She had actually been a little worried when she bought a new bikini after five years. Back when Julian had held her life in his grip, he would sneer and say that any woman who wore a bikini was nothing but a slut begging for men’s attention. It was cruel and completely untrue.

Charlotte had only wanted to wear one because the design was cute, and it made her feel good about herself. Why did Julian always act as if the entire world existed just to please men?

But that was the past, and there was no point wasting her thoughts on that bastard now, not when she was sitting beside Damon, a man who was handsome, kind, and loved her exactly as she was.

Charlotte leaned toward him, pressing a light kiss on his cheek while he was driving. "Thank you." she whispered, then kissed him again with a shy smile. "I really love the gift and I love you."

• •

"The cruise leaves around half past five in the morning," Damon said as he gently pulled Charlotte’s coat snug around her shoulders, making sure she wouldn’t feel the chill once the sea breeze reached them. "Doesn’t that sound wonderful? We’ll watch the sunrise together from the deck."

Charlotte’s eyes lit up, and she nodded with a smile. "That sounds so romantic."

Even though they still had to wait a few more hours before the ship set sail, they were already allowed to board and rest for a while.

However, Charlotte wanted to take a short night walk with Damon, while their luggage was being taken care of by their private porter.

The harbor lights shimmered against the dark water, and the cool night air carried the salty scent of the sea. For Charlotte, even this simple walk beside Damon felt more precious than any luxury the cruise could offer.

"What do you feel today?" Damon asked as they stopped by a small street vendor selling hot roasted corn.

Charlotte let out a soft laugh. "Why do you ask that? Of course I’m happy."

"No, I mean ... this is your first time finishing a film shoot after being away for five years." Damon’s gaze softened, his gray eyes warm as they lingered on her. "What do you really feel?"

Charlotte fell silent for a moment. It was the first time someone had actually asked how she felt that day. Everyone else only assumed she was thrilled, excited to finally return to the industry after so long, and yes, part of her was. But the truth was ... her feelings were far more tangled than that.

"It feels ... mixed," she finally admitted, her voice low.

Damon leaned casually against the iron post beside him. "How so?" he asked in a low voice.

"I’m happy, of course," she said softly. "But at the same time ... I feel nervous. It’s been five years, Damon. What if people think I’m not good enough anymore? I mean, I haven’t even seen my full performance on screen yet."

After the shooting was done, the movie would still have to go through the editing process, and only then would she truly see how everything had turned out. Until that moment came, doubt lingered in her chest like a shadow she couldn’t shake.

Damon tilted his head slightly. "You’re worrying about something that hasn’t even happened yet," he said softly. "From what I’ve seen, you have nothing to be afraid of."

Charlotte let out a small sigh. "You’re only saying that because you love me."

"I do love you," Damon admitted without hesitation. "But when it comes to your work, I try to stay objective. I watched you on set with my own eyes, and the producer even showed me some clips."

He leaned a little closer, his gaze sincere. "And the truth is ... you still have it, Charlotte. That spark. That rising star everyone once saw in you, it’s still there." He smiled warmly. "So don’t worry too much. My Charlotte is still just as talented as she was back then."

Charlotte lifted her face, doing everything she could to keep her tears from falling after hearing Damon’s gentle and warm words, lifting her confidence in ways she hadn’t felt in years.

It was almost as if he already knew she wouldn’t be able to enjoy their vacation fully unless they spoke about her worries first.

A soft laugh escaped her, though her eyes glistened. "You always know how to make me cry at the wrong time," she whispered.

Damon chuckled as he slowly pulled Charlotte into his arms. He held her close, wrapping her tightly against his chest while his hand gently stroked her back. "Oh, don’t cry," he teased softly. "If people see your swollen eyes, they’ll think I kidnapped you and brought you here."

Charlotte let out a watery laugh against his chest, her tears threatening to spill even as a smile curved her lips. "You’re ridiculous," she murmured, swatting lightly at his shoulder.

Damon tilted his head down, brushing his lips against her hair. "Ridiculous about you."

She was about to reply when the street vendor suddenly spoke up. "Sir, here’s your roasted corn."

Realizing they were still in a public place, Charlotte quickly hid her face against Damon’s shoulder. Damon, meanwhile, loosened his hold just enough to take the food with one hand, while the other stayed firmly around her waist in a protective embrace.

"Thank you." Damon smiled at the street vendor before leading Charlotte away to a quieter spot.

They sat down on a wooden bench near the edge of the pier, where the gentle sound of waves lapping against the dock filled the silence.

Damon blew softly on the steaming roasted corn before holding it up to Charlotte’s lips. "Careful, it’s hot."

Charlotte blinked at him, caught between laughter and embarrassment. "Are you seriously feeding me in public?" she whispered, her cheeks warming.

Damon’s lips curved into a teasing smile. "Why not? You’re my baby."

Charlotte let out a soft hiss, feeling a little embarrassed. Sometimes Damon could act like a professional heartthrob, so effortlessly charming that it made her cringe, or maybe ... that was just who he truly was.

She bit into the corn quickly, hoping to hide the flustered look on her face, while Damon chuckled beside her, clearly enjoying her reaction more than the food.

chapters.title: Chapter 138: Will You Be My Wife?
chapters.content:
Chapter 138: Will You Be My Wife?

They finally decided to take a short nap on the ship before sunrise. When Charlotte stepped into the cabin, she couldn’t stop the little gasp that escaped her lips.

"Oh my god, Damon! This room looks more like a hotel suite than a cabin on a ship!" she exclaimed, her voice full of excitement.

Damon only chuckled, watching with amusement as Charlotte wandered around, checking every corner of their cabin like a curious child.

The cabin truly did look like a luxury hotel room. There was a cozy living area with a couch, table, and TV, along with a separate bedroom.

When Charlotte pushed the bedroom door open, her eyes widened in awe. A wide window stretched across the wall, opening to the endless sea. Right now, it was just darkness, but she knew the moment the sun came up, it would turn into a view she’d never forget.

"Don’t go out to the balcony just yet," Damon said gently before she could reach for the door. "The wind will be too strong, and you might get cold."

He took Charlotte’s hand and led her to the bed. "Come on, let’s sleep for a while. We’ll wake up before the sun rises."

Charlotte didn’t argue. She quickly slipped off her shoes and jacket, then climbed onto the bed, nestling into Damon’s arms as if that spot had always belonged to her.

"Do you like the room?" Damon asked in a low voice as he rubbed her back in slow, comforting circles. His eyes softened when he looked at her, and he leaned down to press a tender kiss to her forehead.

"I love it." Charlotte’s smile was as sweet as her voice. She tipped her head up and brushed her lips against his twice. "But more than the room, I love you, Damon."

Damon’s heart warmed at her words, and he couldn’t hold back the smile that spread across his face. "I love you too, sweetheart."

After that, they didn’t say another word. Wrapped in each other’s warmth, they let their eyes drift shut.

When the first light of dawn touched the horizon, Damon gently stroked Charlotte’s cheek. His voice was soft, almost a whisper. "Sweetheart, the sun is about to rise. You’ll miss it if you don’t wake up."

Charlotte stirred, her lashes fluttering before she opened her eyes. Her beautiful blue gaze met his, and just like always, it pulled him in, like the ocean itself had decided to live inside her eyes.

Charlotte blinked slowly, her vision still hazy from sleep, until the glow of orange light began to spill into the room. She turned her head toward the window, and her breath caught. "Damon ... look."

The horizon was painted in shades of gold, pink, and soft lavender. The sun peeked out from the endless stretch of ocean, casting a shimmering path across the waves that looked like liquid fire.

Charlotte immediately got out of bed and hurried to the balcony. The sea breeze rushed to greet her, scattering her long hair like a veil dancing in the wind. From below, the loud blast of the ship’s horn echoed as the cruise finally began its journey.

She gripped the railing, her eyes widening at the breathtaking view before her. The sun was now halfway above the horizon, casting a golden trail across the endless sea. Her lips curved into a smile as she whispered, "It’s so beautiful ...."

Damon followed her out, his gaze lingering not on the sunrise, but on the woman standing before him. He couldn’t stop himself from being captivated as the golden light illuminated Charlotte’s face, making her look like a beautiful goddess when she smiled warmly.

"Yes," he whispered without thinking, "so beautiful."

He swallowed hard, his gaze locked on her as if he couldn’t bear to look away, not even for a second.

The truth was, he hadn’t brought her on this cruise just for a simple vacation. No, there was something much bigger, something he had been holding onto and waiting for all this time.

He slipped his hand into his pocket, his fingers brushing against the small velvet box he had been carrying for days. His heart pounded as if it wanted to break free, but he drew in a deep breath to calm himself.

He had wanted to make her his wife for so long, to tie their lives together officially. But he knew her past marriage had left scars too deep, wounds that took time to heal. That was why he waited patiently until she no longer trembled at the thought of marriage.

Even now, he still felt uncertain, wondering if Charlotte would accept him or ask him to wait longer.

But as he looked at her bathed in sunlight, Damon knew one thing for certain: he couldn’t hold it back anymore. For God’s sake, he wanted her to be his wife, not someday, not later, but now.

On the other side, Charlotte leaned over the railing, her hair fluttering wildly in the salty wind as she stared at the horizon. "It feels endless," she said softly, almost to herself.

Damon’s hand tightened around the velvet box in his pocket. His chest rose and fell with the weight of everything he wanted to say. Slowly, he stepped closer until he stood just behind her.

"Charlotte," he called, his voice lower than usual, almost trembling.

She turned, her blue eyes catching the sunlight, and gave him a curious smile. "Hmm? What is it?"

Damon’s throat went dry, but he forced himself to move. He pulled the box from his pocket and dropped to one knee.

At that moment, the world seemed to pause, the waves crashing below, the wind whipping around them, even Charlotte herself frozen in shock.

Her hand flew to her lips. "Damon ...."

He opened the box, and inside was a ring crowned with a diamond that caught the sunlight and sparkled like fire. Damon’s eyes softened as he looked up at her.

"Charlotte, sweetheart... I know we don’t need a marriage certificate to prove anything because our bond alone already makes you mine."

"But Charlotte, I’m a greedy man. I can’t help but want you to be mine completely." He held his breath before continuing, "I want you to be my home, my mate, my Luna, and my wife."

His chest rose and fell as he took in a deep breath. Then, with his heart in his throat, he spoke the words he had been carrying for so long. "Charlotte Dawson ... will you marry me?"

chapters.title: Chapter 139: When Love Became Forever
chapters.content:
Chapter 139: When Love Became Forever

Damon didn’t move for a few moments, waiting for his heart to hear her answer. But for some reason, Charlotte said nothing.

She just stood there, her hand covering her lips, and suddenly tears began to fall down her cheeks.

The sight made Damon’s chest tighten. Panic rushed through him, and a horrible thought struck him. ’Did I push her too hard? Did I make her feel trapped?’

"Charlotte," his voice came out rough, almost breaking, "if this feels too heavy for you ... it’s alright. You can say no. I can wait as long as you need."

"No."

Damon’s eyes widened, unable to believe that word had truly come from her lips. "You ... you’re rejecting me?"

Charlotte suddenly let out a soft laugh and cried at the same time. "No, Damon. I don’t want to reject you."

This time, his eyes grew even wider. His knees felt weak, and his breath caught in his throat. "Does that mean—"

"Yes!" Charlotte suddenly threw herself into his arms, nearly knocking him over if he hadn’t steadied them both. Her voice trembled but was filled with certainty. "Yes, I want to marry you!"

For so long, she had believed that marriage was something terrifying, something she never wanted to touch again. But the moment she saw Damon kneeling in front of her, holding out that small velvet box, all those fears melted away.

She realized then, it wasn’t marriage she had been afraid of. It was the man who once filled that role. The thought of Julian as her husband had haunted her, suffocating her with fear.

But Damon ... Damon was different. With him, all she felt was safety, warmth, and the kind of peace that made her heart soar. Being with him was like lying on the softest clouds.

She hugged him tightly, burying her face in the curve of his neck. Her tears wouldn’t stop, no matter how hard she tried, but this time they weren’t from fear. They were from joy so overwhelming it spilled out of her.

Damon’s arms wrapped firmly around her, his hand stroking the back of her head with such tenderness it made her cry harder. "You’re crying because you’re happy, right?" he asked softly, needing to hear it.

Charlotte gave his chest a playful smack, half annoyed and half overwhelmed. "Of course I’m happy!" She lifted her tear-stained face to him, her cheeks wet and flushed. "It’s not fair. You’re not even crying."

A smile curved on Damon’s lips, warm and unshakable. "Oh, sweetheart, I am crying, right here in my heart." He brushed her tears away with his sleeve, his touch gentle as he tucked her hair behind her ear. His voice lowered, filled with devotion. "But how could I cry when every time I look at you, all I want to do ... is smile?"

Charlotte murmured shyly, her voice barely above a whisper. "I ... also want to smile every time I see you."

Damon tilted his head with a teasing glint in his eyes. "What was that? I didn’t hear you clearly."

Charlotte knew he was only pretending. There was no way an Alpha like him could miss her words, no matter how quietly she spoke. He just wanted to hear her say it again.

Instead of answering, she slid her arms around his neck, pulling him closer until their breaths mingled. Then, without another word, she pressed her lips to his in a kiss.

It was gentle at first, then deepening as if she wanted to pour every unsaid feeling into it.

The world seemed to melt away in that moment, leaving only the warmth of his embrace and the sweetness of the kiss they shared.

When the sun had fully risen, they slowly pulled away from the kiss, their foreheads still touching as soft smiles curved on their lips.

"I love you, Charlotte," Damon whispered, his thumb brushing gently against her cheek. "I truly love you more than anything in this world."

Charlotte’s eyes shimmered, her lashes wet as if she might cry again. But before the tears could fall, Damon wrapped her tightly in his arms. "Hey, no more crying," he murmured, pressing a kiss into her hair. "If you keep this up, your pretty eyes will get all swollen."

"Then stop being so sweet," Charlotte mumbled, hiding her face in the curve of his neck. "You make me feel so loved it’s overwhelming."

"That’s because I really do love you," he said softly. "And that won’t ever change, not in one year, not in ten years, not even when we’re both old and gray ... even when we’re nothing but bones in the ground."

"Now you sound like an old man." Charlotte pulled her face back to look at him, her lips curving into a smile. "I want to live with you for such a long, long time ... so don’t you dare die too soon."

Damon chuckled. "Good thing we have such a long life ahead of us. I just hope you’ll never get bored of me."

"Never," Charlotte replied without hesitation.

Damon’s chest tightened with affection. Carefully, he lifted the velvet box once more and carefully slid the diamond ring onto her finger. The sunlight caught the gem, making it sparkle brilliantly as if the sunrise itself had blessed their union.

Charlotte stared at the ring, her lips parting in awe. Her heart swelled, and she whispered, almost breathless, "It’s beautiful..."

Damon lifted her hand to his lips, pressing a tender kiss against her knuckles. "It’s only beautiful because it’s on you," he said softly. "This ring means that you’re mine, and I’ll spend the rest of my life proving that I’m yours."

"I love you, Damon," Charlotte whispered softly before pressing her lips against his once more. This time, neither of them pulled away.

The kiss deepened, slow at first, then heated, until it was no longer just a kiss but a fire that pulled them closer and closer.

Damon swept her into his arms, holding her as if she were the most precious thing in the world. He carried her back into the cabin, his lips never straying far from hers.

It was their very first day on the cruise ship, yet neither of them cared about the grand halls, the restaurants, or the endless activities waiting outside. All they wanted was to stay together as long as they could.

They let the world slip away as they gazed at one another, their clothes falling away until there was nothing left between them but bare skin and burning love.

They surrendered to the passion that consumed them, a desire so fierce it left no space for anything else.

In that moment, time itself seemed to stop, leaving only the two of them, wrapped in heat and devotion, lost in each other for hours.

chapters.title: Chapter 140: The Calm Before the Chaos
chapters.content:
Chapter 140: The Calm Before the Chaos

Charlotte lay on Damon’s arm, trying to steady her breathing after letting him claim her again and again from dawn until noon. They had even skipped the breakfast buffet because tasting each other was so much better than food.

"Was I too rough?" Damon asked, covering his eyes with his arm. His breathing was still heavy after pounding into her vigorously for hours.

"Kind of." Charlotte bit her lip, feeling a faint soreness between her thighs. "But don’t worry." She slowly turned to face him, resting her hand on his chest. "I really enjoyed it, truly."

Damon brushed his fingers along her cheek and pressed a soft kiss to her lips. "Are you hungry? Do you want me to go to the restaurant and bring you something to eat?"

Charlotte thought for a moment before smiling. "How about we go together? I want to look around the ship and see the facilities."

It would be such a waste to spend time inside the room when the cruise ship had so many interesting and beautiful things to see.

"Are you sure? Can you even walk?" Damon teased.

She smacked his chest immediately, chuckling. "I’m not that helpless!"

If she were just an ordinary human, she probably would have fainted and been unable to walk for three days. But thanks to her tremendous healing ability, it wasn’t a big deal. After a bath, her body likely wouldn’t ache anymore.

"I know, I know." Damon laughed as well, then climbed out of bed first. In the next moment, he scooped her up and carried her into the bathroom. "At least let me take responsibility for everything I did to you earlier."

Charlotte didn’t protest and simply let her soon-to-be husband take care of her. He gently washed away the mess from her body, even helping her release the seed that was still lingering deep inside her honeypot.

Damon’s touch was surprisingly tender, nothing like the wild passion he had shown earlier. His large hands moved carefully, as if she were made of glass, each gesture filled with patience and devotion.

Charlotte leaned against his chest, closing her eyes while the warm water ran down her skin. For the first time that day, she allowed herself to simply relax, letting him handle everything.

After they had washed up and put on their clothes, the two of them stepped out of the room and asked the cabin steward to change the bed with fresh sheets.

Charlotte’s cheeks burned with embarrassment. To her, it felt like openly telling the staff that they had already had sex, even before the ship had been sailing for a full day.

Damon, of course, wasn’t bothered at all. He slipped an arm around her waist and reassured her with a grin. "It’s normal. Couples on their honeymoon do this all the time."

"We’re not even married yet," Charlotte whispered, leaning closer to him as they walked down the hall toward the restaurant.

"Then just think of it as a pre-honeymoon," Damon said lightly, his teasing tone making her blush even harder.

They spent the afternoon wandering across the cruise ship, hand in hand.

They visited the pool deck, peeked into the theater, and even strolled past the casino where music and laughter spilled out into the halls. Lunch at the restaurant was delightful, the dishes almost as extravagant as the sea view surrounding them.

When they finally reached the ship’s deck, Charlotte closed her eyes, letting the breeze brush against her skin. She was truly enjoying their pre-honeymoon, especially after working so hard for so long.

Everything on this ship felt like a reward, and if she could, she wanted to savor it for as long as possible.

"This feels perfect." Charlotte took a deep breath, inhaling the refreshing scent of the sea.

Damon stood close beside her, his arm securely wrapped around her waist as if he was afraid that letting go would cause her to tumble into the ocean, an idea that was completely ridiculous.

"I wish I could bring everyone here." Charlotte giggled and tilted her head toward him. "Haven would be over the moon."

Damon leaned casually against the railing, his aqua eyes catching the sunlight. "Sweetheart, I brought you here for our honeymoon, and yet you still can’t stop thinking about our family."

Charlotte laughed softly, covering her mouth. "Well, I can’t help it. They’re a part of me. But ..." she pressed closer to him, resting her cheek against his chest, "right now, it’s just us, and I don’t want this moment to end."

Charlotte knew that once this vacation was over, their lives would get busy again. She had to prepare for their promotion and also search for a new script for her next movie.

She wasn’t sure yet what kind of project she would take on, but a part of her wanted to try something different, something bold, maybe even an action film.

Her manager, Chloe, had once suggested that she take on something sweet, like a romance drama, but ... Charlotte couldn’t stand the thought of playing a romantic role with any man other than Damon.

It was just ... she still carried a deep trauma in her heart when it came to "men," so she only found comfort in Damon’s presence. Besides, she had already played several romantic roles in the past, so perhaps it would be better if she built a new persona now.

"What are you thinking about?" Damon looked at her with curiosity. "You always frown when you’re thinking too hard." He reached out and gently rubbed her forehead.

Charlotte smiled. "I’m not thinking about anything bad. I was just wondering what kind of movie I should take on next."

Damon tilted his head slightly and said, "Don’t worry. I think you can take on any movie and absolutely nail it."

She bit the inside of her cheek before speaking hesitantly. "But honestly ... I’ve been thinking about something else. I don’t know if it will work this time, but ... what if we try to have a baby before I return?"

Some people might think she was crazy for wanting another break just as her career was picking up again, but in her heart, she felt that if they truly wanted to start a family, then now was the right time to try, even if the outcome was uncertain.

"I can take a break for about a year," Charlotte said softly. "If it doesn’t work, that’s okay. But if it does ... I’d be overjoyed."

Either way, whether they ended up with a child or not, she could focus on her career afterward without regrets. Besides, people loved drama, and if they saw her trying to build a family with her new, gentle husband, they would only cheer her on.

"Are you sure about this?" Damon asked. He finally straightened his back and looked her in the eyes. "I don’t mind if we don’t focus on having children anytime soon. We have plenty of time."

Charlotte laughed softly. "I know we have a long time, Damon. But I want to build a little family with you."

They had even promised to adopt rescued children one day, which meant their home would be filled with even more love and laughter.

Even if Charlotte couldn’t get pregnant within that year, she could still use the time to bond and grow closer with their adopted children.

Damon let out a gentle breath before pulling Charlotte closer against him. "If that’s what you want, then—"

BANG!

Charlotte’s eyes widened as her body jolted in shock. The loud sound of a gunshot tore through the ship, shattering the peaceful moment.

"HELP! HELP! SOMEONE’S BEEN SHOT!"

chapters.title: Chapter 141: Hostage At Sea (1)
chapters.content:
Chapter 141: Hostage At Sea (1)

"HELP! HELP! SOMEONE’S BEEN SHOT!"

Damon instantly pulled Charlotte into his arms, his hand never leaving her waist. His body was tense, ready to shield her from whatever danger was coming.

"W-what happened?" Charlotte’s face turned pale. She never thought something terrible would happen out here in the middle of the ocean, especially not when she had only wanted a peaceful vacation with Damon.

"I don’t know." Damon swept his gaze across the deck and suddenly saw several people dressed in black, carrying rifles in their hands.

Without wasting a second, he tried to contact Louis or Diana, but the signal on his phone was gone, a clear sign that the satellite Wi-Fi had been shut down.

"Throw your phones into the ocean now!" one of the masked men shouted.

Damon’s eyes sharpened as he finally caught their scent. "They’re werewolves," he growled under his breath. "Rogues."

Charlotte’s fingers curled into Damon’s shirt, her breath caught in her throat. "Julian?"

Damon’s teeth clenched, and the fury blazing in his eyes made her stomach twist. "I think so."

"Maybe we can jump into the ocean?" Charlotte glanced down, only to see small boats circling the cruise ship like sharks. Her hope dropped. "Or ... maybe not."

No wonder neither of them had sensed any werewolves when they first boarded. These bastards must have sneaked in after the ship set sail.

"They’ve even blocked off the ocean." Damon narrowed his eyes, catching sight of a tattoo on one man’s arm, the mark of the Silverfang Pack, Ronan Warren’s pack. His voice was low with anger. "This is why I told the Elders to disband them. But they didn’t listen."

Still, Damon knew it wasn’t entirely the Elders’ fault. Normally, when an Alpha was in trouble or unable to lead, the second-in-command—the Beta—would step in to take charge.

As long as the Beta wasn’t tied to the Alpha’s crimes, the pack was allowed to continue.

But that never sat right with Damon.

The Beta of the Silverfang Pack had been so close to Ronan that it seemed impossible for him to know nothing about the crimes his Alpha had committed.

The Elders probably didn’t want to disband the pack because they thought it would be easier to keep them under the Nocturnal Order’s control rather than letting them roam free.

But in the end, whether they were left free or kept under the Nocturnal Order’s watch, they still managed to cause chaos.

"Hey, you! Throw away your phone!" one of the men shouted, pointing his rifle at Damon. "Do it, or I’ll shoot this woman!"

But he wasn’t aiming at Charlotte. His gun was pointed at a terrified mother clutching her baby boy in her arms.

Charlotte’s heart sank. That was when she realized that they were being targeted. These men would use the innocent passengers as hostages to force Damon and her into obeying.

It was a sick, disgusting move.

"Throw it, Damon," Charlotte whispered.

Damon’s jaw clenched. He took her phone along with his and hurled them both into the ocean. "Are you satisfied now?" he asked coldly.

"Now, on your knees!" the man barked. "Don’t even think about fighting back, or our Alpha will blow this ship sky high! Every human here will die because of you!"

Since most of the people on the ship were families, there were so many children aboard, and Damon knew well that Charlotte had a soft spot for kids; even he himself couldn’t bring himself to let those men hurt them.

"Where’s your Alpha?" Damon growled as he and Charlotte slowly sank to their knees.

The man with the rifle sneered, his eyes narrowing at Damon’s question. "You’ll see him soon enough. For now, keep your mouth shut and obey."

Charlotte could feel Damon’s fury radiating through his body, his muscles taut like a coiled spring. His eyes burned with a deadly glow, but he held himself back for her sake, and for the terrified families around them.

The rogues spread out across the deck, herding the passengers into a corner like cattle. Mothers clutched their children, men tried to shield their wives, and the cries of frightened kids pierced the heavy night air.

Not long after, two men stepped out from inside the ship onto the deck. Their faces were familiar to Charlotte, so familiar that it made her sick to her stomach.

The Nocturnal Order had hunted them for so long, but somehow, Ronan and Julian always managed to slip away. They had learned how to hide their scent so well that no one—not even the Order—could track them.

"My wife," Julian said, a wide smile spreading across his face, his eyes locked onto her. "We finally meet again."

Charlotte’s chest tightened, but before she could even react, Damon outstretched his arm protectively in front of her, blocking Julian’s view of her.

"I’m your ex-wife, Julian," Charlotte replied coldly, her eyes sharp as ice. "We’re divorced."

Julian’s smile faltered for a moment before twisting into something darker, something that made a shiver run down Charlotte’s spine. "Ex-wife?" he repeated, his tone dripping with mockery. "Charlotte, you can’t erase me that easily. A piece of paper doesn’t change the bond we share."

This man ... he was like chewing gum stuck in her hair, no matter how hard Charlotte tried to tear him out of her life, he clung on, showing up again and again.

Julian leaned forward, his voice lowering with cruel delight. "I heard a rumor ... that you had a miscarriage after our night together?"

Charlotte gritted her teeth as he suddenly brought up the night he kidnapped her and hid her in an underground tunnel.

Months had passed since then, but she could still hear the doctor’s voice as if it were yesterday, telling her that her baby was gone.

"Shut your mouth, Julian," Damon snarled, his tone cold and dangerous. "Or I’ll rip it off your face."

BANG!

The gunshot split the air. Damon and Charlotte’s eyes flew wide as they saw Julian fire at a young boy who had been crouching between his parents.

"My baby! Nooo!" the mother’s scream tore through the deck.

Julian only smiled, unfazed by the horror around him. "I guess my men didn’t make it clear enough, dear uncle ...." His eyes gleamed with cruel delight. "If you keep acting so arrogant, I’ll just have to teach you a lesson."

chapters.title: Chapter 142: Hostage At Sea (2)
chapters.content:
Chapter 142: Hostage At Sea (2)

Charlotte’s hands trembled as she watched Julian shoot the boy in the head, like the child’s life meant nothing. Damon immediately reached out and gripped her hand tightly, as if telling her without words that no matter what happened, he would protect her.

"Why...?" Charlotte’s voice cracked as she faced Julian. "Why are you here, Julian? Is this because of me? Do you want to take me with you again?"

Julian tilted his head slightly. "As tempting as that sounds, my dear Charlotte, unfortunately, I’m not here for you." He smiled coldly, then shifted his gaze toward Damon. "We’re here for my dear uncle."

Ronan, who had been standing beside Julian all this time, spoke next. "After you destroyed my lab and my entire pack, did you really think I’d just let you walk free?"

Charlotte ground her teeth when Ronan treated the orphanage he’d built as nothing more than a laboratory.

He went on, "You even kidnapped my wife." Ronan clicked his tongue in irritation. "My life was ruined because of you."

"Kidnapped your wife?" Damon clenched his fists and glared at Ronan. "You abused her and even drove her mad! Instead of ’kidnapping’ her, we actually saved her from you and your disgusting son!"

Ronan spat on the ground, hissing, "Abused her? I only disciplined her because she misbehaved. Why would she tell her son to surrender himself to the Nocturnal Order? As a mother, she should have helped him escape punishment."

Ronan was sick in the head, so no wonder Julian was as crazy as he was. Charlotte suddenly felt a strange sense of relief that she had barely interacted with him in the past.

"Someone like you," Charlotte said sharply, her eyes narrowing, "has done countless horrible things just to gain power. But experimenting on children? Torturing them since they were little? You’re a monster! They’re kids, not lab rats!"

Ronan raised his hand as if to strike her, but Damon placed his hand in front of her, shielding her instantly. His voice dropped into a warning tone. "Alpha Ronan, you’ll be making a grave mistake if you lay a hand on us," he said coldly. "If you think you can walk away after hurting the Alpha and Luna of the Crimson Moon Pack, you’re wrong."

"My wolves will hunt you down," Damon continued, "even if you run to the ends of the world."

Ronan suddenly smiled, laughing as if mocking him. "That’s only if people know I killed you. But what if they don’t? We’re in the middle of the ocean, Damon. Anything can happen out here... even a ship sinking."

The way he spoke to Damon without using his Alpha title was already enough to show that Ronan saw him as nothing more than a pest.

"If you really plan to sink this ship, then why should I even listen to you?" Damon said, slowly getting back on his feet until one of Ronan’s men suddenly threw a brown sack in front of him, stopping him mid-way.

"Mhmm... Mhmm..."

"There’s someone inside!" Charlotte gasped when she saw something moving in the sack. For some reason, the scent coming from it felt oddly familiar.

Damon must’ve noticed it too, because he tore the sack open without hesitation, and found Mona inside. Her wrists and ankles were tied, and her mouth was sealed with duct tape.

"Mom!" Damon quickly pulled her out and untied every rope on her body. "What happened... what happened to you?!"

Mona coughed several times when he ripped the tape off her mouth. Charlotte noticed how pale her face had become, and her eyes had turned a sickly yellow.

"Poison..." Carmen hissed in a hoarse voice. "They poisoned me!"

Ronan clicked his tongue. "Oh, don’t be so dramatic, woman. I’m just returning the favor for what you did to my father," he said casually. "At least I was nice enough to leave the antidote somewhere on this ship."

He lowered his head, glancing at the watch on his wrist. "Good luck finding it within one hour."

BOOM!

Damon immediately wrapped his arms around Mona and Charlotte when a loud explosion erupted from the ship’s lower deck. The entire ship shook violently and started to tilt to one side.

"Or maybe you won’t even need the antidote," Ronan sneered. "Since you’ll all drown anyway."

Damon was about to reach for him, but the ship swayed again, forcing him to use both hands to hold onto his mother and his mate at the same time.

Ronan’s men threw smoke bombs onto the deck. Thick smoke spread everywhere, burning their eyes. Through the haze, Charlotte could faintly see the men wearing gas masks.

"Damon, they’re running away!" she shouted, covering her mouth and nose with her arm.

Damon clicked his tongue in frustration. The situation around them was a mess; the ship was sinking, people were screaming everywhere, and when he heard the sound of small boats speeding away below the cruise ship, he realized Ronan had left them stranded in the middle of the ocean.

"Damn it!" Damon tried to stand, but the smoke had some kind of paralyzing effect. It wasn’t strong enough to knock out werewolves, but the humans around them had already collapsed, some even fell into the sea.

"Damon, calm down." Charlotte pulled him closer to her, pressing her back against the railing. She created a small circle around them, shielding their faces from the thick smoke to prevent it from irritating their eyes or entering their lungs too much.

"There’s no point chasing them now," she said, covering her mouth with a handkerchief. "We need to find another way out. But first, we have to find the antidote and save your mom."

Charlotte took off her cardigan and tied it around Mona’s mouth and nose. Her skin felt ice-cold, and her lips were turning blue.

"Mom, what happened to you?" Damon asked, holding her close so she wouldn’t slip. "Was what Ronan said earlier true?"

He gritted his teeth, not expecting that his entire vacation and proposal day could be ruined in such a short time.

chapters.title: Chapter 143: A Race Against the Sinking Ship
chapters.content:
Chapter 143: A Race Against the Sinking Ship

Ronan had claimed that Mona once poisoned his father, Arnold Warren.

Arnold had been dead for more than fifteen years, around the same time Samuel Sullivan, Damon’s father, also passed away. But Arnold’s death had always been a mystery, because no one ever knew what really caused it.

According to Ronan, his father was found dead in his sleep, which was strange because werewolves couldn’t possibly suffer from heart attacks in their sleep like humans did.

Their healing ability should’ve automatically repaired any damage in their bodies, including diseases that affected their organs.

Because of that, his case remained unsolved. The forensic team found no trace of poison in his system, and in the end, they concluded that his healing ability had failed due to old age.

"He was a bastard," Mona said between violent coughs. "Just like your father, he deserved to die, maybe even worse."

Damon was about to say something, but the ship sank even deeper into the ocean. The smoke slowly began to clear, and the paralyzing effect was fading away.

Charlotte glanced at her watch and set a timer. "Damon, we only have about forty-five minutes left to find the antidote."

Her heart was burning with anger because Julian and Ronan kept playing with people’s lives as if they were nothing more than toys.

Back then, Damon also had to search for an antidote to save her from the Nightshade poison, and now he had to do it again, this time for his mother.

She knew this cruel game was Julian’s idea from the start, just to make Damon suffer even more.

"Alright, stay here with Mom," Damon said. "I’ll go inside the ship and find the antidote."

"No, wait!" Charlotte grabbed his hand tightly. "We should go together. Our time is limited, so it’s better if we don’t split up."

If they waited on the ship’s deck while Damon searched inside, there was a chance he wouldn’t make it back in time to save Mona because it would take too long for him to return once he found the antidote.

That was why it was safer for them to stay together, so the moment he found the antidote, he could immediately give it to Mona.

"The ship is sinking," Damon warned through clenched teeth. "If we move too slowly, we might get trapped inside."

The ship was going down slowly after the explosion; they still had a little time to search, but every second counted.

However, it wasn’t impossible that the whole ship would suddenly plunge into the ocean while they were still inside.

"There’s no time to argue." Charlotte gently cupped his cheek. "We’re fated mates, Damon. If you die, living without you would hurt too much. So I’m staying with you."

If Damon died, she wouldn’t survive the pain. She would rather drown by his side than live on with a wound in her heart that would never heal.

He was her fated mate, so losing him would break her completely, so deeply that she knew she might never want to go on.

She had lived through so much pain already, and she was terrified of falling back into that dark place. If life expected her to go through that one more time... then she would rather sink into the ocean with him than face a world without him.

Damon stared at her for a moment, his jaw tight, his eyes burning with a mix of fear and love.

He didn’t like hearing her talk like that—like she had already accepted death just because he was involved—but deep down, he understood because he felt the same way.

"Alright," he said at last. "But stay close to me. Don’t let go of my hand, no matter what happens."

Charlotte nodded. "I won’t."

He carried Mona on his back while Charlotte tied her cardigan around both of them to keep Mona from slipping. Her skin had turned so pale, maybe as white as paper, that Charlotte couldn’t help but wonder what kind of poison could harm a werewolf like this.

That poison had clearly destroyed her healing ability, just like the substance Julian once injected into her.

"Watch your step," Damon said as they walked into the ship.

It was difficult for them to walk inside because there were so many people running out, trying to save themselves. Charlotte held her breath when she saw bodies on the floor, some crushed by falling debris, others lying unconscious.

She wanted to help them, but at the same time, she had to prioritize Mona. After all, there was no way she could save everyone.

"They’re the victims of us," Charlotte murmured, turning her face away from the corpses scattered on the floor. "If only we—"

"They’re victims of Ronan," Damon interrupted firmly. "That’s all."

Charlotte wanted to argue, but she chose to stay silent because it wasn’t the right time to decide who was right or wrong.

After a moment, she asked, "Do you still remember the scent of the antidote?"

Even though the poison inside Mona’s body was different from what Julian had used on her before, Charlotte was sure the antidote would smell similar, since the poison had almost the same effects as Nightshade.

"I do," Damon said, his face filled with worry. He didn’t even blink because his mind was focused completely on saving his mother. "But it’s hard to trace the scent of the antidote in this chaos."

People kept rushing past them to escape, and the strong smell of the sea made it even harder to catch any scent.

"I have a question," Charlotte said.

Damon only hummed in response, so she continued, "Do you think Ronan really wants us dead?"

It was an important question because if he did, then there was almost no chance they’d find the antidote. He might have hidden it in a part of the ship that sank first.

However, if he actually had plans for them after this, then they might still have a chance to find the antidote.

"I don’t know," Damon replied. "But if revenge is what he’s after, I doubt someone like him would let us die that easily."

chapters.title: Chapter 144: The Price of Two Lives
chapters.content:
Chapter 144: The Price of Two Lives

"I don’t know," Damon replied. "But if revenge is what he’s after, I doubt someone like him would let us die that easily."

"Then..." Charlotte looked up at the part of the ship that wasn’t tilted or sinking yet. "I guess we have to climb up."

The cruise ship had four floors, and Charlotte instantly crossed out the bottom two because they were closest to where the explosion happened. That left only two floors, but since there were so many rooms, it would still be difficult to find the cure.

"The restaurant," she said suddenly. "It’s probably the last place that will sink."

She wasn’t completely sure, but it was their best bet right now.

Damon nodded. "I think so too." He quickened his pace and grabbed Charlotte’s arm so they wouldn’t get separated. "Let’s go."

On Damon’s back, Mona didn’t say anything at all. She had lost all her energy, and the only thing she could do was breathe.

All around them, people were slipping and falling from above. Everyone was trying to get out of the ship, but because it was tilted so badly, only the strongest could hold on. The others either slammed into the walls or got stuck between fallen objects.

Meanwhile, neither Damon nor Charlotte had any trouble climbing up. Damon had tremendous stamina, and even though Charlotte wasn’t as strong as him, she could still keep up by holding onto his arm.

Once they reached the restaurant, everything was already a mess. The tables were still in place because their legs were fixed to the floor, but plates and glasses were scattered everywhere.

"I smell it," Damon suddenly said. "The antidote. I can smell it."

His expression hardened, and his eyes looked extremely focused, as if he had locked onto his prey. Charlotte didn’t say anything when she saw Damon sniffing around for the antidote’s scent because she didn’t want to disturb him.

After a while, Charlotte said, "Maybe it’s inside the locked shelf."

Before Damon could take another step, a cold, familiar voice echoed from behind the overturned counter.

"You’re looking for this?"

Charlotte froze, and Damon instantly moved in front of her, his body tense like a drawn bow.

From behind the counter, Julian stepped out, holding a small glass vial between his fingers. The antidote glimmered under the emergency lights.

Charlotte frowned and hissed, "Damn it, Julian! Why are you still here?!"

Honestly, she would rather sink with the ship than have to look at his face again. She was so sick of him, so sick that she felt like she might throw up right then and there.

"Oh, don’t be like that, my wife." Julian flashed a smile, acting as if nothing was wrong. "I didn’t want to say this in front of my father earlier, but Charlotte... I miss you."

Charlotte almost gagged. The feeling of disgust was so strong she had to swallow it down, and what made it worse was remembering that she used to blush whenever he confessed things like that.

How could she ever have loved someone like him?

Damon tried to step forward, but his legs suddenly weakened. He staggered, barely catching himself on a nearby table.

"Damon?!" Charlotte grabbed his arm in panic.

His breathing turned heavy, and his skin grew pale. Only then did Charlotte notice the faint black veins crawling up his neck, the same ones spreading across Mona’s body.

"What?" Charlotte stared at him in disbelief. "How did the poison spread to you too?"

She reached out to touch his face, but Damon quickly turned away from her.

"Don’t touch me!" he snapped, though Charlotte could hear the fear—not anger—in his voice. "The poison... I think it spreads through skin contact. So don’t touch me."

It seemed like the poison took some time to travel from one person’s skin to another, and since Damon had carried Mona for so long, the poison could easily transfer to him.

As for her... she had been holding Damon’s arm, but his sleeve had been between their skin the whole time. They never touched directly long enough for the poison to jump to her.

But that didn’t make her feel any better.

Julian, on the other side, clapped his hands a few times, mocking them. "How touching," he said, tilting his head. "Really sweet. Almost makes me feel bad." He twirled both vials between his fingers. "Almost."

Wait, two vials? Julian brought two antidotes with him?

From that alone, it was clear they had already predicted this would happen. They were sure Damon would carry his mother, so the poison would spread to his body as well.

How wicked.

Charlotte bit her inner lip, and fury burned in her eyes. "Julian, stop this!" she demanded. "Why is it so hard for you to stay away from me?! I just want to live a peaceful life with my mate!"

Julian’s smile slowly faded. He clicked his tongue and spat on the floor. "Your mate? Charlotte, I am your mate! We’ve been together for years, so how could you forget me just like that?!"

"You’re not my mate," she said through clenched teeth. "You never were."

Julian’s expression darkened instantly. "Oh? Then what was I, Charlotte?" he asked, his voice rising. "A toy? A placeholder? Someone you could just throw away once you found something better?"

This man... Charlotte truly couldn’t understand why he couldn’t process information correctly in his own head.

He acted like a victim, when in reality he was the one who kept breaking her heart over and over again, to the point where Charlotte struggled to trust anyone else.

She even needed so much time before she could finally respond to Damon’s confession properly.

"Julian, shut the fuck up," Charlotte snapped. "I’m so sick of your bullshit."

Julian smiled again. "Oh, Charlotte. You really shouldn’t try to make me angry." He twirled the vials even faster between his fingers. "Right now, I’m holding the lives of your precious mate and his mother."

"Charlotte, just go," Damon forced himself to speak through clenched teeth. "I’ll be fine."

No, he wouldn’t be fine.

The poison was already making his body stiff.

If Charlotte left them now, both Damon and Mona would die.

"Why do you look so sad?" Julian asked mockingly. "It’s not like I won’t give you the antidotes. You found them, after all."

His smile widened as he said, "But there’s one condition..." He lifted the vials higher. "I want Charlotte to come with me. In exchange."

chapters.title: Chapter 145: Awakening of the Luna
chapters.content:
Chapter 145: Awakening of the Luna

His smile widened as he said, "But there’s one condition..." He lifted the vials higher. "I want Charlotte to come with me. In exchange."

"You bastard!" Damon shouted as loudly as he could. He tried to move, but the poison had already made his body stiff.

Even so, he forced himself to push forward, ignoring the awful sound of his bones cracking. But the poison was stronger than he thought. When the ship tilted again, both he and Mona collapsed onto the floor.

"Oh, don’t be so dramatic." Julian rolled his eyes and walked toward them. "Besides, this isn’t the first time I’ve stolen your mate."

He moved quickly to Charlotte and grabbed her arm so tightly that she could feel her bone starting to crack. She hissed in pain and glared at him sharply.

"Oh, scary." Julian grinned from ear to ear. "After seeing you keep rebelling, I’m starting to develop feelings for women who like to rebel, like you."

He licked his lips and said, "You’ll be perfect once you’re broken until you lose your mind."

"Julian, I swear I’ll kill you!" Damon growled, biting down so hard that his gums began to bleed. He tried to crawl toward Charlotte, but falling furniture kept slamming into him again and again.

"You can’t even stand properly," Julian said with a shrug. "Maybe worry about yourself first."

"So, since I will take your mate, I will give you and your mom the antidotes." Without warning, Julian threw the antidotes into the air. The vials spun several times before finally falling back down.

Charlotte’s eyes locked onto them. If those vials shattered, Damon and Mona’s chances of surviving would become zero.

She couldn’t let that happen.

Now that she had finally found happiness, Charlotte refused to let Julian take it away from her again.

He had taken everything from her before, and this time, Charlotte would never allow it again.

Never again.

"Do you want to kill him, Charlotte?"

A strange voice suddenly echoed inside her mind. Her eyes widened slightly, but there was no time to think.

In the next second, she answered, "Yes. I want him to die."

The voice wasn’t human.

It came from deep inside her, from the part of her that had been asleep for so long.

It was her wolf.

For years, that part of herself had been silent, repressed, and locked away. Charlotte had almost forgotten what it felt like to hope to hear her wolf’s voice. But now, in this moment of fear and rage, the wolf had finally awakened.

"Good," the wolf whispered calmly. "

"Then let me help you."

Charlotte’s heart pounded. She didn’t know whether to be afraid or relieved, but there was no time to question it. Power began to flow through her body. It was warm at first, then hot, burning through her veins like fire.

Her vision sharpened, her senses exploded to life, and for the first time in a long while, she didn’t feel helpless anymore.

Her body moved before her mind could even catch up.

Her bones shifted, uscles stretched, and fur burst across her skin. In just a heartbeat, Charlotte was no longer standing there as a woman, but she had fully shifted into her wolf.

Her wolf body leapt into the air, and her mouth caught the vials just before they hit the ground.

She landed smoothly, holding the vials carefully between her teeth, then rushed to Damon’s side.

He stared at her in shock. Even through the haze of poison, his eyes could still recognize Charlotte easily. "Charlotte... you can transform."

She dropped the vial into Damon’s hand, then nudged his face with her muzzle and gently licked his cheek a few times.

Damon’s chest tightened as he looked at her. Even in her wolf form, he could still feel her warmth, and her love. He gently placed his hand on her head, his fingers brushing through her fur.

"Thank you," he whispered, his voice trembling.

Then he quickly drank the antidote. The bitter liquid burned down his throat, but he forced himself to swallow it all. Slowly, the pain in his body began to fade, and the stiffness eased.

Charlotte watched him closely, her blue eyes full of worry.

"It’s okay," Damon said softly, meeting her gaze. "I’m alright now."

Unfortunately, Charlotte wasn’t able to relax because Julian was still there.

She turned her head toward him, a low growl rumbling from deep inside her chest. Her fur bristled, and her claws dug into the floor.

As Damon helped Mona drink the antidote, Charlotte slowly stepped forward toward Julian.

Her eyes were filled with all the anger she had felt for years. All the abuse he had done to her since their marriage — and everything that followed — had become the fuel that filled her wolf with rage.

Julian, on the other hand, couldn’t believe what he was seeing — that Charlotte, the wolfless woman he once married, had suddenly transformed into a wolf.

"H-How is this possible?" Julian’s eyes trembled slightly, but he quickly straightened his back, refusing to show any weakness in front of her.

Charlotte didn’t bother replying. She just kept walking forward, her paws steady on the trembling floor. Her wolf eyes burned with anger, every step heavy with years of pain she could no longer hold back.

"Charlotte, my Charlotte," Julian laughed, mocking her. "Do you really think you can kill me just because you finally learned how to shift? You’re just a newborn werewolf compared to me."

Julian then shifted into his wolf form as well. His wolf wasn’t as large as Damon’s, but he was still a Beta, the second-in-command of his pack.

Compared to Charlotte, who had no experience fighting in her wolf form, Julian clearly had the advantage.

However, for some reason, Charlotte didn’t feel even the slightest bit of fear in her heart.

"Julian, you’re wrong," Damon finally said. He stood behind Charlotte, his gray eyes glaring sharply at Julian. "Charlotte’s wolf form may be new, but she has tied her bond to me. She’s not just an ordinary werewolf, she’s my Luna."

chapters.title: Chapter 146: A Life for a Life
chapters.content:
Chapter 146: A Life for a Life

Julian bared his teeth, his wolf eyes glowing with cruelty. "A Luna?" he scoffed. "Do you really think that title will save you?"

He knew that was Charlotte’s new title, but he had never once thought a Luna mattered. His own father had never treated his wife—the Luna of their pack—with respect.

Because of that, in Julian’s mind, a Luna was nothing more than a beautiful trophy. Something that existed in the pack only as decoration.

But what he didn’t understand was this: That Luna had only seemed weak because the bond between her and the Alpha had been fragile and cold.

Meanwhile, in Charlotte and Damon’s case, their bond had grown stronger than ever.

They hadn’t lived together for long, but they had already learned to care for one another deeply, enough that Charlotte had slowly begun to heal from all the scars in her heart.

To Charlotte, Damon was the sun, the light that guided her out of the darkness that once swallowed her whole.

And for Damon, Charlotte was like the gentle moon, the one who soothed his heart.

Julian still didn’t get it.

To him, strength only came from fear, control, and power. He had never known love, so he couldn’t understand why Charlotte now stood taller than ever, even in her very first shift, and that kind of terrified him.

Julian made the first move. He leapt toward Charlotte, baring his fangs and claws. He wanted nothing more than to hurt her.

But before he could reach her, Charlotte moved fast, almost like the wind. She jumped from one spot to another, darting around him and leaving Julian confused in the middle of the sinking ship.

Water surged across the floor, tilting the ship even further, but Charlotte moved as if the chaos didn’t matter. Her paws were light, her steps were sure. She had never fought like this before, and yet her body seemed to know exactly what to do.

Maybe it was because she had finally become one with Damon, and her wolf was now fully linked to his. Through their bond, Damon’s wolf could share his fighting instinct with her, guiding her every move.

Every step, every turn, every strike flowed through her as naturally as breathing. Damon’s instincts blended with her own, shaping her movements but never controlling them.

Julian growled, anger twisting into fear. He lunged again, claws slashing toward her throat, but Charlotte ducked under him and struck his ribs with brutal force. The impact sent him crashing into the side of the ship, wood splintering beneath his weight.

He scrambled back to his feet, panting.

This wasn’t how it was supposed to be, and she wasn’t supposed to overpower him.

She was supposed to be beneath him. Just like always.

"Julian," Charlotte spoke directly into his mind. Her voice sounded colder than usual, most likely because it had merged with her wolf. "You once took the life of my child, and I still haven’t forgiven you. A life must be repaid with a life."

Julian froze.

For a moment, the sound of crashing waves and groaning metal faded away. All he could hear was her voice inside his head.

For the first time in his life, he was finally afraid of Charlotte, the woman who had once been nothing more than his plaything. Even so, he would never admit his fear.

"You were weak," he snarled back through the mind-link. "You lost because you were too fragile. That child—"

He wasn’t able to finish his words, because Charlotte had already rushed toward him. She moved even faster than the wind, and Julian didn’t even have time to react before she slammed into him with terrifying force.

The impact sent Julian crashing to the floor. Before he could recover, Charlotte was already on him. Her claws tore into his fur and flesh, fueled by every memory he had carved into her heart.

He howled in pain, but she didn’t want to stop.

She slashed him again and again. Her fangs tore through his muscles and bones, while her claws dug into his chest, pinning him in place so he couldn’t move at all. Years of fear, grief, and anger poured out of her with every strike. Julian struggled beneath her, snarling and thrashing, but she was relentless.

This was the monster who had broken her.

This was the man who had taken her child.

And now, he would pay for every terrible thing he had done to her.

Charlotte’s wolf pressed down harder, her growl echoing through the drowning ship. Julian’s strength began to fade. His movements slowed. Panic filled his eyes as he realized he was losing to the woman he once believed would always kneel beneath him.

He tried to pull free, he tried to run, but Charlotte didn’t let him, just like Julian had never allowed her to escape from his abuse.

Water rushed around them. The ship groaned and tilted even further, but Charlotte didn’t move from her place. She kept him pinned, refusing to release the nightmare that had haunted her life.

Not long after that, Julian finally managed to twist away, tearing himself free from her claws. Blood poured from his wounds.

He tried to run as fast as he could, but he never got far because Damon was already waiting for him in the shadows ahead.

Damon’s wolf form stood proudly before him, twice as large as Julian. His sharp eyes locked onto him, and he spoke through the mind-link, "Did you really think Charlotte would let you escape?"

Julian’s instincts screamed at him to run, but there was nowhere left to go.

Behind him was the Luna he had broken, and in front of him stood the Alpha who would never let him hurt her again.

Damon stepped forward, his massive paws splashing through the rising water. His eyes were cold, filled with rage.

Julian bared his teeth, desperation twisting inside him. "She was weak!" he snapped back. "She always—"

Damon moved before he could finish.

In one swift blur of motion, he lunged. His jaws closed around Julian’s throat with crushing force. There was no hesitation, and no doubt.

Damon showed him no mercy, and at last he did what he had always wanted to do: he tore Julian’s head from his body.

chapters.title: Chapter 147: The First Breath of Freedom
chapters.content:
Chapter 147: The First Breath of Freedom

"It’s okay. It’s okay, I’ve got you," Damon said as Charlotte shifted back into her human form. Her clothes had been torn apart, but Mona managed to find two coats for them.

The coats were wet, but it was still better than being naked.

Since it was her first transformation, her body felt weak as soon as she returned to human form. Damon immediately caught her and pulled her into his arms. "You’re alright," he whispered.

"Damon... Damon... I—no, we... we killed Julian." Charlotte’s voice trembled. She rested her head against his chest, her eyes filling with tears, not of sadness, but of overwhelming relief, because she had finally broken free from the chains that had bound her for so many years.

"I’m finally free..." she whispered.

Damon held her tighter, one hand cradling the back of her head as if he never wanted to let go. The chaos still raged around them, the ship creaked and groaned, but for a moment, none of it mattered.

"You are," he whispered softly. "You’re free now, Charlotte."

Her shoulders trembled as she cried quietly against him. Years of pain, fear, and helplessness poured out of her at last, but this time, the tears didn’t hurt.

They felt light.

Mona watched them in silence, her own eyes glistening. She didn’t say anything. She didn’t need to. She simply pulled the wet coat closer around herself and let Charlotte have this moment.

Damon brushed a strand of damp hair away from Charlotte’s face and kissed the top of her head.

"No one will ever hurt you again," he said gently. "Not while I’m here."

They wanted to stay in that moment a little longer, but then Mona finally spoke. "I’m happy for both of you, I really am. But I think we should get out of here first."

Charlotte lowered her head, realizing that the water had already risen up to their waists. Panic suddenly rushed through her. "I don’t think we can go back the same way we came earlier."

If the upper part of the sinking ship was already flooded, then the lower decks were definitely completely underwater.

But since Damon was no longer affected by the poison, and neither was his mother, he no longer looked panicked.

"Don’t worry," he said calmly. "We can make our own way out."

Charlotte didn’t understand what he meant at first until Damon shattered the restaurant window, creating an opening big enough for them to escape through.

"Did the other passengers make it out too?" Charlotte asked, mostly to herself. "There were so many children..."

"We can only hope they managed to escape before the ship truly sinks," Damon said.

After all, the rest of the ship was already going under, and it was too late to search the corridors for survivors.

Charlotte knew that too, so she immediately climbed out through the opening after Mona. When Damon was about to follow them, he glanced at Julian’s severed wolf head floating in the water, and decided to take it with him.

Once they made it outside, they had to cling to the window frame because the ship was already tilting at a sharp angle. Waves crashed against the side of the vessel, pulling everything toward the sea below.

"Hold on," Damon warned them. "When I say jump... jump."

Charlotte swallowed hard. Her fingers tightened around the slippery metal. Mona nodded silently, fear clear in her eyes, but she still trusted her son.

"Now!"

They jumped.

Cold water swallowed them instantly. Charlotte gasped as the icy shock rushed through her body. Damon pulled her close, keeping one arm wrapped around her while helping Mona stay afloat with the other.

"Hey, there are people who just jumped!" someone on one of the lifeboats shouted when they spotted them from afar. "We need to help them!"

"No way! Our lifeboat is already full! And what if they’re the terrorists who sank our ship?!"

Charlotte’s teeth chattered from the cold water. She thought what they said didn’t make sense, because they had clearly seen Ronan and the Silverfang Pack leave the ship earlier.

However, she could still understand them because they were terrified and panicked.

"Do you think Ronan will come back for us?" Charlotte asked, glancing at Julian’s severed wolf head floating not far from them. "After all, his son is still here."

Not long after she said that, she heard the sound of engines approaching, not just one or two boats, but dozens of them. Up in the sky, several helicopters were also flying toward their location.

"I see Damon and Charlotte down there!" Diana shouted from the open side door of a helicopter. She held onto the frame with one hand, while the other gripped a megaphone. "Test! Test! Damon, my brother, are you still alive?! If not, Louis said he wants to be Alpha!"

Louis immediately pulled Diana back inside the helicopter and snatched the megaphone from her.

"No, Damon, she’s lying! Just give me a large sum of money so I can take my wife and daughter on a month-long vacation! That’s the least you can do for letting us save you!"

Even in the middle of the freezing ocean, Charlotte couldn’t help but let out a weak laugh. Damon sighed, shaking his head slightly.

"I think the Crimson Moon Pack and the Nocturnal Order managed to track us," Damon said. "Before I turned off my phone, I sent them an emergency signal."

"Oh, I didn’t know that," Charlotte replied in surprise.

"Of course," Damon said calmly. "I was afraid others might find out, so I didn’t want to mention it openly."

Charlotte let out a small breath of relief. "So... you were already planning a way out."

Damon gave a faint smile. "I always plan for the worst, especially when it comes to you."

The boats that had just arrived turned out to belong to the Nocturnal Order, and it seemed they had successfully captured Ronan and his pack.

"Alpha Damon!"

The Elder himself had joined the mission to surround them. "Thank the Moon Goddess that you’re safe!"

chapters.title: Chapter 148: When the Past Finally Catches Up
chapters.content:
Chapter 148: When the Past Finally Catches Up

Elder Alderic personally escorted Ronan and his pack back to Northbridge’s port. When they arrived, dozens of hunters from the Nocturnal Order were already waiting.

Since Ronan had broken the laws of both the human world and the supernatural world, Elder Alderic planned to work together with the human government to decide Ronan’s punishment.

"Don’t worry," Elder Alderic said to Damon and Charlotte, his voice calm and firm. "After this, he won’t be able to see the light again. His punishment might even last longer than his lifespan as a werewolf."

Charlotte’s eyes shifted toward Ronan, who was being shoved forward by several hunters. He looked annoyed. His hands were locked with special cuffs, ones made to weaken the strength of supernatural beings.

However, somehow Ronan didn’t look sad at all when he saw Julian’s head separated from his body.

If anything, Ronan looked more angry and disappointed rather than sad. At that moment, Charlotte was certain that he had never truly valued his family that much, not even his son, whom he had always been so proud of.

In the end, Julian had only been his father’s pawn, and once he was no longer useful, Ronan stopped caring completely.

"That man is seriously sick in the head," Elder Alderic muttered.

Damon’s jaw tightened. "Only a madman could do so many cruel experiments and still feel no guilt."

He then reached out his hand to the Elder. "Thank you for coming to us."

Elder Alderic shook his hand firmly. "No. I’m the one who should thank you. Without you, I might never have been able to track Ronan and his pack."

"And I can see it now," he said. "You’ve finally awakened your wolf, Luna Charlotte." He smiled. "Congratulations."

Charlotte gave a small smile back. "Thank you, Elder... I honestly didn’t expect it."

"Sometimes, good things come when we least expect them," Alderic replied gently. "You only need to focus on the good things that come into your life. That’s all."

Then his expression turned colder. "Alright. I have to go now," he said. "There’s still so much I need to do to make sure that bastard gets the harshest punishment possible."

He paused and added, "I know you two are still shaken. Tomorrow, you can come to the Nocturnal Order office to give your statements." His voice softened again. "I hope your lives will be calmer after this."

After saying goodbye, Elder Alderic left them. Meanwhile, Damon and Charlotte went to the inn Louis had rented.

On the way there, Carmen also surprisingly came to the port and immediately walked over to Mona. They hugged each other and even cried together, as if they had almost believed they would never see each other again.

Charlotte watched them from afar, feeling a bit strange about why two women who shared the same husband could grow so close instead of becoming enemies.

According to Damon, they had grown even closer after his father died. Charlotte didn’t want to assume anything, but usually... that kind of bond could only form when people went through a terrible situation together.

People called it a victim bond.

Yes, Charlotte understood. Both Mona and Carmen had suffered under Samuel’s cruelty—Damon’s father—but it still felt like there was something deeper between them. Something that made their bond stronger than just shared pain.

Maybe...

Maybe they were both carrying the same guilt because Ronan’s father didn’t die naturally, and it seemed like the truth was connected to them.

Damon once mentioned that his mothers were the ones who took care of Samuel’s grave and handled the investigation into his death. They always told Damon the same thing: He only needed to focus on leading the pack, and Samuel’s case? They would take care of it.

Since Damon had so many responsibilities—his work, the pack, and everything else—he never questioned them, and he trusted them completely.

But now that the past had finally caught up to Mona, Damon couldn’t ignore it anymore.

"The Elder actually told me something a while ago," Damon suddenly spoke beside her, startling Charlotte. "It’s about my late father."

She turned to him and asked, "What is it?"

His eyes shifted toward his mothers as he said, "The rogues who killed my father seem to also have ties to the Silverfang Pack, so... I think Arnold Warren was the one who killed my father."

Charlotte didn’t say anything for a moment before finally replying, "I see..."

She added, "I guess Arnold’s death wasn’t natural after all."

There was a high possibility that the Lunas of the Crimson Moon Pack—Mona and Carmen—had betrayed their own Alpha by asking the Silverfang Alpha to kill their husband.

They might have promised Arnold something, like letting him take over the Crimson Moon Pack, but Mona decided to kill Arnold as well by drugging his drink with poison.

That way, their crime would be buried forever, and there would be no evidence pointing to them. Even Ronan seemed to know about it only through speculation.

Did Charlotte feel bad after finding out the truth? Not really.

After all, the only way to stop people with rotten hearts from committing more crimes was death.

"We’ll never know for sure," Damon said, acting like he wasn’t thinking the same thing Charlotte was. "But their deaths will bring peace to this world, so... who cares?"

He sighed. "Come on. Let’s rest."

Damon wrapped his arm around Charlotte’s waist and guided her into one of the bedrooms in the villa. After they cleaned themselves up and changed into more comfortable clothes, they finally lay down on the bed.

They stared at the ceiling in silence because, honestly... everything that happened today was too much, and too shocking.

After a while, Charlotte slowly lifted her hand. She opened her fingers a little and stared at the ring on her finger. Then she moved it gently, as if she was still trying to believe it was real.

"So, Damon..." she whispered softly. "When are we getting married?"

She turned toward him, smiling sweetly. "I can’t wait to call you my husband."

chapters.title: Chapter 149: The Ring That Shocked the Red Carpet
chapters.content:
Chapter 149: The Ring That Shocked the Red Carpet

Damon let out a soft chuckle when he heard that. He turned to the side and pulled Charlotte closer, making her lie right beside him.

For a moment, he didn’t say anything, but from the look in his eyes, Charlotte could tell... he was feeling guilty. "The cruise trip was supposed to be something fun, but—"

"But what?" Charlotte frowned, looking annoyed. "What do you mean, but?"

She held his gaze seriously. "The cruise trip was fun. You made it fun. You made me happy." Her voice softened, but her words were still firm. "The only reason our happiness was ruined... was because of those jerks."

This time, Charlotte didn’t have to be haunted by the fear that her abuser would one day return to hurt her, or hurt her little family anymore.

This time, she also wouldn’t have to live with an angry man in her house. She wouldn’t have to be scared that if she made one small mistake, her husband would hit her.

Her ex-husband was a cruel man.

A disaster that had destroyed her life, breaking her into pieces. But just like Lena Carter—the character she played in the Broken Bond movie—Charlotte still had a chance to rebuild her life slowly.

Piece by piece.

She had been fixing her heart little by little, and even though Julian had shattered it again, Damon had still stayed by her side. He helped her collect every broken piece, then held her while she tried to put herself back together.

"I’m happy, Damon," Charlotte said softly, her smile full of warmth. "I’m happy that you’re my fated mate..." She paused, then smiled even brighter. "And I’ll be even happier when you become my husband."

Damon opened his mouth, then closed it again. For a moment, he didn’t know what to say. In the end, all he could do was let out a long, tired breath. "I..."

But before he could finish his sentence, tears suddenly spilled from his eyes. He didn’t even understand why he was crying like this.

Maybe because deep down, he never truly believed this day would come. He never expected that his fated mate would return to his arms again, and stay there forever.

"I’m glad you came to me when I called you back then." Charlotte wrapped her arms around him and hugged him tightly, her eyes full of tears. "I’m glad you were still willing to accept me, even though I made such a stupid decision."

Damon returned her embrace, and it felt like the warmest hug Charlotte had ever experienced. "I will always come to you," Damon said softly. "No matter what happens."

He tightened his arms around her a little more, as if he never wanted to let her go again. "Because you’re my fated mate," he continued. "And there’s nothing I want more than to see you happy."

Back then, Damon had forced himself to let Charlotte go because he truly believed Julian could give her the life she deserved.

He thought Charlotte would be happy with him, but once he knew Charlotte had never been happy with her ex-husband, Damon finally understood that the only person who could truly make sure his fated mate stayed safe and happy was him.

Therefore, he wanted to keep her close by his side, and always protect her with everything he had.

"Charlotte... let’s stay together forever." Damon slowly loosened their hug so he could hold both of her hands. He looked into her eyes seriously, as if he wanted her to understand every word.

"I promise you," he said softly, "I will take good care of you... and I will never let go of your hand ever again."

Charlotte’s lips curved into a gentle smile as Damon kissed her hands. "And I won’t let go of your hand again either," she whispered.

With that promise, they hugged each other once more... and slowly fell asleep in each other’s arms.

===

About two months later, the Broken Bond movie was finally released in theaters, and within only one week, the movie had already reached twenty million viewers.

The movie even became popular outside Hamary Country, making Charlotte’s name and the movie trend everywhere. It became an even bigger hit after the news about Ronan teaching Julian to abuse his wife spread quickly overseas.

People on social media showed even more support and sympathy for Charlotte. Meanwhile, her fellow celebrities began contacting her again, giving her encouragement and kind words, whether because they truly cared, or simply because they wanted to look good in front of others.

"Charlotte! Charlotte! Look this way!" Photographers shouted her name again and again when she attended the movie premiere.

The camera flashes became even crazier when she arrived together with Damon, the CEO of Luminix Entertainment, the man who had been quietly supporting her all this time.

For a long time, their relationship had only been rumors. Some people even believed Charlotte was just "playing around" with her ex-husband’s uncle, only because she wanted to benefit from him.

But once the two of them were in the same frame, standing side by side, everyone there immediately knew that Damon and Charlotte were head over heels for each other.
