Every time Charlotte smiled at the cameras, Damon would glance at her with eyes full of love. And whenever she looped her arm around his, he would press soft kisses onto her bare shoulder.

The red gown she wore clung to her body perfectly, elegant and bold at the same time. The fabric shimmered under the flashing lights, making her look like she belonged on a stage meant only for stars.

"Charlotte! Look here!"

"Over here, Charlotte!"

"Mr. Sullivan, please look this way!"

The photographers called out nonstop, their cameras clicking so fast it sounded like rain.

Not long after that, Charlotte suddenly tightened her grip on Damon’s hand. Then slowly she lifted their joined hands up in front of the cameras, proudly showing the engagement ring shining on their ring fingers.

For a second, the world froze, and then, the red carpet exploded.

A wave of loud gasps spread through the crowd, followed by shouting so intense that it almost drowned out the camera flashes.

"Wait—what?!"

"Is that an engagement ring?!"

"Charlotte is engaged?!"

"CEO Damon proposed?!"

chapters.title: Chapter 150: There Is Light Above the Darkness
chapters.content:
Chapter 150: There Is Light Above the Darkness

The shouting didn’t stop. The photographers kept calling her name, begging for one more shot, one more smile, like they were afraid the moment would disappear if they blinked.

Charlotte couldn’t help it anymore. A bright smile spread across her face, one that looked truly happy, truly free.

Then she spoke clearly, her voice soft but confident. "We will get married tomorrow."

"WHAT?!"

"Tomorrow?!"

"Are you serious?!"

"Charlotte, is this real?!"

The cameras flashed so violently it almost hurt to look at. Reporters pushed forward, shouting questions like their lives depended on it.

Charlotte simply smiled wider, as if she had been waiting to say those words for a long time.

Then she turned her face toward Damon. Her eyes softened, full of love and trust. "This time," she said gently, "I chose my future husband very carefully."

She lifted their joined hands again, not for the cameras this time, but like she wanted the world to understand her decision. "So you don’t have to worry at all."

Damon smiled as well, something the cameras rarely ever managed to capture from him. His expression was calm, but his eyes were gentle as he said, "Yes, tomorrow."

Charlotte took a deep breath, then turned to face the crowd again. The flashes didn’t stop and the noise didn’t stop, but Charlotte didn’t flinch.

This time, she didn’t feel like a frightened woman being chased by the world. She felt like someone who had finally survived it.

"I just want people to know," Charlotte began. "that even though there are times when we drown in darkness... there will always be a light above us, waiting for us to crawl toward it."

Her words weren’t dramatic, but they were honest.

The crowd slowly quieted down, as if everyone could feel the weight behind what she was saying.

Charlotte continued, her fingers tightening slightly around Damon’s arm, not because she was nervous, but because she wanted to remind herself that he was real. That this moment was real.

"There was a time when I truly thought I would never be able to escape," she said softly. "I thought my whole life would stay trapped in fear."

"But I was wrong," Charlotte said, lifting her chin. "Because life gave me another chance."

Then her gaze moved toward the giant movie poster behind them, the Broken Bond poster, where her face was printed beautifully, strong and calm, like a completely different woman.

"And that’s why Broken Bond is so special to me," Charlotte added. "Not only because it’s a movie... but because it tells the story of a woman who once lived in pain."

Her voice trembled slightly, but she didn’t stop. "She used to be afraid. She used to be weak. She used to think she deserved everything that happened to her," Charlotte said. "She was abused by her ex-husband, and she thought she would never be able to stand back up."

Many people in the crowd were already wiping their eyes. Even some of the reporters who usually loved asking cruel questions... didn’t say a word.

Charlotte looked straight ahead, like she was speaking not only to the cameras, but also to every woman watching from home. "But then, one day," Charlotte continued, "she realized something."

"She realized she wasn’t broken," Charlotte said. "She was just hurt, and hurt people can heal."

Her voice grew stronger at the end. "They can rebuild. They can start over. They can find themselves again."

"So I hope... if you watch this movie," Charlotte said, "you won’t only see it as entertainment."

She placed her hand gently over her chest. "I hope you see it as a reminder," she said. "That no matter how painful your past is... your future can still be beautiful."

Then she lifted her ring finger slightly, allowing the cameras to capture it again, not to show off, but to show proof. Proof that she truly made it out.

"And just like Lena in Broken Bond ," Charlotte said, "I also made it out." She smiled sadly. "But I know there are still so many women out there who are still trapped," Charlotte continued. "Still stuck... still going through the same terrible things I once went through."

"I just want you to know that you matter," she said. "You are not something that other people get to define. I know stepping out of that darkness is scary," she said gently. "But that’s okay."

"It’s okay to be scared. It’s okay to cry, and It’s okay to feel like you can’t breathe sometimes."

She paused, taking a slow breath, as if she was holding herself together the same way she once had to. "But please... don’t give up on yourself," Charlotte said. "Even if you feel weak right now, you’re still fighting. Even if you feel alone... you are not."

Charlotte’s eyes became watery. "But you don’t deserve to live in pain forever," she continued. "You don’t deserve to be hurt, to be controlled, or to be treated like you’re nothing."

"That’s why..." Charlotte said softly, "if you feel like you’re ready, and if you feel like you’re brave enough to take even one step out of that darkness, you can call us."

Her hand trembled as she raised it, pointing gently to the large screen beside the red carpet, where a simple line of text appeared, bright and clear.

THE LUNA LIGHT FOUNDATION

A safe place for women who are hurt, abused, or trapped.

Underneath, there was a contact number, an email, and a website.

"I created the Luna Light Foundation," Charlotte said proudly. "It’s a foundation made for women who are suffering from abuse, so please... Please call us and let us help you."

Over the past two months, she had talked to Damon about how she wanted to use all the wealth she had taken back from Julian to build a charity organization, one that would help women who had suffered the way she once did.

And of course, Damon supported her. He didn’t just support her with words, he became her very first donator. He even rented an entire building for the foundation so it could start operating as soon as possible.

Fortunately, the foundation was able to settle in just two weeks before the premiere was held. That way, Charlotte could tell women out there that they could always ask for help whenever they needed it, just like how she once asked Damon for help when she ran away from Julian.

"My fellow reporters, I hope you can also help me spread this information to the public," Charlotte said with a warm smile, and that simple smile was already more than enough to melt their hearts.

chapters.title: Chapter 151: A Fresh Start in Velmoria
chapters.content:
Chapter 151: A Fresh Start in Velmoria

In just one night, the Luna Light Foundation gained huge public attention. Big names also began donating, and more people started talking about it online.

Charlotte also received a report from the staff that their phones had been ringing nonstop, a sign that she had successfully convinced those women to step out of the darkness.

Some of them might return to their abusers later, but at least they had taken the first step by reaching out. After that, little by little, they would surely begin to realize that their abusers were nothing more than cowards.

"It’s going to be a busy year," Charlotte murmured as she stared at her work phone, reading the names of the women who were already being handled directly by the foundation. She let out a slow breath. "I hope I can manage my schedule well."

After the premiere, Charlotte and Damon immediately flew to Velmoria City, Charlotte’s hometown, and the place where she had lived her life before Julian ever entered it.

She wanted to start her life over from the beginning with Damon, so she chose that city for their wedding ceremony. It wasn’t only because she wanted to erase Julian’s traces, but also because Velmoria City meant a lot to her.

"Yeah... your life is definitely going to be very busy," Diana said while sitting in the bride’s makeover room with them. "But at least, you don’t have to worry about facing a psychopath like Julian anymore."

"Oh, come on!" Aveline, one of Charlotte’s friends, immediately scolded her. "Don’t say that J word !" She pointed at Diana like a strict teacher. "Charlotte, your future sister-in-law can’t even remember the rule!"

Diana was taken aback because that human girl dared to scold her like that. But since her body was covered in a lycan’s scent, she decided to just play nice.

"I just forgot!" Diana said defensively. "It’s not like someone will die just because we say his name."

"Yes, but it might make Charlotte remember things again!" Aveline snapped back.

Before their argument got any worse, Charlotte finally said with a smile, "Alright, alright. Don’t fight." She added, "Besides, Julian can’t hurt me anymore. He means nothing to me now."

After all, his head had already been separated from his body, so what else could he possibly do?

"Auntie Charlotte! Auntie Charlotte!" Haven shouted excitedly, her face glowing with happiness. "They said it’s time for you to go to your prince!"

She looked like she was about to explode from excitement. Behind her, Vera ran after her, looking a bit out of breath because her daughter was way too fast. At that moment, Vera was sure Haven could grow up to be just as strong as Diana.

"Haven!" Vera finally caught her and lifted her up. "I told you not to run around the hallway!"

Then Vera turned toward Charlotte. The moment she saw Charlotte in her wedding dress, her eyes softened.

Charlotte looked beautiful, so beautiful that Vera froze for a second before smiling warmly.

"Your father has been waiting for you," Vera said gently. "I think he’s even crying a little."

Charlotte couldn’t help but laugh, and then she slowly stood up from her chair. "This isn’t even my first wedding," she joked lightly.

But when she said the same thing to Derek earlier, Derek immediately frowned like she had offended his whole bloodline. "This is the first time I approved your future husband," Derek said firmly.

Then he cleared his throat right after, acting like he hadn’t said anything emotional at all. "I mean..." Derek muttered stiffly, "there’s no rule that says a father isn’t allowed to feel emotional at his daughter’s wedding... even if it’s... the second time."

Charlotte couldn’t help but smile a little. Honestly, when she married Julian, Derek didn’t look like he wanted to cry at all. Instead, he looked like he wanted to throw a chair.

That was exactly why Charlotte knew this moment was different. This time, her father truly felt happy, and more than that... he felt relieved because Damon would be the one holding her hand next and Damon would be the one protecting her now, a man her father could finally trust.

"Ms. Dawson, it’s time," the wedding staff said with a warm smile.

Charlotte nodded without hesitation. "I’m ready."

She gently nudged her father, then slipped her arm around his. Derek took a deep breath, then said quietly, "I’m ready too."

A second later, the staff opened the large doors of the building. The moment the doors opened, a soft breeze from the garden brushed against Charlotte’s face.

Warm sunlight spilled into the hallway, gentle and golden, like the world itself was welcoming her.

For a second, Charlotte froze, not because she was scared, but because the view in front of her was too beautiful.

The garden was filled with white flowers and pale ribbons that moved softly in the wind. Long rows of chairs were arranged neatly, and everyone was already seated, turning their heads toward the entrance the moment the doors opened.

"The kind lady is here..."

Charlotte couldn’t help but smile when she heard those small whispers from the children she had invited from the rescue facility.

Over the past two months, she had visited them again and again. She didn’t just come once and disappear, but she kept showing up. Slowly, some of the children began to trust her, and that trust meant everything.

That was why Charlotte decided to bring them to Velmoria City too, so they could witness her happy day.

Of course, they were accompanied by their caretakers, and she also made sure to give them seats in a corner where they wouldn’t become the center of attention.

After that, Charlotte looked forward, gazing at Damon, who was standing stiffly beside Louis, looking both nervous and serious at the same time.

And yes, Louis would be the one officiating their wedding.

Damon’s eyes lit up the moment he saw Charlotte walking down the aisle.

She looked like a dream.

Her white dress flowed softly around her as she moved, and a bouquet of flowers rested in her right hand. Her hair was neatly tied into an elegant bun, and a delicate tiara sat on top of her head, making her look like royalty.
